authors: Claudio Casetti
publish: IEEE Vehicular Technology Magazine

title: Threshold Voltage Variability Induced by Localized Ferroelectric Phase Distributions in FeFET and Its Mitigation Strategies
authors: Omkar Phadke, Minji Shon, Stuart Wodzro, Halid Mulaosmanovic, Stefan Dünkel, Sven Beyer, Asif Khan, Suman Datta, Shimeng Yu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Electron Devices
abstract: Threshold voltage variability is a key concern in ferroelectric field effect transistor (FeFET) technology. In this work, a study spanning experiments, modeling, and SPICE simulations is conducted to evaluate the impact of localized FE/ dielectric (DE) distributions on the threshold voltage (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${V}_{\text {TH}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>) of FeFET. The device characterization is performed on the 28-nm FeFET foundry platform. The experimental study on device-to-device (D2D) and cycle-to-cycle (C2C) variations reveals that the FeFET can be an asymmetric device, with the V<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">TH</sub> showing dependence on the direction of current flow, as well as the magnitude of applied drain voltage (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${V}_{\text {DS}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>) to the device. The TCAD modeling study reveals that the cause of asymmetry is the spatial localization of DE phases that may occur due to the random nature of the distribution. SPICE simulation for two-FeFET-based ternary content-addressable memory (TCAM) reveals that as few as two devices exhibiting the localization-induced threshold voltage shift can lead to a 64-bit search failure. Various strategies are investigated to minimize the impact of such localized phase distribution-induced threshold voltage shift (LPTS) effect. Notably, the capacitive readout of FeFET and the insertion of a floating metal electrode in the gate-stack improve the robustness.

title: Direct measurement of transient temperature at the tool–chip interface using a 3D-printed sensor-integrated cutting tool
authors: Chao Wang, Tatsuki Saito, Haruka Yamamoto, Zhitong Xie, Toru Kizaki, Tanibuchi Takahito, Hirosaki Kouji, Kumai Kenji, Keisuke Nagato, Naohiko Sugita
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Due to the extreme thermal-mechanical stress at tool-chip interface, built-in sensors in the cutting tool are prone to failure during machining process despite their high sensitivity. Eroding thermocouple (ETC) can operate at high stress and high wear conditions; however, traditional adhesive bonding methods introduce significant thermal inertia because the low-thermal-con-ductivity bonding materials reduce response speed and cause sys-tematic measurement errors. In this study, we demonstrate for the first time the integration of an ETC at the tool–chip interface using a solid-state diffusion-bonding approach, which creates direct joints between the sensor and substrate materials. A 3D-printed stainless-steel tool was co-sintered with the ETC, achieving seam-less bonding, rapid response, and robust temperature monitoring based on solid-state diffusion. The performance of this sensor-in-tegrated cutting tool was first evaluated through calibration ex-periments and subsequently verified through real cutting tests. To process the measurements, an extended Kalman filter was em-ployed to filter and regulate the data into an equivalent first-order model. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed sensor exhibits superior response speed and robustness compared with the conventional sensor, achieving a 67.8% mean reduction in thermal time constant and improving quasi-steady-state robust-ness by as much as 36.2%. Furthermore, the method enhances measurement accuracy, as evidenced by a distinctly narrowed un-certainty band.

title: Distance-Security Tradeoffs for Repeaterless End-to-End QKD Networks_supp1-3678029.pdf
authors: Janis Notzel
publish: N/A
abstract: Quantum Key Distribution (QKD) offers provably secure, information-theoretic key exchange, but in long-distance scenarios without quantum repeaters, Trusted Nodes (TNs) are commonly employed despite introducing critical security risks. We propose a redundant key management method for QKD network that combines Twin Field QKD (TF-QKD) (or Measurement-Device Independent (MDI)-QKD) with a novel key-routing scheme to eliminate the need for truly trusted TNs. Quantum measurements are handled entirely within the network, minimizing end-user hardware requirements. Multiple QKD links connect intermediate nodes such that a successful attack requires the collusion of multiple adversarial nodes, greatly enhancing security over the traditional TN model. In this contribution, we discuss the tradeoff between security, key rates, and distances supported by the new method. Our analysis reveals that the improved redundant key management system may enable true end-to-end connectivity over several thousand kilometers while maintaining high security standards.

title: The Franklin Stove: An Unintended American Revolution—Joyce E. Chaplin (New York, NY, USA: Farrar, Straus and Giroux, 2025, 422 pp.)
authors: Karl D. Stephan
publish: IEEE Technology and Society Magazine
abstract: Presents reviews for the following list of books, (The Franklin Stove: An Unintended American Revolution—Joyce E. Chaplin (New York, NY, USA: Farrar, Straus and Giroux, 2025, 422 pp.)).

title: Top-K-Aware Set Optimization for Component-Sharing Multiobjective Optimization_supp1-3678952.pdf
authors: Qingfu Zhang
publish: N/A
abstract: Component-sharing multiobjective optimization (CSMOO) is an emerging research topic that seeks to identify a set of optimal solutions with shared components in the decision space. This task can be modeled as a bi-level structured multiobjective optimization problem (MOP), where the upper level is a single-objective optimization problem, and the lower level is an MOP parameterized by upper-level variables. However, existing methods often generate an unnecessary large set of solutions when solving each lower-level MOP. This not only complicates decision-making, but also leads to significant resource waste when only a small number of top-K solutions are ultimately required. To mitigate this issue, we adopt a concatenated vector to encode the size-K solution set and introduce a new problem formulation that explicitly incorporates the top-K requirement in CSMOO. To efficiently solve the newly formulated problem, we develop a top-K-aware bi-level search (TopK-BLS) algorithm. It leverages similarities among the top-K-aware lower-level MOPs to solve multiple lower-level tasks in a cooperative manner, thereby improving computational efficiency. The effectiveness of our proposed TopK-BLS is demonstrated through experiments on benchmark and real-world problems. Experimental results indicate that the proposed method can not only generate superior top-K solution sets but also significantly reduce the number of function evaluations compared to other state-of-the-art methods.

title: A Deep Learning-Based Spatio-Temporal Fusion Model Accounting for Strong Phenological Changes
authors: Aijing Li, Qunming Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Spatio-temporal fusion enables the generation of time-series data with both fine spatial and temporal resolutions, which are essential for timely, precise monitoring of land surface processes. However, existing spatio-temporal fusion methods face significant challenges in handling large phenological changes, especially when there is a large spatial resolution difference between fine and coarse spatial resolution images, leading to noticeable spectral distortion and spatial blurring during in the fusion predictions. To address this issue, we proposed a deep learning-based spatio-temporal fusion model designed for scenarios with strong phenological changes, namely, PC-STF. The proposed network adopts a dual-stream architecture, where one branch captures spectral variations across different times, while the other compensates for sensor differences and extracts fine spatial details. The features from both branches were then fused by a U-shaped architecture with ConvNeXt V2 blocks as the backbone for multi-scale feature extraction. Moreover, we employed a pyramid pooling module (PPM) to capture global contextual information and a local consistency block to preserve spatial coherence. Additionally, a phenological-aware loss function was applied during training to maintain fine visual details across phenological changes. In the experiments on fusion of 10 m Sentinel-2 MSI and 300 m Sentinel-3 OLCI data, PC-STF was compared with seven benchmark methods, including three traditional and four deep learning-based methods. It is found that PC-STF can consistently produce more accurate fusion results, and it is more robust under different phenological changes.

title: Rotating Scaled Synchronization of Networked Agent Systems With Hybrid Time Delays
authors: Weihao Li, Mengji Shi, Lei Shi, Boxian Lin, Kaiyu Qin
publish: IEEE Transactions on Signal and Information Processing over Networks
abstract: Rotational coordination is a canonical collective behavior in natural and engineered systems, including satellite constellations and swarm robotics. However, hybrid time delays, arising from sensing/measurement, computation, and communication, can degrade coordination and compromise stability. With this in mind, this paper addresses the rotating scaled synchronization problem for second-order networked agent systems (NASs) subject to hybrid time delays. A fully distributed control scheme is proposed that drives agents to rotate on concentric circles with a prescribed scaling vector. Unlike conventional approaches that enforce identical trajectories, the proposed design admits heterogeneous circular orbits while synchronizing angular velocity and phase relationships. Theoretically, we establish sufficient conditions for rotating scaled synchronization and derive an explicit maximum admissible delay bound (delay margin) via a frequency-domain characterization of the closed-loop dynamics, providing a tractable certificate of stability and synchronization under hybrid time delays. Finally, simulation results are presented to validate the effectiveness and robustness of the proposed approach.

title: A novel VGSOT-pMTJ write circuit for hybrid CMOS/MTJ CIM architecture
authors: Prashanth Barla, Ramya Moodakare
publish: IEEE Journal on Exploratory Solid-State Computational Devices and Circuits
abstract: Hybrid computation-in-memory (CIM) architecture has emerged as the most promising alternative to overcome the drawbacks of the conventional CMOS-only devices used in the conventional von-Neumann architecture. In the hybrid CIM architecture, a pair of perpendicular magnetic tunnel junctions (pMTJs) are used to store one bit of information. Though there are different methods by which one can store information into the pMTJ pair, the voltage gate spin-orbit torque (VGSOT) is the most prominent due to its advantages over the rest. In this work a novel write circuit has been proposed that stores information into the pMTJ pair using the VGSOT mechanism. The proposed circuit implemented and evaluated using the Cadence Virtuoso tool. Comparison of the novel write circuit with the existing one reveals a significant improvements of 77.56% in energy efficiency, 87.01% reduction in delay, 97.09% in energy delay product and a 50% decrease in transistor count. Monte Carlo simulations further show that the proposed write circuit achieves lower energy consumption in its worst-case than the best-case energy consumption of the existing design, highlighting its robustness and energy efficiency.

title: Confidence-Aware Cascaded Learning for Real-Time Weakly Supervised Anomaly Detection in Surveillance Video
authors: K. Mohanarangan, P. Palanisamy
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Weakly supervised video anomaly detection (WS-VAD) has advanced significantly, yet most approaches remain tailored to long, untrimmed streams and fail to meet the latency and reliability demands of real-world surveillance. In asset-protection scenarios, where motion-triggered clips must be processed in real time, existing methods often compromise either accuracy or efficiency. We present a Confidence-Aware Sequential Routing Ensemble (CASRE), a cascaded framework designed for Clip-Level Weak Supervision (CLWS). CASRE integrates a temperature-scaled ResNet3D (TDR3D) primary detector with a novel Temporal-Scale Delta Loss to enforce confidence separation. Only low-confidence clips are routed to a transformer-based MViTv2 fallback, and final predictions are obtained through calibrated probabilistic fusion. This selective routing ensures that high-capacity models are invoked for fewer than 6% of clips, while the majority benefit from lightweight inference. Extensive evaluation on UCF–Crime, UCSD–Ped1/Ped2, and the newly introduced SSAD dataset, a real-world benchmark curated for asset-protection surveillance, demonstrates that CASRE achieves up to 98.64% accuracy and 99.91% AUC, while maintaining sub-0.03 s inference for most samples. By combining confidence calibration, selective routing, and heterogeneous backbone fusion, CASRE establishes a deployable WS-VAD paradigm that balances accuracy, robustness, and computational efficiency, making it well-suited for large-scale surveillance deployments under strict operational constraints.

title: Beyond Diagonal RIS: A New Frontier for 6G Internet of Things Networks
authors: Wali Ullah Khan, Chandan Kumar Sheemar, Eva Lagunas, Symeon Chatzinotas
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Magazine
abstract: Reconfigurable intelligent surface (RIS) technology is emerging as a key enabler for upcoming sixth-generation (6G) and beyond wireless networks, shifting from passive radio environments to programmable wave propagation. While diagonal RIS (D-RIS) offers benefits, its restricted manipulation capability limits overall performance. This paper explores the novel concept of beyond-diagonal RIS (BD-RIS) for the Internet of Things (IoT), which introduces non-diagonal elements in the scattering matrix to achieve finer control of electromagnetic wavefronts. We first outline the drawbacks of conventional D-RIS and present representative BD-RIS architectures with their operating modes. We then emphasize the distinctive advantages of BD-RIS in supporting advanced 6G IoT applications. A case study on BD-RIS-enabled vehicle-to-vehicle (V2V) communication demonstrates potential performance improvements. Finally, we identify key challenges that remain and propose new research directions to advance energy-efficient, scalable, and reliable 6G IoT networks.

title: Vehicle Trajectory Prediction in Highway Weaving Areas Considering Interaction-Transfer Characteristics
authors: Zhenyu Gu, Xiaoyun Xu, Jiawei Shen, Rentao Liu, Jiajia Zheng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: In order to enhance safety in dynamic traffic, autonomous vehicles must predict surrounding vehicles’ trajectories to reduce accident risk. Addressing the issue of insufficient consideration of vehicle interaction transfer characteristics in existing studies, this paper proposes a trajectory prediction framework for highway weaving areas that explicitly accounts for vehicle interaction transfer effects. This method uses a hierarchical dynamic graph to comprehensively represent interaction transfer characteristics and achieves cross-layer coupling at both the feature level and the decision level, which helps to address the complexity of vehicle interactions in mixed traffic environments. The proposed graph attention network for trajectory prediction, which models the transfer characteristics of vehicle interactions, fully exploits temporal continuity and spatial coupling to jointly extract spatiotemporal features. This paper infers latent interaction information transmitted by indirectly adjacent vehicles based on vehicle historical data. The inferred information is integrated with vehicle state features and direct interaction information. A hierarchical dynamic graph is then constructed to account for interaction transfer among vehicles. To overcome insufficient modeling of coupled spatiotemporal dependencies, we design a spatiotemporal dynamic graph attention network model for trajectory prediction. Experiments were conducted on publicly available datasets. The results indicate that the model’s predictions surpass those of the currently advanced baseline models, demonstrating higher prediction accuracy.

title: A 6.5-pJ/Step Reconfigurable Readout IC With Duty-Cycled Resistor and Switched Capacitor for High Linearity RC-to-Digital Converter
authors: Siyuan Xu, Yingrui Wei, Yuechen He, Peter R. Kinget
publish: IEEE Journal of Solid-State Circuits
abstract: The time-domain, reconfigurable resistance (R) and capacitance (C)-to-digital converter (RCDC) combines a Wheatstone bridge (WhB), a duty-cycled resistor (DCR), and a switched capacitor to realize a pulsewidth-locked loop (PWLL) and a frequency-locked loop (FLL) for highly linear and energy-efficient R&C sensor readout. A low-frequency, relaxation voltage-controlled oscillator (VCO) performs voltage-to-time conversion so a compact, low-power, counter-based time-to-digital converter (TDC) can be used, which further enhances energy efficiency. The feedback loop maintains high system linearity. All key circuits operate rail-to-rail to support the large R&C sensing range needed for multi-type sensing applications. The 65-nm CMOS prototype consumes 5.7–6.2<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mu $</tex-math> </inline-formula>W from a 0.9-V supply. It achieves a very high linearity with R<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${}^{2} \gt 0.99993$</tex-math> </inline-formula> (ten channels measured), along with a wide R&C sensing range of 50–400 k<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\Omega $</tex-math> </inline-formula> and 35–260 pF. The sensing resolution is 262 <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\Omega $</tex-math> </inline-formula> for R<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">sen</sub> and 21 fF for C<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">sen</sub> with a conversion time of 0.5 ms and improves to 23 <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\Omega $</tex-math> </inline-formula> and 3.3 fF when the conversion time is increased to 32 ms; the corresponding figure of merit (FoM) is 6.5 pJ/step in R-mode and 0.75 pJ/step in C-mode.

title: Cross-Ambiguity Function Hierarchical Optimization Doppler Estimation for Underwater Acoustic OFDM Communication
authors: Bin Li, Zhuoya Huang, Xiujing Gao, Fang Wang
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: Underwater sensor networks (USNs) are essential for marine monitoring, yet their performance is constrained by the instability of underwater acoustic (UWA) communication links. Orthogonal frequency division multiplexing (OFDM) is widely adopted in UWA systems for its high data rate capability and support for multi-user access, however, it remains highly sensitive to Doppler effects, which degrades demodulation performance.Hence, Doppler estimation and compensation are essential in UWA OFDM communications. The Doppler scale is typically estimated by the calculation of the optimal cross-ambiguity function (CAF) between the locally generated and received signals. However, this type of algorithm generates prohibitive computational complexity due to the necessity of exhaustive search over a two-dimensional delay and Doppler factor space. In this paper, a cross-ambiguity function hierarchical optimization (CAF-HO) framework is proposed to partition the Doppler estimation into two stages for low complexity Doppler estimation. Furthermore, the particle swarm optimization (PSO) is integrated with adaptive parameter control to effectively navigate the Doppler estimation landscape. The numerical simulation and sea trial experiment demonstrate the effectiveness and superiority of the proposed strategy over the CAF estimation and block estimation approaches. By diminishing computational burden and energy consumption, this framework facilitates the large-scale deployment of USNs crucial for marine monitoring, climate research, and maritime security.

title: MHCFNN: Multihead Attenuated Neuro-Fuzzy based Deep Learning Model for Cybersecurity in Internet of Things
authors: Anand Prakash Dwivedi, Omkar Agrahari
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: Cybersecurity is crucial for securing the information or data generated in the Internet of Things paradigm, as it experiences certain challenges, including the problem of data scarcity, data breaches, and increased network traffic. Multiple machine learning and deep learning strategies were designed to overcome such downsides, but their inability to mitigate the computational complexity, overfitting, and poor integrity issues affected their utilization in the attack determination process in the IoT networks. Therefore, in order to address such drawbacks, an effective Multihead attention-enabled Neuro Fuzzy Convolutional Neural Network (MHCFNN) model is proposed in this research. The utilization of the preprocessed and log-transformed data increased the stability and the attack determination accuracy of MHCFNN. Further, the multi-head attention incorporated aided in the accurate determination of the cyber threats by concentrating only on the relevant attack features. Also, the Adam Optimizer included in MHCFNN fine-tuned the model parameters and stipulates the proposed model to attain optimal convergence. The efficacy of the proposed MHCFNN is evaluated using numerous performance metrics using the BoT-IoT dataset, and the proposed research is found to obtain superior performance by attaining 97.09% accuracy, 97.66% sensitivity, and 95.73% specificity for a training percentage of 80.

title: Measurement Techniques for Power Analysis in Side-Channel Attacks on Integrated Circuits
authors: Victor Y. Mikhailov, Alexander S. Tishin, Valery D. Alekseev
publish: 2026 ElCon Conference of Young Researchers in Computing &amp; Processing, and Information Security (ElCon-CP)
abstract: This article examines the evaluation of hardware security against side-channel attacks (SCA) during the prototyping phase of digital integrated circuits. A study was conducted on the correlation between measurement equipment parameters (bandwidth and sampling rate) and the effectiveness of correlation power analysis (CPA) for an AES-128 encryption algorithm implementation on an FPGA. A specialized measurement methodology is proposed, including calibration, integration of protection mechanisms, leakage differentiation, and effectiveness assessment. Experimental verification focuses on the calibration phase using a specialized measurement setup with variable bandwidth (20–500 MHz) and sampling rate (50–5000 MS/s). The results show that limiting the bandwidth significantly improves the signal-tonoise ratio (SNR) and reduces the number of traces required for key recovery. Furthermore, the study reveals the influence of input data entropy on leakage metrics. These results enable optimization of leakage detection setups, reducing computational and hardware costs while maintaining high assessment reliability.

title: IEEE WiCAS and YPCAS Host Joint Event IEEE at ISICAS 2015 in Qingdao to Explore Career Reshaping in the AI-Powered Era [CASS Conference Highlights]
authors: Yanan Sun, Xiaozhe Wang
publish: IEEE Circuits and Systems Magazine
abstract: The joint event hosted by IEEE Women in Circuits and Systems (WiCAS) and the Young Professionals of the Circuits and Systems Society (YPCAS) on 26 October 2025, in Qingdao brought together leading academics, industry experts, and emerging professionals to explore how artificial intelligence is reshaping careers in the circuits and systems field. Under the theme “Beyond AI: Reshaping Your Career in the AI-Powered Era,” the program featured keynote presentations covering advancements in integrated circuit reliability, biomedical microsystems, and AI-driven hardware design. The event concluded with an interdisciplinary roundtable discussion focused on the evolving role of AI in academia and industry, the critical competencies needed to remain competitive, and the high-value skills that complement AI technologies. This gathering fostered meaningful dialogue and provided strategic insights to help professionals navigate an increasingly AI-centric technological landscape.

title: An Ultra-Low Power Built-in-Self-Test Architecture for CMOS Image Sensors Using Adaptive Thresholding and Clock Gating
authors: Krishna Neeraja Periyala, Bungatavala Mithun Chakravarthi, Sri Adibhatla Sridevi
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: This paper presents an ultra-low power Built-In-Self-Test (BIST) architecture for CMOS Image Sensors (CIS), incorporating adaptive thresholding and clock gating to improve defect detection efficiency while minimizing power consumption. The proposed architecture identifies pixel defects, including hot and stuck-at faults, by comparing each pixel against a dynamically derived neighborhood-based threshold. The design features a finite state machine (FSM) that governs module activity and enables logic blocks during relevant test phases. Power aware techniques like clock gating and minimal switching are applied to pixel sequencer and defect detection logic to reduce dynamic power by preventing unnecessary switching when modules are idle. Adaptive thresholding enhances robustness by adjusting detection boundaries based in logic pixel statistics. Simulation results confirm <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{1 0 0 \%}$</tex> test coverage for known defects. Compared to always-on implementation, the gate design achieves an estimated 31.3% reduction in dynamic power. This architecture is fully synthesizable and suitable for FPGA or ASIC deployment, making it ideal for low power imaging systems.

title: LEXA-IDS- A Lightweight Explainable Autoencoder-Assisted Intrusion Detection System for Resource-Constrained Iot Networks
authors: R. E Franklin Jino, P. Anantha Christu Raj, Subha Hency Jose
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: Intrusion detection in Internet of Things (IoT) environment remains a challenge due to constraints like limited processing capabilities, heterogeneous device behaviors, and fluctuating traffic patterns at network edge. Although existing Deep Learning (DL)-based Intrusion Detection Systems (IDS) have achieved high accuracy, increased computational complexity is observed restricting their applicability for realtime deployment. To address this gap, “LEXA-IDS”, a Lightweight Explainable Autoencoder-Assisted IDS model is developed in this paper which is suited for resource-constrained IoT networks. LEXA-IDS comprise of a Micro-Feature Stabilization (MFS) unit that smoothes volatile traffic samples and prunes redundant correlation using low-complexity mechanisms. Further, an Edge-Optimized Sparse Autoencoder (EOS-AE) learns compressed representations of benign behavior and detects anomalies through reconstruction deviations. Finally, an Explainable Feature Attribution Layer (EFAL) produces operator-friendly explanations for identifying dominant features that contribute to each anomaly. For the evaluation process, an open-access IoT dataset for IDS -BotNeTIoT-L01 from Kaggle is used. The approach attained a detection accuracy of 90% and reliable learning capability in both training and testing phase. Preliminary evaluations on the LEXA-IDS model demonstrate reduced computational overhead of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0. 4 m s}$</tex> and improved detection of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 7. 8 \%}$</tex> compared to DNN-IDS, LSTM-RNN, GRU-RNN-IDS, and DRL-IIoT IDS models making it suitable for IoT settings.

title: Design and Implementation of an Autonomous Neural Network Subsystem for Monitoring Computer Systems Digital Components
authors: Alexander K. Krutikov, Dmitry A. Strabykin, Vasily Yu. Meltsov, Aleksei A. Golovin
publish: 2026 ElCon Conference of Young Researchers in Computing &amp; Processing, and Information Security (ElCon-CP)
abstract: The presented study examines the most common techniques used to automate monitoring and diagnostics of the functioning of computing equipment. The novelty of the approach proposed by the authors lies in the segmentation of the initial diagnostic information collected in real time and the formation of the structure of the neural network subsystem, taking into account the interdependencies of digital components. The sequential connection of neural modules into tiers makes it possible to identify not only potential failures and failures of system elements, but also to predict cascading shutdowns of blocks, nodes, and entire subsystems. The paper describes in detail the key features of the architecture of an intelligent diagnostic and monitoring module operating in automatic mode.

title: Integrative Graph-based Learning for Multi-Omics Characterization of Drug Resistance in Breast Cancer
authors: Malathi K, Jeyabharathi J
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: Integration of various omics data is an essential aspect of the research process that enables researchers to use multi-omics data to facilitate cancer diagnosis, subtype classification, predicting drug response, applying precision medicine principles and developing improved ways to manage patients suffering from cancer. In this review, I aim to highlight recent advancements in the use of graph-based machine learning and deep learning approaches for multi-omics integration. Furthermore, this study will assess existing frameworks such as graph neural networks, graph convolutional networks, attention-based models and hybrid deep learning methods to evaluate their ability to model complex biological interactions across heterogeneous datasets through integrated analysis of multi-omics data. Additionally, this research study will examine the use of network-based approaches to prediction of cancers, discovery of biomarkers and designing personalized treatment strategies. Although great strides have been made, there are still many challenges that need to be addressed, such as data heterogeneity, high dimensionality, scalability issues, limited interpretability of models and lack of clinical validation for the new methods. Additionally, advanced techniques that involve multimodal learning and attention mechanisms will require further investigation in order develop effective methods for data fusion. This review is intended to provide a snapshot of key research trends, limitations and future research directions, as well as provide ideas on developing effective computational frameworks for providing data integration of multi-omics data for advancing precision medicine applications.

title: A Unified Multi-Modal System for Image Forgery, Text Plagiarism, and AI-Generated Content Detection
authors: Ambarish A, R Abhiram, Sreelakshmi V S, Anirudh P R, Bharath N, Remya M
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: The rapid development of content creation technologies and generative AI systems has created more risk of content manipulation, plagiarism and misuse of various types of data. The old systems of plagiarism detection and forgery detection can only operate on one type of content hence it is not applicable to the multimodal threats in the contemporary world. This paper presents Truevision, a multimodal framework designed to detect image forgery, text and PDF plagiarism and AI generated content detection within a single system. The proposed approach integrates deep learning based image analysis for detecting the manipulations like copy move forgery, splicing for image forensics and natural language processing techniques for text similarity and plagiarism detection. The system also incorporates AI generated content detection mechanisms to differentiate human written content from machine generated ones. Experimental evaluations conducted on various datasets indicate that the proposed system enhances detection accuracy compared to the traditional single modal approaches. This unified approach makes it easy to reliably spot complex changes. TrueVision is perfect for real world applications in academia, digital forensics and content verification services.

title: A Study on Extract Bits of Pseudorandom Sequences Generated by Me Operation on the Elliptic Curve
authors: Renta Yamauchi, Natsuo Hayashi, Takeru Miyazaki, Shunsuke Araki, Satoshi Uehara
publish: 2025 11th International Workshop on Signal Design and Its Applications in Communications (IWSDA)
abstract: In this paper, we propose an extracting method of pseudorandom bits from sequences generated by Shirase's Me operation on elliptic curves over finite fields, whose security is based on the hardness of the Elliptic Curve Discrete Logarithm Problem (ECDLP). In Shirase's original pseudorandom number generator, only the least significant bit (LSB) of the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$x$</tex> coordinate of each point generated by the Me operation was extracted. However, since this approach is time-consuming and impractical for real-world use, we conclude a new method that extends the number of extracted bits. We evaluate the statistical properties of the generated sequences from multiple perspectives: 0/1 occurrence rate at each bit position, randomness with respect to initial values, calculation cost, and randomness of the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$y$</tex>-coordinates. Furthermore, we compared the ECDLP based pseudorandom number generator with the DLP based method on which it is derived, in order to demonstrate the superiority of the ECDLP based approach. The comparison was conducted from three perspectives: 0/1 occurrence rate at each bit position, randomness with respect to initial values, generation speed and memory usage. Experimental results confirmed that the ECDLP based method achieved higher randomness and stability across all metrics, demonstrating its superiority over the DLP based method. These findings suggest that the generator has strong potential for practical deployment in cryptographic applications requiring secure and efficient pseudorandom number generation.

title: A Hybrid Attribute-Content-Based Access Control Model for Medical Information Systems
authors: Ke Ma, Xinyu Xu, Yizhen Yang, Pingping Xu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Cyber Resilience and Endogenous Safety &amp; Security (CRESS)
abstract: Access control is fundamental to facilitating secure data sharing in Medical Information Systems (MIS). While prevalent models like Role-Based Access Control (RBAC), Attribute-Based Access Control (ABAC), and Mandatory Access Control (MAC) provide foundational security, their granularity and flexibility are often insufficient for dynamic and collaborative clinical scenarios, such as multi-disciplinary consultations. This limitation creates a critical challenge: balancing stringent security with operational practicality, as overly restrictive policies impede workflows while excessively lenient ones risk sensitive data leakage. To address this, we propose a novel hybrid access control model that enhances the ABAC framework by integrating data content and security level as core attributes for authorization decisions. This composite model enables context-aware and content-driven permissions, effectively supporting secure yet practical data sharing in healthcare environments.

title: Advanced Learning Based Quantum Key Distribution Framework for Practical Secure Communication Networks
authors: ShivaPrasanna. K, B.Rajesh Shyamala Devi, Ravichand S
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: The integration of Quantum Key Distribution (QKD) with Machine Learning (ML) has developed a high-end, secure model for accurate data transmission and network security. Many existing systems face several issues due to advances in quantum computing. Thus, integrating these models improved the efficiency, accuracy, and feasibility of QKD systems. In this paper, an Advanced Learning Approach (ALA) is combined with the Quantum Key Distribution Framework to refine key generation, error correction, and intrusion detection in quantum communication channels. The proposed ALA also used reinforcement learning algorithms to predict quantum channel fluctuations, dynamically adjust key exchange parameters, and strengthen authentication protocols. Simulation results demonstrate improved key rates, reduced Quantum Bit Error Rates (QBER), and enhanced resilience against eavesdropping attacks. The study contributes to the realization of practical, scalable, and intelligent quantum-secure communication systems—bridging the gap between theoretical quantum security and real-world network deployment.

title: Efficient Signal Detection Method Based on Sparse Bayesian Learning in ZP-OTFS Systems
authors: Xueping Lan, Xiaoxu Zhang, Pingzhi Fan, Zhengquan Zhang, Xianfu Lei, Yi Zhou, Li Hao, Zheng Ma
publish: 2025 11th International Workshop on Signal Design and Its Applications in Communications (IWSDA)
abstract: Orthogonal time-frequency space (OTFS) modulation has gained significant attention recently for its robust performance in doubly-selective wireless channels. However, designing high-performance signal detection methods for such channels remains challenging. To address this challenge, this paper proposes an efficient detection algorithm, delay-time domain maximum ratio combining with sparse bayesian learning (dtMRC-SBL), focusing on ZP-OTFS modulation. ZP-OTFS systems exhibit quasi-sparse characteristics due to the large number of active users. Consequently, the proposed signal detection operates in two stages. The first stage employs a dtMRC equalizer for coarse detection of high-signal-strength components. Subsequently, undetected low-signal-strength components are filtered. Given the lower signal strength of these components, SBL performs fine detection. The results from both stages are then aggregated to generate the final output. Compared to conventional MRC, the proposed dtMRC algorithm achieves lower complexity and better performance. The integration of the SBL algorithm yields significant performance improvements, demonstrating the effectiveness of the dtMRC-SBL scheme for quasi-sparse signal detection in ZP-OTFS systems.

title: QuadQ: Quadratic-Based Value Decomposition for Cooperative Policy Optimization in Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning
authors: Siying Wang, Ruoning Zhang, Yang Zhou, Jinliang Shao, Yuhua Cheng
publish: IEEE/CAA Journal of Automatica Sinica
abstract: Dear Editor, This letter introduces a value decomposition method based on a quadratic function for multi-agent reinforcement learning, aiming to derive cooperative policies among agents modeled by artificial neural networks. By constructing a quadratic function with specific parameter constraints, it ensures that the function achieves a global maximum when the local optimal actions are used as inputs. Based on this structured quadratic function, a novel value decomposition algorithm is proposed to facilitate optimal cooperative policy training within the centralized training with decentralized execution (CTDE) framework. Furthermore, a theoretical analysis is provided to guarantee the satisfaction of the individual-global max (IGM) condition. Finally, simulation results validate the effectiveness of the proposed method in solving relative overgeneralization problems.

title: Smart AR-Based Sign Language Recognition System for Real-Time Communication and Translation
authors: Parminder Singh, Kawal Preet Kaur, Samriti
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: Sign language users have not yet been properly connected to the rest of the world, through effective communication. In this paper, we present a state-of-the-art real-time sign language recognition and translation system that not only labels continuous hand gestures but also renders them into AR via dynamic speech bubbles. The recognized gestures are mapped into coherent English sentences which are translated into multiple languages (Hindi, Arabic, etc.) and vocalized via TTS synthesis. Our main framework incorporates hand tracking with MediaPipe, a sequential modelling of LSTM-based classification, and Google Translate API for multilingual translation. User interaction is controlled by the Tkinter toolkit within a graphical user interface (GUI). One important feature added to the system is the use of augmented reality (AR). In this setup, AR is used to show the translated or recognized sentence in a small floating bubble next to the user's hand. This appears in real time and makes it easier for users to see and understand the translation. The system uses buffering methods to avoid recognizing gestures too early, which helps the translated sentences flow more smoothly. Tests show that the system recognizes gestures accurately, responds quickly, and works well even with different languages. User feedback also shows that <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$A R$</tex> makes the experience more responsive and satisfying. Overall, this mix of technologies creates a helpful and easy-to-use tool for communication across different languages, with AR making it more engaging and practical.

title: Threshold Anonymous Credential Scheme Based on Attribute-Based Encryption
authors: Zhengxi Zhong, Hongyang Yan, Weichu Deng, Gongming Zhang, Zhexuan Ni
publish: 2025 International Conference on Cyber Resilience and Endogenous Safety &amp; Security (CRESS)
abstract: Decentralized anonymous credentials (DACs) enable users to prove possession of specific identity attributes without disclosing additional information or relying on a centralized authority. However, existing DAC schemes commonly rely on complex zero-knowledge proofs, resulting in high computational overhead. They also lack sufficient flexibility and efficiency in handling multi-authority environments and supporting complex access policies, while facing limitations in trust assumptions and scalability. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a novel threshold anonymous credential scheme. Specifically, we introduce a ciphertext-policy attribute-based encryption (CP-ABE) scheme that supports threshold key distribution and aggregation, and leverage it to construct the threshold anonymous credential scheme. Experimental results and security analysis demonstrate that the proposed scheme exhibits high efficiency and flexibility in constructing authentication for complex access policies.

title: SP $^{2}$ Join: Secure and Practical Multi-Table Pairwise Equi-Join Queries over Encrypted Databases
authors: Sen Zhao, Siyi Lv, Zheli Liu, Fuchun Guo, Xiaofeng Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dependable and Secure Computing
abstract: Practical equi-join queries over encrypted databases aim to provide clients with flexible query capabilities while safeguarding data confidentiality. So far, the most promising and practical solution is CHT, which is based on Trusted Execution Environments (TEEs). However, such schemes can only support two-table equi-join queries, which are rather limited compared to Multi-Table Pairwise Equi-Join (MTPEJ). Furthermore, in terms of efficiency, throughput, and access pattern, the join implemented in TEEs is still problematic compared to a practical and secure scheme. In this work, we focus on this important type of equi-join, which facilitates join across any two tables among multiple tables. Specifically, we propose SP <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$^{2}$</tex-math></inline-formula> Join in this paper, the first MTPEJ framework that supports secure pairwise equi-join among multi-table by leveraging a novel oblivious pipeline join algorithm in TEEs. To validate our approach, we evaluate SP <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$^{2}$</tex-math></inline-formula> Join in MySQL using the benchmarks and real datasets, and the results demonstrate that SP <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$^{2}$</tex-math></inline-formula> Join is superior to the state-of-the-art in terms of secure equi-join. Particularly, our pipeline join query time is only 2.68% of CHT, yet throughput can reach up to 16.56 × of CHT. Even for MTPEJ, it is still 14.95% of CHT when implementing pairwise equi-join among 10 tables, with a time cost of approximately 140.30 ms.

title: Fabrication of High-Resolution Digital Torque Wrenches With Quartz Crystal Force Sensors
authors: Cheng-Yi Wu, Qiushi Liu, Kun Li, Gangyin Ding, Jung-Chieh Chen, Jiahui Liang, Chin-Yin Chen, Chang-Chun Zhao, Min-Chiang Chao
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: A sensor head of quartz crystal resonant chips was developed as a force/torque sensor, incorporating parameters such as resolution, sensitivity, and response time. This sensor was applied to the torque detection technology of digital torque wrenches. The main body of the digital torque wrench was mechanically designed and simulated in advance to concentrate mechanical energy within the groove structure where the sensor head was installed. Compared with traditional strain gauge-based digital torque wrenches, which typically exhibit a measurement resolution of more than 1%-FS, the digital torque wrench utilising the quartz crystal force sensor head achieved a detection resolution of less than 0.2%-FS. Additionally, the reliability of the digital torque wrench was verified, and the results demonstrated its effectiveness.

title: Security-Trust-Determinism Co-Design Using Hybrid Intrusion Detection With Temporal Modeling for Real-Time Publish-Subscribe Middleware
authors: Ramakrishna Penaganti
publish: IEEE Communications Standards Magazine
abstract: This paper proposes a trust-aware hybrid intrusion detection (IDS) for real-time pub/sub middleware that jointly targets security effectiveness and determinism preservation. The proposed approach fuses a low-latency inline detector with a temporal convolutional network (TCN) through a calibrated score-level combination, and then aggregates the fused evidence using a lightweight Bayesian trust engine to maintain continuous trust scores for middleware entities. Trust-guided enforcement is constrained by deterministic quality of service (QoS) budgets to avoid degrading real-time guarantees. Experiments on the publicly available UNSW-NB15 benchmark demonstrate that the hybrid design improves separability and robustness to time-dependent attack behaviors while remaining compatible with real-time execution. The obtained results of the proposed work establish a practical security–trust–determinism trade-off for deploying hybrid machine learning-based IDS in real-time middleware environments.

title: Connected Vehicular Network Ground-to-Air Target Tracking Based on Bearings Measurements: Pseudolinear Information-Guided Strategy
authors: Zhihao Xiao, Luyao Zhang, Ning Liu, Tong Guo, Luhan Jin, Yao Mao
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Vehicular Technology
abstract: This paper investigates ground-to-air Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) tracking using bearing-only line-of-sight (LOS) measurements provided by electro-optical tracking systems (ETS) mounted on a connected vehicular network. The key challenges arise from asymmetric mobility constraints and limited observability in three-dimensional space. To address these issues, a closed-loop tracking framework is proposed that integrates state estimation with ground vehicle coordination. A distributed pseudolinear filter is developed to handle the nonlinear nature of bearing-only observations and improve estimation stability in a decentralized setting. On top of this, an information-theoretic guidance strategy is formulated by adapting the Fisher Information Matrix (FIM) to ground-to-air sensing geometry, enabling explicit quantification of the relationship between sensor positioning and estimation accuracy. The framework employs Voronoi-based spatial decomposition to achieve scalable coordination among multiple ground vehicles, balancing estimation precision with spatial coverage. Extensive simulation results demonstrate that the proposed method enhances tracking accuracy and improves target observability, offering a practical solution for UAV tracking under realistic conditions.

title: Load Disaggregation in Residential Appliances with Multiple States: Comparative Techniques
authors: Carlos A. Urzagasti, Isaac S. De Freitas, Oswaldo H. Ando, Euzeli C. Dos Santos, Fabiano Salvadori
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: This study presents a comparative analysis of load disaggregation techniques applied to residential appliances with multiple operational states, aiming to enhance Non-Intrusive Load Monitoring (NILM) capabilities. The research evaluates three appliances with multi-state consumption patterns (refrigerator, washing machine, air conditioner) using DATAPORT and UK-DALE datasets across four experimental scenarios: intra-household, inter-household, multi-household, and multi-dataset. The methodology employs the Torch-NILM framework to implement and compare five deep learning architectures: Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN), Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM), Sequence-to-Point (Seq2Point), Denoising Autoencoder (DAE), and Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU). Results reveal distinct technique-appliance specializations in the intra-household scenario: Seq2Point achieves optimal refrigerator disaggregation (F1-score: 0.92, MAE: 18.0W, RMSE: 32.0W), GRU excels for washing machines (F1-score: 0.76, MAE: 22.0W, RMSE: 90.0W), and CNN demonstrates superior air conditioner performance (F1-score: 0.85, MAE: 24.0W, RMSE: 80.0W). Cross-household transfer induces 15-20% performance degradation, with GRU exhibiting superior robustness (refrigerator: 0.78, washing machine: 0.68) compared to CNN and Seq2Point. Multi-household training recovers 8-10% of lost performance through implicit regularization from diverse usage patterns. multi-dataset evaluation (DATAPORT to UK-DALE) reveals catastrophic degradation (40-50% loss), with maximum F1-scores of 0.55 (GRU, refrigerator) attributed to sampling rate mismatch (1s versus 6s), regional electrical differences (120V versus 230V, 60Hz versus 50Hz), and cultural usage pattern divergence. These findings quantify fundamental limitations in current NILM architectures for cross-domain generalization and establish empirical benchmarks for technique selection based on appliance characteristics and deployment constraints.

title: FedAligns: A Federated Learning-Based IoT Intrusion Detection Method with Direction-Aligned Updates
authors: Keyuan Qiu, Zhejie Xu, Jianlin Lu, Xiaohui Xu, Shuangshi Zhao, Meifang Yan, Tao Luo, Dejie Yang, Zhigang Li
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: IoT intrusion detection faces critical challenges from device heterogeneity, non-iid data distributions, and malicious updates. To address these, we propose FedAligns, a robust and efficient federated learning intrusion detection framework. First, we design a Temporal Attention Autoencoder that integrates TCN, GRU, and self-attention to capture local patterns and global dependencies in complex IoT traffic, enhancing feature representation. Second, we introduce a dual-aggregation algorithm, AlignMSE, to tackle non-iid heterogeneity and malicious threats. Its first phase filters anomalous updates via temporal direction alignment and sign consistency to ensure robust aggregation. Its second phase dynamically allocates weights based on local Mean Squared Error, prioritizing high-performing clients to better adapt to non-iid data. Experiments on N-BaIoT and ToN IoT datasets show FedAligns significantly outperforms traditional methods in accuracy and robustness, demonstrating superior resilience against malicious updates in non-iid scenarios.

title: An EEG-Based Multi-Source Domain Knowledge Transfer Framework for Cross-Session and Cross-Subject Emotion Recognition
authors: Xiaoya Zhu, Haohao Zheng, Xiaolong Chen, Hong Zeng, Chenpu Li, Motonobu Hattori, Xiang Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Affective Computing
abstract: Multi-source domain adaptation (MSDA) aims to transfer knowledge from multi-source domains to an unlabeled target domain, making it highly suitable for cross-session and cross-subject emotion recognition based on electroencephalography (EEG). Nevertheless, current MSDA models primarily focus on the relationship between the global feature distribution of the source and the target domain while neglecting the relationship between local feature pairs, resulting in poor robustness. In this study, we propose an EEG-based Multi-Source Domain Knowledge Transfer Framework (MS-KTF) for cross-session and cross-subject emotion recognition. The MS-KTF uses a triplet loss combined with a maximum mean discrepancy (MMD) to achieve unbiased alignment at both the domain and data-pair levels of each pair data in the source and target domains. Moreover, the introduction of knowledge distillation enables MS-KTF to achieve better robustness without requiring additional training time. We evaluated the MS-KTF's performance on the DEAP, SEED and SEED-IV datasets for two-, three- and four-class emotion recognition, respectively. The experimental results demonstrated that MS-KTF achieves competitive performance in both cross-session and cross-subject scenarios.

title: Model Predictive Control of DAB Converters for RMS Inductor Current Minimization Under ZVS Constraints
authors: Nguyen Ngoc Nam, Young Il Lee
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Electronics
abstract: This paper introduces a model predictive control (MPC) method for dual active bridge (DAB) converters, which minimizes the root mean square (RMS) inductor current while ensuring zero-voltage switching (ZVS). First, the power losses that reduce DAB efficiency are analyzed. It is revealed that achieving optimal efficiency requires both minimizing the RMS inductor current and maintaining ZVS, thereby reducing switching, conduction, and magnetic component losses. Subsequently, an expression for the RMS inductor current is derived, which involves two decision variables: the inter-phase and outer-phase shifts. By formulating and solving an optimization problem that minimizes the RMS inductor current under ZVS constraint, the steady-state solutions for the inner and outer phase shifts are determined. To achieve fast-tracking performance and eliminate steady-state error, the MPC approach is designed based on the output filter. Additionally, Lyapunov stability theory is employed to guarantee system stability. The steady-state inner phase shift solution is used to satisfy the ZVS constraints, while the final outer-phase shift is determined based on the MPC approach and inter-phase steady-state solution, ensuring power transmission. To validate and highlight the effectiveness of the proposed method, extensive hardware experiments are conducted and compared with recent studies.

title: From Structure to Semantics: Hypergraph-Based AR Assembly Guidance with LLM-Mediated Narration
authors: Xinda Liu, Bowei Zhang, Jiaju Xu, Jian Wu, Guohua Geng, Lili Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: Effective Augmented Reality (AR) guidance for complex assembly faces a dual challenge: the inability of conventional liaison graphs to represent procedural logic, and the cognitive burden imposed by visual instructions. We argue that the solution requires a more expressive structure to overcome these representational deficits and a narration approach to mediate instruction complexity. Our method first employs an assembly hypergraph to capture the task's hierarchical information, from which an A* search algorithm generates an optimal assembly path. Then a Large Language Model (LLM)-mediated narration workflow is designed to address the ergonomic deficiencies of the machine-centric path. It employs an optimizer to improve fluency, followed by a narrator that crafts the steps into an intuitive instruction narration. A within-subjects user study (N = 24) revealed a progressive enhancement from our method's components. The transition from a liaison-graph baseline to the hypergraph alone improved objective outcomes by reducing task time and errors and improving subjective ratings (SUS, NASA-TLX, TAM, and ARI). Subsequently, augmenting the LLM-mediated narration maintained these gains while lowering cognitive load and elevating user experience and usability. Our findings indicate the value of our AR assembly design and discuss the opportunities of using LLM as a mediation layer for better user interaction.

title: Accuracy-Efficiency Trade-Off Analysis of Deep Learning Models for Breast Cancer Diagnosis Using Ultrasound Images
authors: Sunny Kumar, Abhijeet Dhanotiya, Jasmine Selvakumari Jeya I, Tushan Kumar Sinha, Anuj Gupta, Aldrin Joan Pandian W
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: This paper presents a comparative analysis of four deep learning architectures on an ultrasound image dataset of 2104 training and 529 test images of human breast, differentiating between three classes (benign, malignant, or normal). These models are evaluated on multiple parameters -accuracy loss, Cohen's Kappa coefficient, Area Under Curve (AUC), and computational efficiency. The results show InceptionV3 achieves the highest overall accuracy of 94.14 % with a Cohen's Kappa score of 0.9121, followed by DenseNet121, which provided competitive performance with 93.01 % accuracy and the lowest test loss of 0.2024. VGG16 delivered an accuracy of 92.06 %, whereas MobileNetV2 showed a lower accuracy of 86.96 %. Robust comparisons between models are ensured using statistical significance testing and highly sophisticated evaluation metrics. By tackling issues with model selection, accuracy over computational efficiency trade-offs, and deployment considerations, this study offers helpful insights for well-informed choices in deep learning applications.

title: Context-Aware Explanation Drift Detection (CA-EDD): For Plant Disease Severity Estimation
authors: Sonu Varghese K, R Satheesh Kumar
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: Deep learning models have shown high accuracy in automated plant disease classification; however, their black-box nature limits adoption in precision agriculture, where biological validity and interpretability are critical. Conventional Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) methods, such as Grad-CAM, generate visual saliency maps that often lack alignment with true pathological symptoms, leading to predictions that are accurate yet biologically inconsistent. This paper proposes SymptomConsistency Guided Explainable AI (SCG-XAI), a novel ContextAware Explanation Drift Detection (CA-EDD) framework that validates model reasoning against established plant pathology principles. The framework integrates an explanation generator, an explanation embedding module that encodes attribution maps into structured symptom descriptors capturing lesion color, texture, and spatial distribution, a temporal drift analyzer to detect shifts in model reasoning across disease severity stages, and a context integration layer that constrains explanation validity using agronomic criteria, specifically the Relative Lesion Height (RLH) and the Standard Evaluation System (SES) for rice sheath blight caused by Rhizoctonia solani. By evaluating the semantic consistency between model explanations and physiological disease symptoms, SCG-XAI enables the detection of logic drift that is not reflected in conventional performance measures. Experimental results on a multi-severity rice sheath blight dataset demonstrate that SCG-XAI maintains competitive classification accuracy while ensuring that model explanations are biologically consistent and trustworthy for real-world field deployment.

title: Smart Pill Dispenser with Facial Recognition and Health Monitoring
authors: Ranjini R, Sabarigirinathan C, Sanjay V, H. Joseph Prabhakar Williams
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: With the increasing need for personalised healthcare and medication adherence, smart healthcare devices have become essential. This paper presents a Smart Pill Dispenser with Facial Recognition and Health Monitoring, designed to ensure accurate medication delivery and real-time patient monitoring. The system integrates facial recognition for patient authentication, a microcontroller (ESP32) for intelligent control, and sensor-based health monitoring to track vital signs such as heart rate, temperature, and blood pressure. Simulation and prototype testing confirm accurate medication dispensing, reliable patient identification, and continuous health monitoring. The proposed system demonstrates promise for enhancing medication adherence, patient safety, and remote healthcare management.

title: Artificial Intelligence in Recruitment: Frameworks and Innovations
authors: Pankaj Khurana, Jimmy Singla
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: Now a days, the entire recruitment process is changed with the automation and artificial intelligence. There is no doubt that candidate screening, evaluation and shortlisting processes have been changed with the help of intelligent systems. In this paper, the latest developments of artificial intelligence - based frameworks and innovations for recruitment processes are reviewed. The existing studies use machine learning, natural language processing and deep learning techniques to improve resume parsing, job matching and interview analysis. Some existing studies are based on the combination of blockchain technology with gamification. In these studies, hiring process is more transparent, fair and engaging for all the stakeholders. The emerging trends from the existing studies are hybrid transformer-based models and smart recommendation systems. These models and systems are able to make sensible recommendations for employers of the organizations. Further there are some issues with these artificial intelligence-based systems for recruitment. These issues are data bias, privacy and explainability. Basically, this paper offers understanding of how intelligent systems are transforming the process of recruitment through automation and incorporation of artificial intelligence.

title: Detection of Algorithmic Bias in AI-Based Recruitment Systems Using Explainable AI
authors: P. Hari Krishna, K. Uday Kumar Reddy, G. Leela Aravinda, V. Anjali, C. Bhavana Reddy, SK. Abdur Rehman
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: This project is about an intelligent recruitment forecasting system that uses advanced machine learning models to evaluate and score candidates for hiring. The system combines Logistic Regression, Multi-Layer Perceptron (MLP), and XGBoost to make dependable predictions from candidate facts like age, experience, and education level, the skills, certifications, and requirements of the job. In order to raise the quality of data, the system gets job and candidate details ready, calculates skill intersections, and changes the categorical features into suitable numerical formats. The problem with the class that was solved was by using Random Over Sampling, so that the models remain stable and not biased during the training. The performance of the models is very well measured through the use of the different metrics like accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score together with the visualization tools such as confusion matrices and ROC curves, thereby enabling the recruiters to benchmark the algorithms and pick the one that is most effective for candidate evaluation. In order to open up more, the system uses Explainable AI methods like SHAP (Shapley Additive Explanations) and LIME (Local Interpretable Model-Agnostic Explanations). SHAP offers both global and local feature importance, while LIME clarifies how individual candidate features influence a prediction. These explanations make it possible for the recruiters to know the “hired” or “rejected” decisions' logic and thus help in establishing fairness, trust, and accountability. To sum up, this project is a proof of concept that the use of machine learning and explainable AI in recruitment can lead to a more secure decision-making process, fewer biases from algorithms, and a more consistent and morally right way of choosing candidates.

title: A Unified Modeling Framework for Cognitive Wildlife Monitoring
authors: P. Venkateswarlu Reddy, D. Ganesh, Sivakala Sridevi, Thupakula Venkata Srikala, Ukkusuri Arun Kumar, V. Yugesh
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: Automatic identification of common species plays a crucial role in biodiversity monitoring, ecological research, and agricultural management. However, existing approaches often struggle with inter-class similarities, intraclass variability, and limited generalization across diverse datasets. To address these challenges, this work proposes an automatic species identification method based on ensemble learning integrated with deep learning. The proposed framework leverages multiple deep neural architectures to capture complementary feature representations and employs an ensemble strategy to improve classification robustness and accuracy. By combining convolutional neural networks (CNNs) with advanced ensemble techniques such as stacking and weighted voting, the model effectively mitigates individual model biases and enhances generalization across species categories. Experimental validation of image datasets demonstrate that the proposed method achieves superior performance compared to single deep learning models with improvements in accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score.

title: WirelessEV-OPT: Metaheuristic Optimization of Resonant Frequency and Power Transfer Using PSO and GWO
authors: Anupriya S, K. Malarkodi
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: Wireless electric vehicle (EV) charging has become one of the potential successful solutions to traditional plug-in charging systems, but its efficiency is highly influenced by the misalignment of the coils, resonant frequency error, and thermal variations. In order to solve these problems, this paper presents WirelessEV-OPT; a hybrid metaheuristic optimization model combining Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) and Grey Wolf Optimization (GWO) to optimize power transfer efficiency and voltage stabilization in dynamic charging conditions. It has in place a comprehensive two-way wireless power transfer (WPT) model, which involves compensation networks, frequency restrictions and thermal limits. The hybrid algorithm PSO-GWO comes up with a multi-objective fitness criterion that combines coil alignment, resonant frequency selection as well as reduction of losses. Results of simulations show that simulation exhibits better convergence behavior and is more robust than single algorithm methods. WirelessEV-OPT is more efficient, less prone to misalignment, lower thermal spikes, and is more efficient in various loads. The next-generation wireless EV chargers infrastructure suggested has an optimization framework that is a scalable/intelligent togetherness that is capable of delivering a robust energy transfer in the face of real world uncertainties. The WirelessEV-OPT model is able to promote the sustainability of metropolitan mobility by using energy-efficient EV charging. Its wireless power transmission is optimized; it enhances transportation and urban energy infrastructure to encourage greener and smarter cities.

title: Towards Trustworthy LLMs: A Hybrid RAG-Enhanced, Attention-Driven Multi-Metric Framework for Model Evaluation and Comparison
authors: Suryaram S, Getzi Jeba Leelipushpam Paulraj, Stewart Kirubakaran S, Immanuel Johnraja Jebadurai
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: The rapid development of Large Language Models has transformed the field of NLP, enabling a range of applications from auto-generated content to intelligent conversational systems. This work proposes an overall hybrid interpretability and evaluation framework for transformer-based Large Language Models (LLMs), including multi-model comparison, Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG), attention-based mechanistic analysis, and multimetric performance scoring. It processes user queries with optional contextual information extracted from PDF, text, or CSV files and enhances the input using an offline RAG mechanism built with SentenceTransformer embeddings and FAISS indexing. Clickable evidence links retrieved from a locally cached NewsAPI dataset provide additional external context to ground the responses and reduce hallucination, improving their factual reliability. The interpretability module computes token-level attention importance directly from the encoder, producing normalized attention maps and visual heatmaps. Output length and inference speed are also measured, and all four metrics contribute to a weighted overall score: 40% faithfulness, 30% length, 15% coherence, and 15% speed. A comparison engine runs each model in sequence, using strict memory management, attention fallback handling, and CPU/GPU switching to avoid serialization and CUDA memory issues. An end-to-end visualization layer through Streamlit is provided with a comparison table of the models, score breakdown pie chart, attention heat map, and RAG retrieval display. Experiments show that the Flan-T5-XL strikes the best balance among interpretability, detail, and speed: faithfulness scores were between 0.86, coherence scores averaged 4.5/5, and the inference times were rather moderate (2-10} seconds) outperforming the other Flan-T5 family of models.

title: Synchronization Control Based on Sequential Convergence
authors: Huan Li, Shuangsi Xue, Hui Cao, Dongyu Li
publish: IEEE/CAA Journal of Automatica Sinica
abstract: Dear Editor, This letter focuses on the synchronization of system state components under the constraint of convergence sequence. Firstly, we introduce the predefined-sequence-synchronized control problem, a unique synchronization challenge where all state components must be synchronized to the origin following a predefined convergence sequence. A controller is proposed to solve the predefined-sequence-synchronized control problem in first-order affine systems, and the proposed controller can regulate the convergence sequence of state components by adjusting their ratios. Finally, the effectiveness of the theoretical findings is demonstrated through a numerical examnle.

title: Distributed Generalized Distributionally Robust Equilibrium Seeking for Dynamical Games Under Unknown Time-Varying Interference
authors: Longcheng Liu, Shuai Liu, Yiguang Hong, Lihua Xie, Guangchen Wang
publish: IEEE/CAA Journal of Automatica Sinica
abstract: This paper investigates a distributed generalized Nash equilibrium-seeking problem in stochastic dynamical systems, focusing on two key challenges: 1) nonlinear coupled constraints and nonlinear dynamics, and 2) nonconvex objectives influenced by disturbances with unknown time-varying distributions. To address these challenges, a distributionally robust game framework with an exact penalty is proposed. We introduce a first-order equilibrium concept suitable for nonconvex-nons-mooth settings and ensure finite-sample guarantees. Furthermore, a distributed zeroth-order feedback algorithm is proposed to solve the problem. This algorithm utilizes gradient estimators for the objective functions and subgradient estimators for the exact penalty terms. We provide a detailed analysis of the relationship between communication errors and the dynamic energy of the system, along with an expected upper bound for the zeroth-order gradient estimation. Our findings indicate that the expectation of the time-accumulated regret grows at a sublinear rate. Furthermore, as the distribution stabilizes, we show that the empirical distribution converges with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$O(1)$</tex> sampling complexity.

title: Swin-ResU-Net: Hybrid Transformer CNN for Spatial Segmentation of Satellite Imagery
authors: Tejas Chauhan, Rajakumar K, Charan Rajana, Kaviti Sravan, Sai Bhargav Kasetty
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: The resolution of satellite imagery has significantly improved due to the unprecedented growth in Earth observation technologies, necessitating the use of suitable and efficient semantic image segmentation techniques to perform spatial analyses. Most traditional CNN-based segmentation models fail to model long-range spatial dependencies and multiscale contextual relationships, which are inherent in satellite imagery. In this paper, the authors present a hybrid deep learning framework that combines the Swin Tiny Transformer with a ResNet50 backbone within a UNet-style architecture. The suggested model maintains spatial localisation through encoder-decoder skip connections and models global dependencies by utilising Swin Transformer's hierarchical selfattention mechanism. With the ResNet50 backbone, this enhances feature reuse by enabling precise gradient flow. The OpenEarthMap dataset, which featured a varied collection of globally dispersed land-cover samples, was then used to train and validate the model. With a peak validation IoU of 0.54 and an accuracy of 0.67, the experimental results show a progressive improvement over epochs, outperforming a number of baseline architectures in terms of both boundary precision and spatial consistency. The Swin-ResU-Net framework that was suggested brings together hierarchical transformer representations and convolutional feature learning so that the satellite imagery can be segmented spatially with precision. The results of the experiment show that the proposed method is not only robust but also generalizable, as it has consistently improved its performance across different evaluation metrics.
