title: DFE-Net: A Discriminative Feature Enhancement Network for Aircraft Detection and Recognition based on Spaceborne SAR Complex-valued Images
authors: Yiming Zhao, Qingbiao Meng, Guocun Xie, Youming Wu, Heming Yang, Wei Dai, Wenhui Diao, Xian Sun
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) aircraft detection and recognition play a crucial role in tasks such as flight scheduling and analysis of airport traffic. However, SAR images suffer from inherent sidelobe interference and speckle noise. These factors degrade the integrity of aircraft scattering features, thereby limiting the accuracy of fine-grained detection and recognition. To address these challenges, a discriminative feature enhancement network (DFE-Net) is proposed. The key lies in constructing a discriminative feature from SAR complex-valued images, where scattering invariants and characteristic variables are effectively decoupled and enhanced. A discriminative channel optimization (DCO) module is proposed to obtain a tri-channel representation for sidelobe and speckle suppression as well as target feature enhancement. DCO learns tri-channel interactions and adaptively fuses shared and specific cues. This mechanism expands inter-class separability and provides a robust feature space for fine-grained tasks. Furthermore, a contour-prior guidance (CPG) module fuses contour priors with deep features. This highlighting of critical contours enhances geometric consistency while reducing false alarms. By delivering stable and semantically enriched features, DFE-Net significantly improves detection and recognition performance under limited image quality. Extensive experiments on the SAR-CARD dataset from Gaofen-3 demonstrate that DFE-Net consistently outperforms existing baselines in terms of both accuracy and robustness.

title: Reaction-Uncertainty-Aware Motion Planning for Interactive Driving via Conditional Multi-modal Motion Prediction
authors: Hanbin Jang, Joonhee Lim, Abi Rahman Syamil, Dongsuk Kum
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: For autonomous vehicles to drive safely and in a socially collaborative manner, it is essential to consider interactions with surrounding agents. To model these interactions, recent motion planning works employ conditional motion predictions, which model the impact of the ego vehicle’s plan on surrounding agents by treating the ego’s future plan as query for prediction. However, most existing planning studies rely on uni-modal conditional motion prediction models, which struggle to represent the uncertain intentions of surrounding agents. Although a few studies adopt multi-modal mechanisms, they still fail to model reaction uncertainty—how surrounding agents respond to the ego’s future plan. In this paper, we propose a reaction-uncertainty-aware motion planning framework that integrates a newly designed conditional multi-modal motion prediction (CMMP) with a tree-structured policy planner (TPP). CMMP effectively models the reaction uncertainty of surrounding agents through multi-modal and causal masked attention mechanisms. TPP generates tree structured ego trajectory candidates and probabilistically evaluates them based on the multi-modal prediction results to enhance the robustness of decision-making. The proposed framework is validated on the large-scale real-world nuPlan dataset. Experimental results demonstrate that, compared to the baseline relying on uni-modal conditional motion prediction, our method achieves up to a 32.39% improvement in planning performance, particularly excelling in interactive scenarios.

title: TIGER: Task planning framework for Instruction following of GEneralized Robots
authors: Chulyong Lim, Jaewon Baek, Junhee Han, Wooyeol Bae, Woochul Nam
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Service robots require instruction following capabilities to perform various tasks, regardless of environmental changes. Thus, a task planner must accurately infer user intent even when human instructions are ambiguous. Furthermore, when initial plans become invalid due to unpredictable environmental states, the planner should modify its low-level policies and update target objects accordingly. To this end, we developed TIGER, a new task planner that generates highly reliable action sequences by deriving immutable subgoals from instructions. It also improves action quality by correctly selecting objects that match the user’s intent. Moreover, a task–representative one-shot strategy was proposed to improve the subgoal generation. Although TIGER uses ten times fewer examples for subgoal generation than a previous in-context learning model, it significantly improves high-level planning performance. Specifically, TIGER outperforms a previous LLM-Planner in ALFRED benchmark, increasing success rates from 15.09% to 35.06% on the seen set and from 19.73% to 42.57% on the unseen set. TIGER also successfully executed ambiguous instructions in multi-room layouts with cluttered scenes, even without prior knowledge of the environment. Furthermore, its scalability was verified in real-world experiments.

title: DBAF-Net: Dual Branch Alignment and Fusion Network for Remote Sensing Change Detection
authors: Yikui Zhai, Yuqi Li, Xueqiang Zheng, Yijun Zhou, Chaoyun Mai, Hao Xie, Xiaolin Tian, C. L. Philip Chen
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: The field of Remote Sensing Change Detection (RSCD) has witnessed remarkable progress. Most existing RSCD methods currently follow the encoder–feature interaction–decoder architecture. However, these methods often treat spatial details and semantic information in a combined manner during the feature interaction stage, leading to insufficient extraction of spatial and semantic features, while overlooking the distribution inconsistency between these features. Moreover, there is a lack of a unified alignment and fusion strategy. To address these issues, we propose a novel Dual Branch Alignment and Fusion Network (DBAF-Net). Specifically, the network is designed with a dual-branch structure in the feature interaction stage to achieve efficient differential feature extraction, where the Enhancement Space Difference Module (ESDM) and Semantic Aware Difference Module (SADM) separately extract spatial and semantic features from the image. To alleviate the feature distribution discrepancy between the two branches, we design a Cross Branch Alignment Fusion Decoder (CBAFD), which leverages domain adaptation and differential attention mechanisms for the deep fusion of spatial and semantic features. Finally, the refined features are progressively aggregated to generate the final change mapfrom deep to shallow layers. Comprehensive evaluations per formed on three well-established benchmark datasets—LEVIR CD, SYSU-CD, and UAV-CD, indicate that the proposed DBAF Net achieves superior change detection performance compared to current state-of-the-art methods. The source code are available at: https://github.com/yikuizhai/DBAF-Net.

title: Current Feedforward-Induced Instability in Diode Rectifier Unit-Based HVDC Integrated Grid-Forming WTGs System: Mechanism and Self-Stabilizing Control
authors: Haotian Yu, Renxin Yang, Jing Lyu, Xu Cai, Lie Xu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Power Electronics
abstract: The diode rectifier unit-based high voltage direct current (DRU-HVDC) integration for long-distance large offshore wind farms (OWFs) has garnered significant attention due to its economic potential. However, as an emerging isolated system consisting of multiple grid-forming wind turbine generators (GFM-WTGs), the potential instability risks threaten the reliable operation of the system, especially under light-load conditions of OWFs. To fill this gap, leveraging system small-signal sequence-domain single-input and single-output impedance models (SISO-IMs), this paper first demonstrates the system instability issues under the light-load conditions of OWFs. Subsequently, the dominant impact of current feedforward is identified via sensitivity analysis, and the right-half-plane zeros (RHZs) induced by current feedforward in the sequence-domain SISO-IMs of WTGs are revealed as the system instability mechanism. Besides, the limitations of stability improvement control by current feedforward attenuation and cancellation are analytically demonstrated. Consequently, a self-stabilizing control (SSC) strategy that integrates virtual impedance with adaptive filter-based current feedforward is proposed. The SSC effectively eliminates system instability risks by ensuring the positive resistance characteristics of WTGs under all operational conditions, while maintaining the dynamic voltage control performance. Ultimately, controller hardware-in-the-loop (CHIL) results are provided to substantiate the efficacy of the proposed SSC.

title: Evolutionary Hypergraph-assisted Hybrid Sampling for Imbalanced Data Classification
authors: Wenbin Pei, Fayang Wan, Weishi Han, Bing Xue, Yiu-Ming Cheung, Qiang Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Evolutionary Computation
abstract: In classification with imbalanced data, sampling methods have proven effective in rebalancing data distributions. However, most existing methods fail to adequately consider higher-order relationships (i.e., complex interactions) among instances. This often results in the exclusion of potentially informative instances during the undersampling process and thereby undermines the classifier’s ability to accurately distinguish between classes. To overcome this drawback, we propose a novel evolutionary hybrid sampling method that explicitly leverages hypergraph-based higher-order relationship modeling with a genetic algorithm to select good-quality instances. In the proposed method, after oversampling the imbalanced data, hypergraphs are employed to model the complex interactions among the oversampled data. The obtained interaction information is then utilized to guide genetic algorithms to select informative and representative instances that reflect the core characteristics of the data and are essential for a classifier to distinguish different classes. Experiments have been conducted on 18 imbalanced datasets1 from the KEEL repository, which covers diverse application domains, e.g., biomedical classification, material analysis, and food quality assessment. Experimental results show that the proposed method outperforms baseline sampling methods in helping four types of classifiers (Random Forest, Decision Trees, Gradient Boosting Decision Tree, and k-Nearest Neighbors) to improve their performance in imbalanced classification. Statistical significance tests (including Friedman, Bonferroni-Dunn, and Wilcoxon tests with the significance level of 0.05) have also been conducted, demonstrating statistically significant improvements. The proposed method is applicable to real-world scenarios involving class imbalance issues, such as medical diagnosis and industrial defect detection.

title: Security Analysis of the Session Initiation Protocol (SIP): Vulnerability Assessment and Threat Modeling
authors: Yaroslav V. Smirnov, Anna V. Nikiforova
publish: 2026 ElCon Conference of Young Researchers in Computing &amp; Processing, and Information Security (ElCon-CP)
abstract: The widespread communication via the Session Initiation Protocol (SIP) presents a threat to confidentiality, integrity, and availability of communication. The paper presents a comprehensive security analysis of transferring data via SIP. The study aims to assess specific vulnerabilities in SIP data transmission and design a detailed threat model. The article concludes at a set of proposals to enhance security of SIP-based communication systems from identified threats.

title: Verifiability of SPIR Schemes with Malicious Users
authors: Emelie Ekenstedt, Lawrence Ong
publish: 2025 11th International Workshop on Signal Design and Its Applications in Communications (IWSDA)
abstract: Symmetric Private Information Retrieval (SPIR) ensures mutual privacy guarantees for users and servers in data access protocols - a property that is particularly important amid growing privacy concerns over pervasive data collection. This paper introduces the concept of private verifiability of SPIR schemes, enabling malicious user requests to be detected without compromising user privacy. We propose two schemes based on sending masked requests to an external verifying server and prove that internal verification among database servers is insufficient. These approaches enforce database privacy levels in PIR and SPIR schemes with no additional user input - in contrast to existing schemes.

title: Low Cost IoT Based Smart Home Automation with Energy Monitoring
authors: Vikraman S, Vijayapriya P
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: Home automation is a cutting-edge technology that has developed in modern times. As smartphones have become a part of daily life, people aspire to lead a smarter life with the aid of them. The smartphone enables them to save time and lead a more comfortable life using intelligent techniques. This is because certain household devices must be activated prior to their intended use. This article provides a solution that enables web-based and switch-based control of household appliances along with a mobile application with a wireless, low-cost home automation system that integrates IoT and energy monitoring. Moreover, an open-source, cloud-based IoT application has been created that offers the status of appliances and supports energy monitoring of household devices. This application also assists in detecting power theft. This home automation system is based on IoT that allows individuals who wish to simplify their lives to have greater accessibility to their household appliances.

title: SmartBi-BDC: Hybrid Machine-Learning-Driven Control for Non-Isolated Bidirectional DC-DC Converters in Electric Vehicles
authors: Rajesh Kumar Mohapatra, S. Gomathi
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: The high rate of electrifying transport requires sophisticated power-electronic control techniques that may guarantee high effectiveness, thermal stability and secure bidirectional energy transmission in electric vehicle (EV) converters. Fixed-parameter MPC Traditional controllers like PI/PID and fixed-parameter MPC have difficulties in operating during nonlinearities, aging of components and dynamic driving conditions. This paper has presented a hybrid machine-learning-based control of non-isolated bidirectional DC-DC converters to apply to EV powertrains with SmartBi-BDC. The architecture proposed combines surrogate neural models, where fast converter-state prediction is done, with deep reinforcement learning (DRL), where long-horizon optimization is done, model predictive control (MPC), where formal constraint enforcement is done, fuzzy logic, where mode scheduling is done and finally an autoencoder-based anomaly detector, where safety and reliability are ensured. Simulations of the high fidelity, surrogate-accelerated MPC, and hardware-in-the-loop validation exhibit better performance based on critical metrics: voltage regulation, conversion efficiency, regenerative braking energy capture, thermal stress reduction and fault-response latency. SmartBi-BDC demonstrates the real-time capabilities on embedded automobile processors and is therefore a prospective next-generation <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{E V}$</tex> converter controller. Such a hybrid paradigm offers a scalable, safe and adaptive platform of intelligent future EV power management systems.

title: Resilient Event-Triggered Control for MAS under DoS Attacks: Harnessing Data and Smart Edge Weights
authors: Wenrui Liang, Mingyang Yu, Ying Wan, Guanghui Wen
publish: 2025 International Conference on Cyber Resilience and Endogenous Safety &amp; Security (CRESS)
abstract: This paper investigates the data-driven event-triggered tracking control for multi-agent systems (MASs) subject to Denial-of-Service (DoS) attacks. To accelerate convergence and further reduce the number of triggering events, a time-varying edge weight update mechanism (EWUM) is introduced. A co-design theorem is established to ensure that all followers successfully track the leader, which leverages the input, state, and state derivative data collected from the agents to jointly determine the feedback control gain and event-triggering parameters. Finally, a practical application in platooning control for the established theory is conducted in simulations.

title: Next-Gen Electric Vehicle Architecture: IPMSM Design, Optimisation and Intelligent Control for Sustainable Transport
authors: Vijayalakshmi R, Devika K, Guruprasad V, Thirumalai Vel Ganesh A
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: This paper employs Artificial Intelligence (AI) techniques for the design and enhancement of an Interior Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motor (IPMSM), a crucial component in light electric vehicles (EVs). While the reference study employed finite element analysis (FEA) and OptiSlang to enhance efficiency and cut costs, the new strategy incorporates Random Forest (RF) and MultiLayer Perceptron (MLP) models to accelerate prediction and optimisation. The models take design parameters such as magnet gap, slot depth, tooth width, bridge thickness, and magnet volume as inputs to forecast performance metrics, including efficiency, torque ripple, cogging torque, reluctance torque, cost savings, and WLTC driving range. Comparative results demonstrate that the AI-driven system improves effectiveness, reduces torque ripple and cogging torque, lowers costs, and extends range, providing a swift and scalable alternative to conventional optimisation. The study shows that employing AI in motor design can significantly cut development time while maintaining accuracy, suggesting it holds considerable promise for future EV applications

title: Real-Time Water Leak and Usage Monitoring for Smart Apartments Using IoT Flow Sensors and LSTM Based Leak Detection
authors: M. Misba, Ramith Raveendran, B. Lakshmi Jaithri, Harigovind P
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: The approaching world water crisis based on the demand of water within the next five years it is predicted that there will be a 40% deficit of freshwater supply, emphasizes the necessity to manage water appropriately, especially in the built environment, which accounts for almost 15 percent of all freshwater usage. Residential houses are central to this problem, with residential leaks alone wasting as much as 9,400 gallons of water annually per family, or nearly 900 billion gallons nationwide. Beyond ecological problems, hidden leaks result in expensive economic and structural degradation in the form of compromised building structure, mold and mildew, increased utility bills, and diminished property value. Traditional leak detection techniques are reactive, identifying issues after extensive damage has been caused, whereas modern smart systems are known to be less accurate, have battery needs, and be prone to false alarms that erode user confidence. This paper introduces a new Internet of Things (IoT)-based system supported by a Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) anomaly detector and a module for leak localization to offer real-time water usage monitoring and leakage in smart apartments. The system utilizes flow and pressure sensors over inlet and branch pipes to continuously read data streams, whereas an LSTM autoencoder is used to learn patterns in consumption and identify anomalies with high precision. A differential flow-based localization algorithm is then integrated to predict the likely pipeline segment where a leak is happening, making prompt corrective measures. The suggested architecture utilizes opportunistic integration of edge and cloud computing to provide low-latency notifications, effective data processing, and actionable insights. Experimental results illustrate not only enhanced detection accuracy but also effective zone-level localization, thus supporting water conservation, homeowner cost savings, and predictive property security in smart homes.

title: Emotional Voice Reconstruction with Intonation Alteration and Language Model
authors: Ian Iakubchik, Anna Iakubchik
publish: 2026 ElCon Conference of Young Researchers in Computing &amp; Processing, and Information Security (ElCon-CP)
abstract: Modern robots or speaking software needs to speak with the correct emotions to have a rich and efficient communication with humans. In our previous work, we proposed a general method to change the emotional information in a given recorded utterance. However, the method shown can only be effectively applied when there is a language model which helps us split emotional information and semantic information from the recorded utterance. In this work, we propose a model for Russian language which includes 6 time points and 3 tempos to show the emotions and meaning. We then split the emotions from meaning with PCA method and change the intonation. The resulting speech was natural.

title: Cyberbullying Victimization and Youth Anxiety: A Support Vector Machines Analysis
authors: Saranya E, Satish N, Ranjith A, Harish A, Dhanapal C, Sridhar A
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: The domain of Cyberbullying has developed as a focus of concern in the digital age, with its profound, psychologically based consequences often described as strengthening the levels of anxiety among adolescents. This research study describes the association between cyberbullying and youth-aged anxiety, describing a machine learningsupportive model that leverages Support Vector Machine (SVM) to improve the accuracy rate of psychologically related risk detection. The proposed framework may define a systematic way of structurally modelling the related data sets patterns merged based on the behavioural patterns, and to make an experience depending on the data. This will enable the effective effectiveness for the betterment in the vulnerable and to have an immunity against to the individuals related external attacks. In the employment of SVM's ability to manage complex forms of trails and any nonlinear type of related information, the proposed system gives an adaptable way of approach for detecting starting time warning signs of anxiety relevant to the cyberbullying process.

title: Multimodal Recommendation System for Accurate Predictions Using RoBERT and VGG-16 with Co-Attention Mechanism
authors: Shilpa Mathpati, Sindhu P Menon
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: The recommendation systems play an important role in delivering customized content to users on all platforms, though there are challenges such as data sparseness, cold-start challenges, and insufficient knowledge of multimodal user preferences. Older systems based on single-mode data cannot convey the whole range of user preferences, and hence, they become less precise in real life. The present paper, to resolve these problems, introduces a multimodal recommendation system that merges text and pictures as data sources. One of the methods applied here is the ROBERTa transformer, which captures the underlying meaning of the user reviews, while the other is VGG-16, which models the product images. A co-attention technique is utilized to align and merge the two kinds of inputs into one user-item representation, which is further processed by a multi-layer perceptron to predict preferences. Several metrics are employed in the evaluation of the system, yielding the following results: MRR of 0.6867 and MAP 10 of 0.5645. This signifies that the system excels at relevant item ranking, achieving top-10 performance. The model has also marked a high intra-list diversity (ILD =0.90) and coverage (0.88).

title: Enhancing System Performance and Resilience: An MLOPs + SRE & Chaos Engineering Framework for Robust Machine Learning Operations
authors: Pramod Begur Nagaraj, Winner Pulakhandam, Sreekar Peddi, Swapna Narla, Nila Feby Puspitasari, Lalit Kumar
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: The growing use of machine learning (ML) systems in dynamic, mission-critical environments will raise significant issues of performance, scalability, and resilience that will need to be addressed. To tackle these challenges, contribute an integrated MLOps, SRE, and Chaos Engineering framework. This framework uses MLOps to automate updating and deploying machine learning models, incorporates SRE practices to measure system reliability and scalability, and implements Chaos Engineering to test system resilience in failure modes. Ultimately, implementing the right framework will provide assurances that machine learning models can be reliable in performance and recovery so that robust ML operations can be sustained in the real world.

title: Complete Charge-Based Artificial Neural Network Augmented Compact Model for Common Multi-Gate FETs
authors: Anamika Singh, Mohammad Ehteshamuddin, Abhishek Kumar, Mohit Shukla, Dariskhem Pyngrope, Srishti Parandiyal, Kumar Sheelvardhan, Amit Kumar Behera, Samrat Ray, Abhishek Somani, Sourajeet Roy, Avirup Dasgupta
publish: IEEE Transactions on Electron Devices
abstract: This work presents a novel charge-based artificial neural network (ANN) augmented compact model that bridges physics-based and data-driven modeling for advanced semiconductor devices. Unlike prior ANN or knowledge-based ANN (KBANN) approaches that predict only terminal characteristics, our model embeds analytical charge expressions to ensure physically consistent predictions of current, capacitance, and higher order derivatives, enabling accurate RF, transient, and large-signal analysis. Fully compatible with the industry-standard Berkeley short-channel IGFET model—common multi-gate (BSIM-CMG), the proposed model integrates seamlessly into conventional simulation workflows. It achieves up to 80% reduction in simulation time for a 101-stage ring oscillator (RO) while maintaining accuracy. Furthermore, its runtime scales very slowly with increasing circuit complexity, in contrast to the quadratic scaling of BSIM-CMG, making it a scalable and efficient solution for next-generation electronic design automation (EDA) tools.

title: Ferroelectric-Driven Asymmetric Band Engineering for Self-Powered Visible–SWIR Reconfigurable Photodetection
authors: Rongen Xiang, Xie Liu, Zhao Han, Jinlong Xu, Guodong Zhang, Qinyang Li, Qiancui Zhang, Ningning Zhang, Liming Wang, Huiyong Hu, Tian Miao, Zhangming Zhu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Electron Devices
abstract: The integration of ferroelectric materials with 2-D semiconductors offers a powerful route toward adaptive, low-power, and multifunctional optoelectronics. Here, we report a ferroelectric-gated broadband photodetector based on a WSe<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> lateral homojunction integrated on a PMN–PT substrate. Asymmetric graphene contacts expose only part of the WSe<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> channel to the ferroelectric polarization field, creating localized electrostatic potential gradients that enable ferroelectric-controlled formation of homo-PN and NP junctions. Consequently, the device exhibits nonvolatile rectification (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\gt\!10^{{3}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>) and a polarization-switchable, self-powered photoresponse under visible illumination (532 nm), enabling near-zero static power operation. Under 1550 nm illumination, a unipolar pyroelectric photocurrent arising from PMN–PT provides short-wave infrared (SWIR) detection, extending the spectral range. Scanning photocurrent mapping directly reveals a polarity reversal of the WSe<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> homojunction under opposite ferroelectric polarization states, confirming localized channel modulation by the substrate. Raman spectroscopy shows that only the nonshielded WSe<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> region experiences ferroelectric-induced phonon shifts, corroborating the asymmetric field-modulation mechanism. Driven by these dual physical mechanisms, we propose an event-driven readout scheme that delivers visible-band event imaging and SWIR fusion imaging on a single platform, facilitating robust perception in high-dynamic-range scenes such as tunnel exits. This work establishes a multifunctional route for integrating ferroelectric gating with 2-D semiconductors toward reconfigurable, broadband, and low-power vision systems.

title: Towards Robust and Efficient Ground Segmentation: A Dual-Stage Approach With Dynamic Parameter Optimization
authors: Pakzada Jilil, Tayir Mijit, Eksan Firkat, Bin Zhu, SarDar Parhat, Enyong Xu, Jihong Zhu, Askar Hamdulla
publish: IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Technology
abstract: Ground segmentation from LiDAR point clouds is a critical task in autonomous driving and intelligent transportation systems, as it directly affects road surface estimation, obstacle detection, and path planning. Existing methods, however, often degrade on sloped or irregular terrains and fail to preserve clear ground–object boundaries. To address these limitations, we propose a dual-stage ground segmentation framework with dynamic parameter optimization. In the coarse stage, adaptive plane modeling with slope-aware parameter adjustment enables efficient separation of ground candidates. In the fine stage, spherical depth map restoration andmulti-feature fusion refine boundary localization and recover fine-grained details. Extensive experiments on SemanticKITTI and Rellis-3D show that our method achieves 95.90% accuracy with 14.59 ms latency per scan in structured urban roads, while sustaining 90.64% accuracy in unstructured off-road environments. These results demonstrate that the proposed approach delivers both robustness and efficiency, meeting real-time requirements for autonomous driving applications. The source code of our method will be made publicly available at: <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/PKZD-JLL/DipGSegplus</uri>.

title: Fault Testing Circuit and Algorithm for PCRAM Array Based on Temperature Regulation
authors: Xiaojuan Lian, Yunbo Zhang, Zi Li, Shiyu Li, Chenfei Hua, Nan He, Zhikuang Cai, Lei Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Electron Devices
abstract: Emerging nonvolatile memories based on phase change memory (PCRAM) are expected to break through the performance bottlenecks of traditional memory systems. However, the intrinsic thermal sensitivity of phase change materials, coupled with the challenges of high-density integration, can easily induce special faults such as thermal interference and phase change threshold shift, causing to reliability degradation. To address these issues, this article proposes a temperature-regulated fault detection method based on a comprehensive thermal analysis of the device model. A simulation platform integrating defect-oriented (DO) and resistance defect (RD) models is established to support the design of a dual-path dynamic monitoring circuit. This circuit incorporates dual-margin comparators within a memory built-in self-test (MBIST) architecture, enabling real-time detection of compound faults through dynamic comparison of in-bitline (I<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">BL</sub>) and in-source line (I<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">SL</sub>) data paths. Additionally, a March-PCRAM fault detection algorithm is developed to exploit temperature gradients for activating fault-prone regions. Simulation and experimental results demonstrate that minor faults can be better detected through cyclic testing and temperature regulation, thereby reducing fault detection latency and enhancing test sensitivity. The proposed approach offers a robust testing framework and significantly improves the fault coverage of PCRAM arrays.

title: DiffPC: Diffusion-Based Projector Photometric Compensation
authors: Yuxi Wang, Haibin Ling, Bingyao Huang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: Projector photometric compensation corrects color distortions introduced by surface texture, reflection, and ambient lighting. Existing deep learning-based methods usually require professional scene-specific data collection and lack consideration for perceptual quality. To address this limitation, we present a diffusion-based photometric compensation method that reconstructs compensation images under photometric and content-aware guidance. Specifically, we first model the photometric distortions introduced during projection as environment-dependent additive noise, thereby reformulating the photometric compensation problem as a denoising task with physical constraints. Next, we introduce a diffusion model, which generates compensation images by following an additive trajectory to iteratively remove the noise. Finally, to accurately estimate the noise at each timestep, by analyzing the factors that contribute to distortions in the physical process of projection and capturing, we design a noise estimation network that incorporates features of both photometry-aware and content conditions. Experiments show that our method achieves superior visual performance in unknown scenarios, thereby exhibiting significant practical advantages over prior art. Our source code is available at https://github.com/cyxwang/DiffPC.

title: Front Cover
authors: N/A
publish: IEEE Vehicular Technology Magazine

title: Low-Cost Monocular End-to-End Autonomous Driving With Enhanced Interpretability
authors: Wei Dong, Yingfeng Cai, Hai Wang, Xiaoqiang Sun, Long Chen, Jianmei Lei
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: In recent years, end-to-end autonomous driving has achieved remarkable progress, yet challenges remain in achieving efficient deployment. Existing methods often rely on multi-modal sensor fusion, which incurs high costs and computational demands, limiting their large-scale adoption in low-cost scenarios. Reducing system cost while maintaining performance has consistently been a key focus of the industry. Moreover, the opaque nature of end-to-end models, due to the lack of intermediate representations, limits interpretability and hampers root-cause analysis when misjudgments occur in complex environments, thereby posing safety risks. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a lightweight and interpretable end-to-end autonomous driving architecture that utilizes only monocular camera images as input. The aim is to enhance decision-making efficiency and trajectory planning accuracy while ensuring system safety. The proposed architecture comprises two core components. First, in the scene perception part, a depth information supervision module is introduced to enable hierarchical geometric perception, and an online vector map construction module is designed to build structured environmental representations at low cost. This replaces high-definition maps with explicit constraint expressions, thereby improving model interpretability and decision-making reliability. Second, in the planning part, a dual-layer prediction framework is developed to decouple speed and waypoint prediction. It independently forecasts vehicle velocity and waypoints, while an optimized decoder structure produces fine-grained spatiotemporal trajectories, enhancing planning capabilities in complex traffic scenarios. Experimental results on multiple benchmarks from the CARLA Leaderboard 1.0 demonstrate that the proposed method significantly improves system efficiency while maintaining advanced decision-making performance. It outperforms existing multi-sensor fusion approaches, showcasing strong potential for practical deployment.

title: Towards Intelligent Radio Maps: Evaluation Metrics, Construction Schemes and Future Trends_supp1-3679645.pdf
authors: Cheng Li
publish: N/A
abstract: Intelligent radio maps (IRMs) have emerged as a critical enabler for next-generation wireless networks, offering comprehensive spatiotemporal awareness of the electromagnetic environment with limited sensing resources and low computational overhead. They play a crucial role in enhancing spectrum efficiency, enabling intelligent resource allocation, supporting anti-jamming communications, improving interference management, and facilitating environment-aware networking. This paper presents a systematic overview of how to construct high-quality IRMs. We first introduce six evaluation metrics aligned with practical deployment requirements and evolving wireless network demands. Guided by these metrics and recent advances in artificial intelligence (AI), we provide an in-depth review of spectrum sensing approaches and state-of-the-art methods for spectrum inference. We further explore the intrinsic connections between these two steps and propose an integrated sensing–inference construction scheme. Extensive experiments demonstrate that the integrated scheme achieves superior IRM construction performance under sparse sensing, validating its practical potential for future wireless networks.

title: Table of Contents
authors: N/A
publish: IEEE Circuits and Systems Magazine

title: An Electrically Implicit FDTD Method Based on the Newmark-Beta Scheme
authors: Faxiang Chen
publish: IEEE Antennas and Wireless Propagation Letters
abstract: In this paper, an electrically implicit finite-difference time-domain (FDTD) method with the stability condition which is weaker than that of the conventional FDTD method is proposed. The Newmark-Beta scheme appearing in the Magnetically mixed Newmark-Leapfrog (MNL-) FDTD method recently is used to formulate updating equations for electric field components, and the updating equations of the magnetic field components are identical to those in the conventional FDTD method. The proposed method contains the modeling of frequency-dependent materials without generating derivatives of current and is able to handle frequency-dependent materials more naturally compared with the MNL-FDTD method. Then the memory-efficient implementation of the proposed method based on divergence is also proposed, which is difficult in the MNL-FDTD method for dispersive media. Numerical tests validate the proposed method.

title: Efficient Transformer Models via Language-Aware Frequency-Based Vocabulary Pruning
authors: Mukhlish Fuadi, Adhi Dharma Wibawa, Surya Sumpeno
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Multilingual Transformer models offer effective cross-lingual generalization capabilities. However, their architecture suffers from embedding-parameter overhead due to massive vocabulary sizes, where most embedding parameters are redundant for specific monolingual tasks. This study investigates language-aware frequency-based vocabulary pruning as a deterministic and reproducible strategy to mitigate this inefficiency without architectural modifications or additional large-scale pre-training. We evaluate the mDeBERTa-v3-base model under monolingual (Indonesian) and hybrid (Indonesian–English) vocabulary configurations on Named Entity Recognition (NER) and Sentiment Analysis (SA) tasks. Multi-seed experiments show that reducing the vocabulary to 30k tokens lowers peak GPU memory usage during inference from 6.64 GB to 4.70 GB (≈29%), while maintaining comparable inference latency and minimal performance degradation, with an average drop of less than 1.5% across entity-level (NER) and sentence-level classification (SA) F1-scores. Further analysis attributes the hybrid model’s stability to substantial cross-lingual subword overlap (over 24,000 tokens) and efficient tokenization behavior, reflected in a subword fertility of approximately 1.008, indicating minimal word fragmentation. Statistical significance testing confirms that the hybrid model’s performance on sentence-level classification tasks shows no statistically significant difference compared to the baseline (p > 0.05). Although evaluated on Indonesian as a representative case study, the results illustrate the potential of this language-agnostic framework as a practical solution for optimizing Transformer models in resource-constrained deployment scenarios.

title: Advancing Micro-Expression Recognition: a Task-Specific Framework Integrating Frequency Analysis and Structural Embedding
authors: Zheng Li, Yuan Zong, Cheng Lu, Tianyi Zhang, Jingang Shi, Xin Liu, Wenming Zheng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Affective Computing
abstract: Micro-expression recognition (MER) remains a challenging task due to the subtlety and low intensity of facial motion patterns. Existing deep models often struggle with spectral bias and structural mismatches when applied to MER, leading to notable performance degradation. To tackle these issues, we propose a task-specific framework named Adaptive Attenuation Compensated Polarity Network (A2CPN) that introduces two key innovations. First, we conduct a frequency analysis paradigm to reveal the degradation of high-frequency motion cues in deep networks. To mitigate this, we propose a layer-wise compensation method to preserve decision-relevant cues. Specifically, frequency and spatial-domain features are projected into a shared wavelet feature space in a band-wise manner, while the hierarchical compensation mechanism establishes a mapping between frequency specific attenuation levels and network layer indices to adaptively enhance ME motion cues. Second, we embed facial structural priors via a polar coordinate system. It constructs correlations among radial cells, aligning the model's representations with radial motion-distribution patterns. Extensive experiments on four benchmark datasets, CASME II, SAMM, SMIC, and MEGC2019, demonstrate state-of-the-art (SOTA) performance, validating the effectiveness of our frequency-domain and radial analysis paradigms, offering new insights for MER research.

title: Project Resource and Schedule Optimization for Maximum Productivity Under Budget Constraints
authors: Mohit Mathur, Nishant Kumar
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: The topic of this research is how to allocate limited resources of projects among a number of development teams in order to maximize the overall productivity and maintain an overall budget constraint. The problem is typical in most of the industries, as the productivity of different teams and the level of efficiency are not similar, and simple allocation rules are not the best choice. To address this nonlinear constrained problem, the research devises a concave objective problem and employs four commonly used optimization methods, namely the Bisection method, the Newton method, the Steepest Descent method and Conjugate Gradient method. The analysis is performed on four different test scenarios; each one describes a particular project environment and budget constraint. Due to the concave nature of the objective function, all the algorithms though they may have a different search strategy, tend towards the same global optimum. Such convergence proves the mathematical model strong and that the different methods of getting solutions are consistent and it can be assured that, the suggested allocation strategy will closely work in reality. Future research can consider the possibility of dynamic adjustments in which team performance data is continuously updated, and the allocation algorithm can adjust to the unexpected alterations. Also, sensitivity analysis would be able to measure the impact of minor changes in budget on optimal distribution, thus helping managers in contingency planning.

title: Revolutionizing Healthcare with Blockchain: A Review
authors: Greeshma R, Gladston Raj S
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: This review examines the adoption of blockchain in healthcare by analyzing integration models that combine distributed ledgers with IPFS, IoT systems, cloud services, and machine-learning components. The primary aim is to evaluate how these hybrid approaches support secure data exchange, interoperable record management, and decentralized processing. Focusing on studies published between 2020 and 2025, the review synthesizes architectural strategies implemented in healthcare contexts and presents a conceptual EHR–blockchain workflow to illustrate data flow, storage, and integrity mechanisms. It further compares major healthcare blockchain platforms, assessing their design objectives, security features, and suitability for clinical environments. The review also identifies research gaps that need to be addressed to strengthen practical deployments. Finally, it discusses current challenges like scalability, privacy, and regulatory alignment and outlines mitigation strategies and future directions to advance secure, interoperable, and patient-centric healthcare systems.

title: Mitigating ORM-Induced SQL Injection via Keyword Randomization
authors: Pengwei Yin, Gaofei Zhang, Hao Liu, Yuan Yao
publish: 2025 International Conference on Cyber Resilience and Endogenous Safety &amp; Security (CRESS)
abstract: The research on identifying and intercepting SQL injection attacks (SQLIA) using runtime SQL detection methods has gradually drawn more and more attention, but the complexity of the architecture, excessive overhead, or strong invasiveness make it difficult for this research to be widely applied. This research utilizes the idea of randomization, using the commonly used Object-Relational Mapping Frameworks (ORMF) in Java engineering development as a carrier, to implement a runtime intrinsic secure SQLIA defense method, SQLFR. SQLFR can integrate a set of interception mechanism for SQLIA defense without making any changes to the architecture and code of the original project. Experimental results demonstrate that SQLFR is capable of defending against not only traditional SQLIAs, but also those capable of bypassing conventional defense mechanisms in SQL frameworks, while maintaining an extremely high interception rate and accuracy.

title: Optimization of Solar PV Systems Using Classical Methods
authors: Niyanta Prasad, Nishant Kumar
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: This paper, Optimization of Solar Photovoltaic system, addresses the issue of maximising energy efficiency under diverse environmental conditions. The performance of Solar Panels highly depends on various non-linear factors like solar irradiance, tilt angle, ambient temperature, MPPT voltage, inverter efficiency and battery dispatch in a hybrid system. This formulates as a constrained optimization problem where we can apply the following optimization algorithms: Steepest Descent, Newton's Method, Quasi Newton, Conjugate Gradient and Line Search. There are four test cases that are analysed here are residential rooftop PV system, utility-scale solar farm, hybrid PV-battery microgrid and tropical off-grid village and all these scenarios have real-world data related to the parameters mentioned above like irradiance, temperature etc. Based on the comparison analysis we see that Quasi Newton and Line search deliver the best performance. Both these algorithms are shown to achieve rapid convergence and robust accuracy even under ill-conditions. The Quasi-Newton with the Hessian approximation is faster and more stable. Line search on the other side ensures optimal step size and improves efficiency. The other method like Newton's Method has higher precision but is numerically unstable whereas Steepest Descent and Conjugate Gradient have given moderate results. Thus, the study concludes that PV efficiency and reliability can be significantly improved using the gradient-based algorithms with adaptive step-size strategies

title: A Comparative Analysis of Edge and Cloud Architectures for Real-Time Industrial Control Systems
authors: Maxim S. Silantiev, Maxim S. Minin
publish: 2026 ElCon Conference of Young Researchers in Computing &amp; Processing, and Information Security (ElCon-CP)
abstract: Traditional Industrial Control System architectures that rely on sending raw sensor data to a centralized cloud face three critical challenges: high latency, unreliability upon connection loss, and massive data traffic costs. For real-time control loops, this latency is unacceptable. This paper analyzes the «Edge Computing» paradigm as a solution. This approach involves moving computational tasks from the distant cloud directly «to the edge» of the network – onto local gateways or industrial controllers on the factory floor. Instead of transmitting gigabytes of raw streams, the Edge device processes data locally, sending only valuable, processed results to the cloud. The analysis demonstrates that Edge Computing radically reduces latency to the millisecond level, ensures autonomous system operation during connectivity failure, and significantly conserves network bandwidth. This makes Edge Computing a key enabling technology for building responsive, resilient, and scalable Cyber-Physical Systems.

title: A Text-Driven Attentive Approach for 3D Shape Segmentation_supp1-3679038.pdf
authors: chenyu zhu
publish: N/A
abstract: 3D shape segmentation is an essential task in computer graphics and is widely used in many applications. It plays a critical role in understanding the structure and semantics of 3D models. Traditional approaches to 3D shape segmentation primarily rely on geometric features to partition models into meaningful components. However, these methods often struggle when the geometric characteristics of different parts are similar, resulting in ambiguous segmentation outcomes. To address this fundamental limitation, we introduce a novel text-driven multi-modal framework that systematically integrates textual semantics with geometric analysis for enhanced 3D shape segmentation. Our approach leverages a pre-trained language model with prefix tuning to bridge the semantic granularity gap between part-level annotations and face-level segmentation, while a specialized mesh self-attention module captures contextual relationships among neighboring faces. We design an attention-based text-driven integration mechanism that dynamically weights multimodal features, complemented by a Laplace-Adaptive Attention Module (LAAM) that better handles the distributions of geometric features. Through contrastive learning, we align textual and geometric representations in a shared semantic space, enabling effective disambiguation of geometrically similar but semantically distinct parts. We also contribute the Fine-grained HumanBody benchmark for comprehensive evaluation. Extensive experiments on Princeton Segmentation Benchmark, COSEG, ShapeNetCore, and our proposed benchmark demonstrate that our method significantly outperforms existing approaches, achieving superior segmentation accuracy while effectively resolving geometric ambiguities through semantic understanding.

title: MFTriNet: A Multi-modal Fusion Triple-flow Network for Remote Sensing Image Semantic Segmentation
authors: Yan Wang, Li Cao, He Deng
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: The fusion of high-resolution remote sensing imagery (HRRSI) with digital surface models (DSM) provides complementary spectral and height information, significantly enhancing the potential for accurate semantic segmentation. However, effectively reconciling the inherent domain disparities between these modalities and mitigating noise from defective data sources remain challenging. While prevailing encoder-decoder networks facilitate cross-modal fusion, their performance often degrades in complex scenes due to limited robustness to inter modal discrepancies and noise. To address these challenges, we propose MFTriNet, a novel multi-modal fusion triple-flow network that jointly considers modality-specific characteristics and cross-modal interactions. MFTriNet decomposes the segmentation task into two modality-specific flows and one modality complementary flow. The two modality-specific flows, sharing identical encoder-decoder architectures, independently process HRRSI and DSM data to generate preliminary segmentation predictions and preserve unique modal characteristics. In the modality-complementary flow, each encoding layer incorporates a dedicated modality-induced feature modulator (MFM) designed for feature alignment and discrepancy suppression, utilizing a strategy of correction followed by fusion, and each decoding layer integrates a modality-aware dynamic aggregation module (MDAM) for dynamic and gated attention-based fusion. The final prediction is generated by a learnable weighted fusion of outputs from all three flows. Extensive experiments on the ISPRS Vaihingen and Potsdam benchmarks demonstrate that MFTriNet achieves state-of-the-art performance, with mIoU/OA values of 84.21%/92.05% and 86.78%/91.78%, respectively. Furthermore, evaluations on the more challenging MMHunan dataset verify the effectiveness and generalization capability of MFTriNet in complex scenes. The source code is available at: https://github. com/YanWang-WHPU/MFTriNet.

title: GSCC: Graph-Structural Relationship Quantification for Remote Sensing Image Change Captioning
authors: Heming Yang, Yu Tian, Dongshuo Yin, Yiming Zhao, Xian Sun, Hongfeng Yu, Chubo Deng
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Remote Sensing Image Change Captioning (RSICC) is critical for interpreting Earth surface dynamics from bi-temporal imagery. Current approaches focus on isolating change regions in bi-temporal images, which is essential for bridging the semantic gap between multi-modal data and generating accurate captions. These approaches usually fall into two categories: relying on feature subtraction or auxiliary masks. However, subtracted features fail to distinguish true change regions and are allergic to noises, while auxiliary masks from annotated labels or pre-trained models introduce additional annotation and computational costs. In this work, we propose GSCC, a novel RSICC framework featuring a Change Feature Filtering Module (CFFM) and a Feature Fusion Module (FUM). The CFFM introduces a difference vector to quantify graph-structural relationship alterations across bi-temporal representations, which enables the model to selectively enhance significant changes without the need for external supervision. The FUM integrates multi-stage features and enriches semantic information for accurate caption generation. Extensive experiments on LEVIR-CC show GSCC outperforms state-of-the-art methods, demonstrating gains in semantic alignment and robustness. Code is available at <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/RiHY99/GSCC</uri>.

title: An Enhanced Ship-Speed Prediction Model With Stacking Ensemble Learning
authors: Jinfeng Zhang, Yueqi Zhang, Ran Yan, Weihao Ma, Min Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: In the field of maritime transportation, precise accurate prediction of ship-speed is paramount for route planning, ship scheduling, and navigational safety. However, the difficulty of the prediction lies in the fact that ship-speed is influenced by numerous complex and variable factors, and traditional speed prediction methods often struggle to attain the desired accuracy in the presence of complex maritime environments and ship characteristics. To address this challenge, this paper aims to develop a robust ship-speed prediction model capable of effectively capturing complex data relationships and improving prediction accuracy and stability. A stacking ensemble learning model is proposed, integrating extra trees (ET), random forest (RF), categorical boosting (CatBoost), extreme gradient boosting (XGBoost), light gradient boosting machine (LightGBM), and histogram gradient boosting (HGBT) as base learners, with support vector machine (SVM) as the meta-learner to leverage diverse models’ strengths. A standardized workflow for data cleaning, multi-source data fusion, and feature engineering is established. Additionally, the SHapley Additive exPlanation (SHAP) is introduced for model interpretability. Experiments with historical trajectory data from five ships and meteorological-oceanographic data show that the stacking model outperforms single models in prediction accuracy and stability. SHAP analysis reveals that ship course and wave height are key influencing factors, with their impact varying across different navigation scenarios. The proposed model enhances operational efficiency, safety, and decision-making in the maritime industry by providing reliable speed predictions and interpretable insights.

title: YPCAS Presents “Sustainability and Eco-Design in Electronics” at NEWCAS 2025 in Paris [CASS Conference Highlights]
authors: Yang Jiang, Bokyung Kim, Patricia Desgreys
publish: IEEE Circuits and Systems Magazine
abstract: IEEE Young Professionals in Circuits and Systems YPCAS organized a successful professional development event on Tuesday 24 June 2025 at the historic former Faculty of Medicine, University of Paris, France, as part of the IEEE Interregional NEWCAS Conference NEWCAS 2025. Under the theme Sustainability and EcoDesign in Electronics, the event brought together experts, researchers, and students to explore sustainable microelectronics design, circular electronics, life-cycle assessment, and eco-innovation, attracting significant participation and fostering exchanges between established professionals and early-career researchers.

title: AsymSAM:Asymmetric Feature-Enhanced SAM for Remote Sensing Polygonal Buildings Mapping
authors: Ancheng Liu, Mengke Yuan, Tong Wang, Weize Quan, Dong-Ming Yan
publish: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Letters
abstract: Accurate polygonal building extraction requires not only region-level segmentation but also geometrically consistent vertex modeling. However, existing SAM-based adaptations primarily optimize mask quality and lack explicit mechanisms for structured polygon prediction, leading to irregular contours and unstable vertex localization, especially under cross-domain scenarios. To address these limitations, we propose AsymSAM, an asymmetric feature-enhanced framework based on the Segment Anything Model (SAM) for polygonal building extraction. AsymSAM integrates an asymmetric hierarchical feature enhance scheme and a hybrid fusion module in vanilla SAM. These components effectively leverage multi-level spatial and semantic information to enhance the representation of building contours and structural cues. Additionally, we extend the mask decoder to support joint prediction of polygon vertices and boundaries, enabling both automatic and prompt-guided extraction modes. Experiments on the WHU-Mix and SpaceNet datasets show AsymSAM consistently outperforms existing methods in terms of Average Precision (AP), Average Recall (AR), and Intersection over Union (IoU).

title: Real-Time Short-Term Delay Compensation for Optoelectronic Tracking Control System Under Uncertainty of Target Model
authors: Xiushuo Wang, Zhaobing Chen, Letang Xue, Zhaolong Wu, Xiangru Ding, Lihua Cao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Control Systems Technology
abstract: To address various short-term delay issues caused by detectors, fast steering mirrors (FSMs), and lasers in the photoelectric tracking system (PTS) based on a moving platform, this article presents a prediction method that integrates an improved tracking differentiator (TD), an adaptive zero-order hold (AZOH), and a Markov probability correction model. The method utilizes the tracking differentiator with speed feed forward and rapid exponential integral function (SFREITD) to extract velocity information from delayed data and enables initial prediction of global stochastic data. Additionally, the AZOH is introduced to address the issue of data prediction failure when the velocity is zero. Finally, the Markov probability model is applied to correct the prediction results. The superior performance of SFREITD has been fully verified through time-domain simulations in MATLAB. Meanwhile, based on a single-detector photoelectric platform, the prediction trajectories and prediction errors of inertial spatial angles are presented. The results demonstrate that the proposed scheme offers greater practical application value than traditional prediction methods such as segmented data fitting and long short-term memory (LSTM) networks and can effectively resolve the delay problem of global stochastic data.

title: Additively Manufactured Multi-Material Dual-Band Millimeter-Wave Reflectarray for Beyond 5G
authors: Jianfeng Zhu, Xinmin Lian, Yang Yang
publish: IEEE Journal of Radio Frequency Identification
abstract: This article presents a millimeter-wave (mm-wave) K-band and D-band large-frequency-ratio reflectarray for Internet of Things (IoT) devices, fabricated using industrial-level multi-material additive manufacturing. The reflectarray's unit cell (UC) employs a metallic resonant patch embedded in the printed dielectric material. By adjusting the vertical position of the patch, different electromagnetic wave reflection paths are obtained. This provides an additional degree of freedom for phase shifting. Specifically, phase shifting in the K‑band relies entirely on the different lengths of reflection paths. In contrast, the D-band phase-shifting depends on metallic resonant patches with varied sizes and the different lengths of reflection paths. This unique configuration effectively avoids the coupling issues that challenge conventional dual-band reflectarrays. Besides, the period of the UC can be sufficiently small at K-band (only 0.1 free-space wavelengths) to provide good phase correction and a large beam tilting angle. The reflectarray with multiple metal layers is implemented in a single substrate with an ultrathin profile, using the advanced multi-material industrial-level additive manufacturing technique. The reflectarray antenna can be potentially used in base stations for K-band satellite communications (SatComs) and mm-wave point-to-point beyond 5G communications simultaneously.
