title: Efficient Calculation of the Partial Elements for the Orthogonal PEEC Method With the Integer-Varying Nonuniform Meshes
authors: Xiao Chen, Gang Zhang, Francesco de Paulis, Alistair Duffy, Dazhi Yang, Jinghao Su
publish: IEEE Transactions on Electromagnetic Compatibility
abstract: A nonuniform orthogonal mesh generation scheme, namely, the integer-varying meshes, is proposed to model realistic conductor systems with the partial element equivalent circuit (PEEC) method in this article. The integer-varying meshes resolve the weak geometry description ability and unrealistic of uniform meshes, thus yielding frequency-independent circuits for skin-effect modelling. In comparison to generic nonuniform meshes, the calculation of the partial elements can be significantly accelerated by the integer-varying meshes in coordination with the partial inductance approximation scheme and tabulation. The accuracy of the partial inductance approximation scheme is validated for partial inductance units with a wide range of aspect ratios and relative distance. In addition, to deal with the cells with extreme aspect ratios generated by the integer-varying meshes, stable model order-reduced formulas for partial elements are adopted; such proposed model, at the same time, is able to speed up the calculation, when the formula for the partial self potential coefficient is modified for stability at extreme aspect ratios. Through three numerical examples, including an L-shaped trace over a ground, a single loop coil and a 3-D spiral inductor, the efficiency of the integer-varying meshes with the partial inductance approximation and tabulation is verified by comparison to the case without approximation or tabulation, and the results demonstrate good consistency to the references.

title: Towards Edge Holography via Implicit Neural Representation and Compression
authors: Hyunmin Ban, Wenbin Zhou, Yifan Peng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: Holographic displays offer the promise of realistic 3D visualization for virtual and augmented wearable solutions. Nevertheless, existing computer-generated holography (CGH) methods often struggle with either a high computational burden or limited display realism. While the emerging cloud-edge computing mechanism can enable the real-time streaming of holograms, classic image compression techniques struggle to efficiently encode and decode the substantial high-frequency information inherent in hologram data. In light of these challenges, we present a display-aware and lightweight CGH framework, leveraging implicit neural representations (INRs) and camera-calibrated wave propagation, to generate and compress high-fidelity phase-only holograms. Specifically, our approach interprets hologram generation as a continuous function approximation problem, enabling the network, with reduced parameters, to effectively learn the inherent periodicity and high-frequency components of 2D and 3D hologram data. To enable efficient deployment, we further incorporate quantization-aware training, followed by entropy coding. Experimental results evaluated on an unfiltered holographic display prototype demonstrate that the proposed INR-CGH retains image quality comparable to that of existing optimization-based methods in both 2D and 3D scenarios. In addition, our compact INR representation achieves up to 11× compression rate with minimal quality degradation and can be further reduced via quantization-aware training. The resulting model enables ≥250 fps in decoding speed, paving the way towards edge holography.

title: Novel GP-Based Gate Voltage V GE Unsupervised Precise Monitoring Strategy for IGBT Module Over High-Speed Switching Transients
authors: Xiaotian Zhang, Weiye Wang, Yuan Liu, Jingwei Zhang, Yihua Hu, Jose Rodriguez
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Electronics
abstract: This article proposes a novel Gaussian process (GP)-based gate voltage V GE unsupervised precise monitoring strategy for insulate-gate bipolar transistor (IGBT) modules in high-speed switching transients. First, a GP with constrained variational inference model is proposed to reconstruct low sample rate V GE back to extreme high sample rate in real time without pretraining. Second, a V GE-specific physics-guided hybrid kernel function is designed to accurately capture the features of V GE waveforms. Unlike traditional compressed-sensing-based monitoring method, the proposed method is not only applicable to signals with significant sparsity but also more suitable for V GE, and it avoids the severe distortion of the V GE during sudden switching changes. Finally, real experiments are conducted on an IGBT double pulse test platform under various operating conditions, including different bus voltages, junction temperatures, load currents, and gate resistances, which validate the robustness and superiority of this technology. The method applicability and impact of multiple potential factors such as loop stray inductance are also analyzed.

title: Modified Local Binary Pattern-Based Brain Tumor Detection for Resource-Constrained IoT and Industrial Electronics Platforms
authors: Suman Rekha Dip, Hemant Kumar Meena
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: Detection and categorization of brain tumors (BT) using Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI) are essential yet formidable problems in clinical diagnosis. Timely and precise diagnosis of BTs can markedly enhance patient outcomes via prompt intervention and tailored treatment approaches. While deep learning methods achieve high accuracy in tumor localization, their dependence on large annotated datasets and computationally heavy models with millions of parameters limits their applicability in resource-constrained environments, such as IoT-enabled healthcare devices and edge computing platforms in industrial electronics. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a cost-effective and efficient machine learning model employing a Modified Local Binary Pattern (MLBP) feature extraction technique. MLBP captures both local texture and global connectivity patterns in MRI brain images, providing rich spatial information for distinguishing tumor from non-tumor regions, even at reduced resolutions. This approach minimizes computational complexity while retaining strong discriminatory capability. The extracted MLBP features were evaluated with multiple ML classifiers, where Extreme Gradient Boosting (XGB) achieved the highest accuracy of 93.50%. Remarkably, the XGB model used only 0.0139 million parameters with a compact size of about 0.99 MB, making it highly suitable for IoT devices and embedded medical systems requiring real-time, low-power, on-device diagnostics. The proposed lightweight yet accurate framework supports practical deployment in smart healthcare ecosystems, enabling IoT sensors and edge devices to provide clinicians with rapid, reliable diagnostic assistance, thereby enhancing patient care and operational efficiency in both medical and industrial electronics applications.

title: Training Design for Generalized Spatial Modulation Systems Based on Sparse Zero-Correlation Zone Arrays
authors: Cheng-Yu Pai, Hen-Geul Yeh, Chao-Yu Chen
publish: 2025 11th International Workshop on Signal Design and Its Applications in Communications (IWSDA)
abstract: The generalized spatial modulation (GSM) offers a flexible trade-off between spectral efficiency and hardware complexity by encoding additional information in the indices of active transmit antennas, without increasing the number of required radio frequency (RF) chains. This paper proposes a novel construction of two-dimensional (2-D) sparse zero-correlation zone (SZCZ) arrays for efficient training matrix design in GSM systems. An SZCZ array is specifically characterized by a high degree of sparsity and exhibits zero periodic auto-and cross- correlation zone properties between any two row sequences. In contrast to existing kernel-based training schemes, the proposed SZCZ arrays are constructed using 2-D restricted generalized Boolean functions (RGBFs) and hence can be readily applied as training matrices in GSM systems. Numerical results validate that the presented SZCZ-based training design provides robust channel estimation performance over frequency-selective fading channels.

title: Land Cover Change Detection Using DeepLabv3ResNet50
authors: Prathamesh Nirnay Chaudhari, Rajakumar K, Shivam Sultaniya, Sameer Satyajit Palkar, Sai Bhargav Kasetty
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: Land cover changes are important to understand environmental dynamics, monitor urban expansion, and manage natural resources effectively. High-resolution remote sensing imagery, coupled with recent developments in deep learning, has significantly improved the accuracy of automatic land cover classification and change detection. This work describes an improved semantic segmentation framework using the DeepLabv3 architecture and ResNet50 backbone for accurate land cover change detection from the DeepGlobe Land Cover dataset. The proposed architecture modifies the ASPP module to capture multi-scale contextual information while preserving fine spatial details across varied heterogeneous terrains. The main aim is to develop a robust and computationally efficient segmentation model that shall be able to handle challenges such as spectral similarity between classes and complex landscape variations. Experimental evaluations reveal that the proposed DeepLabv3-ResNet50 outperforms conventional architectures, which include FCN-8s, U-Net, and SegNet. It achieved higher mIoU, F1-score, pixel accuracy, and faster inference time, hence generalizing better for diverse land cover classes. From these results, the potential of this model for scalable, real-time land cover monitoring and long-term environmental change analysis can be realized.

title: TEAL: Efficient and Effective Anti-Money Laundering based on Dynamic Spatio-Temporal Feature Modeling and LLMs
authors: Xiaolei Yin, Zihan Wang, Xing’E Yan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Cyber Resilience and Endogenous Safety &amp; Security (CRESS)
abstract: With the widespread use of blockchain technology, money laundering activities involving cryptocurrencies have become increasingly prevalent. These activities exhibit highly dynamic patterns characterized by their large scale and sophisticated organization. Modeling transaction networks as dynamic graph networks has become a critical research direction in Anti-Money Laundering. However, existing methods face two major challenges in practical applications. First, iterative message passing on transaction graphs comprising billions of nodes results in low computational efficiency. Second, it is challenging to capture long-term temporal dependencies and hidden patterns in transaction behavior, which negatively impact detection accuracy. To address these issues, this paper proposes TEAL, an efficient and effective AML method based on dynamic spatial-temporal feature modeling and Large Language Models. TEAL introduces a training free higher-order propagation mechanism for spatial feature modeling, enabling a lightweight representation of complex multi-hop interactions between accounts. For the temporal dimension, it leverages Transformer architectures to extract sequential dependencies in transaction evolution. Concurrently, it integrates LLMs to perform risk classification and interpretation based on spatiotemporal holistic representations of nodes. Evaluation results on the UpbitHack dataset, constructed from real blockchain transaction data, demonstrate that TEAL outperforms existing models in both detection accuracy and computational efficiency.

title: Adaptive Machine Learning Framework for Comprehensive Disease Risk Forecasting From Electronic Health Record
authors: Premkumar Duraisamy, Abijith P Suthi, Ben Richards R, Niranjani V, J. Prasad, Karthik S
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: This work investigates the application of machine learning algorithms for predicting the risk of multiple chronic diseases using Electronic Health Records (EHRs). Structured clinical datasets incorporating demographics, laboratory results, medical history, and lifestyle factors were utilized to evaluate six algorithms: Logistic Regression, Support Vector Classifier (SVC), K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN), Decision Tree, Random Forest, and Naïve Bayes. The models were assessed based on their accuracy, precision, and robustness in identifying disease risk patterns. Among them, the Random Forest algorithm achieved the highest accuracy of 94.4%, effectively capturing complex non-linear relationships across diverse patient profiles. Logistic Regression and SVC produced interpretable and stable outcomes, while KNN and Decision Tree demonstrated adaptability to heterogeneous data distributions. Despite its simplicity, Naïve Bayes achieved competitive performance in handling high-dimensional categorical variables. These findings highlight the potential of machine learning in enabling data-driven early disease risk prediction and advancing personalized healthcare. Future directions include integrating heterogeneous EHR data sources, leveraging deep learning techniques, and developing clinically interpretable decision support systems to enhance preventive care and improve patient outcomes.

title: Pain-Sense: A Real-Time Pain Detection and Alert System for Non-Verbal Individuals
authors: Rathieswaran K S, S Jalaja, Rithik T S, Steewough Sebastian
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: The evaluation of pain non-verbally is still one of the most important issues in the healthcare facilities, including intensive care units, geriatric wards, and post-operative recovery units. Conventional methods of observation usually depend on the subjective basis of the aberrant behavior or changes in the vital signs that lead to the delayed recognition of the abnormality and lack of consistency in the assessment. The restrictions may cause uncontrolled pain, poor recovery and more medical complications. PainSense is filling the void by deploying an intelligent and real-time real-time pain platform that works with those people who cannot communicate the presence of discomfort. The system combines the multi-sensor inputs, such as the electromyography of muscular activity, galvanic skin response of detecting stress, optical heart-rate monitoring of cardiovascular changes, temperature monitoring, and inertial sensing of agitation and tremor. Captured physiological measurements are given to a continuous preprocessing, feature extraction and classification by a supervised machine-learning model, optimized to run on an embedded platform. The system further sends real-time notifications by a web-based dashboard and offers visual representation of full physiological trends as clinical review. PainSense reduces subjective evaluation and improves clinical decision-making, utilizing portability, low cost and automated analysis. This method enhances timeliness of interventions, decreases the work of caregivers, and enhances patient safety.

title: Proactive Cyber Supply Chain Security Using Machine Learning and Behavioral Analytics
authors: Patan Feridoz Khan, Miriyala ManiTeja, Nusum Mounika, Nuthalapati Himapriya, Nune Raja, P Bhaskar
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: The global supply chain's increasing complexity has therefore presented a broader attack surface for cyber-attacks and is providing an impetus for advanced analytics in proactive threat detection and response. This paper presents an integrated application that incorporates supervised and unsupervised learning processes into comprehensive cybersecurity monitoring. The application employs the Random Forest for supervised anomaly detection based on labeled data, identifying known attack types, while the Isolation Forest models serve unsupervised detection of insider threats by recognizing deviations in behavior. The proposed framework embeds real-time analytics on user behavior, anomaly detection, and classification of network threats. In addition to the aforementioned data preprocessing methods, this research also utilized feature scaling, encoding, and noise injection. The visualization element can support interpretable visualizations, specifically bar charts, pie charts, and donut visualizations to represent proportions of anomalies and distributions of occurrences. The experimental evaluation has shown the ability of the framework to classify normal activities and anomalies while being adaptive and agile towards the detection of new threats. This approach contributes to enhance cyber resilience within the supply chain ecosystem by detecting, classifying, and visualizing possible risks, enabling organizations to take quick action to mitigate and remediate any vulnerabilities they encounter well before exploiting these vulnerabilities.

title: Fuzzy-Wiener Graph Framework for Heart Disease Classification
authors: Khin Khin Lay, Nay Lynn, Aung Zaw Myint
publish: 2026 ElCon Conference of Young Researchers in Computing &amp; Processing, and Information Security (ElCon-CP)
abstract: This study introduces a hybrid graph-theoretic framework for heart disease prediction that integrates the Fuzzy Connectivity Index (FCI) and Wiener Connectivity Index (WCI) to enhance classification accuracy and interpretability. The proposed model begins with data preprocessing using feature standardization and one-hot encoding to handle mixed data types. A Logistic Regression classifier generates baseline membership probabilities, representing each patient’s likelihood of having heart disease. To capture inter-patient relationships, a Gaussian-based fuzzy affinity graph is constructed, modeling the similarity between patients as weighted connections. The FCI evaluates the maximum connectivity strength between patients and seed nodes through a max–min path principle, while the WCI quantifies the global compactness and efficiency of the fuzzy network. These indices are fused with probabilistic membership scores to generate refined predictions. Experimental results demonstrate that the FCI–WCI hybrid approach effectively identifies key relational patterns among patients and improves diagnostic reliability compared to traditional classifiers.

title: Quantum-Inspired FPGA Design of an Energy–Efficient Hamming (7,4) Encoder–Decoder using Hybrid Peres–Toffoli and Double-Feynman Gates for Fault-Tolerant Communication
authors: Ponamala Jishitha, Vemugadda Sankarnath, S Rambabu, Kandula Madhavi, Bikkili Alekya Himabindu, Shaik Mohammad Eliyas
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: Effective and low-power error-control coding techniques are necessary for reliable data transmission in faulttolerant communication systems. In this research, a unique hybrid of Peres-Toffoli and Double-Feynman reversible logic gates is used to create an energy-efficient, quantum-inspired Hamming <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(7,4)$</tex> encoder-decoder design. The suggested design is implemented on an Artix-7 FPGA after being designed in Verilog HDL and synthesized with the Xilinx Vivado Design Suite. Performance and resource usage are significantly improved when compared to traditional hybrid reversible architectures like Toffoli, Peres, Double Feynman, and HNG gates. The suggested encoder reduces static and dynamic powers by 6.2 % and 34.4 % respectively and achieves a total power consumption of 2.376 W, which is 67.8 % less than the highest conventional design. By using just two LUTs, hardware complexity is reduced, resulting in a nearly 60 % reduction in logic usage. Comparably, the decoder uses only four LUTs and almost half the hardware resources of earlier systems, exhibiting a 23.7 % total power reduction and a 13.7 % dynamic power reduction. Overall, the hybrid Peres-Toffoli and DoubleFeynman architecture ensures precise and quick error correction by offering an optimal area-power trade-off, fewer garbage outputs, and minimal logic depth. These findings prove that the suggested design is an extremely area-optimised and power-efficient solution appropriate for low-power VLSI systems for developing quantum computing platforms.

title: Structured Multimodal Visual Fusion Via Implicit Representation for High-Fidelity Motion Capture
authors: Huan Huang
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: With the increasing demand for high-precision motion capture and real-time signal estimation in fields such as sports science and virtual reality, related theories are constantly being designed. Traditional single-modal methods have obvious deficiencies in accuracy and real-time performance in complex motion scenes. Therefore, this study proposes a multimodal visual fusion model based on neural implicit representation. This model can achieve high-fidelity motion capture and real-time signal estimation. Specific innovations include Constructing a multimodal fusion framework based on error energy minimization. The framework integrates images, point clouds, depth and inertial sensor signals; Designing feature-level weighted fusion and neighboring point correction strategies. This strategy achieves consistency and precise mapping of cross-modal data; Using neural implicit representation to model the geometric, color and temporal characteristics of motion; Introducing multimodal attention mechanism and reinforcement learning feedback. Experiments were conducted on the self-built ZISU-Mocap dataset (which includes 12 subjects, 4 action categories, and approximately <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1. 4 4}$</tex> million frames), as well as external datasets including Human3.6M and CMU MoCap. Experimental results show that compared with the traditional multimodal method LateFusion, this method reduces the average MPJPE error by approximately <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0. 7 ~ m m}$</tex>. Simultaneously, PCK is improved by approximately 6.5 percentage points, and PSNR is significantly improved. In terms of system efficiency, the average system latency is only 28 ms. In a comparison of action categories, this method performs stably under highly dynamic movements such as jumping, and is effective in recovering and estimating subtle arm movements. In noise robustness testing, even when moderate noise <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\sigma=0.05)$</tex> is added to the IMU data, the MPJPE only increases by approximately <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3-4 ~\text{mm}$</tex>. This result still enables stable recovery of large-scale movements. The average latency for real-time estimation of sequential motions is <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3 8 ~ m s}$</tex>, and the joint prediction error is stable within 2°.

title: Data-Driven Product Road-Mapping Using Feature Adoption Analytics in Personal Finance Management
authors: Gayathri Ganesh, Mukhila Rajamani, S. Pavithra, Deepshika Anandaraman
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: Product teams in the fast-evolving Personal Finance Management (PFM) field must confront the dual challenges of increasing user expectations and complex feature sets. To address these two challenges, we have developed a data-driven product road-mapping framework to evaluate and prioritize these features based on user-centric realities and user feature adoption behaviors (with user behaviour analytics). This process involves activity-tracking, clustering user behavior, scoring an adopted feature — and it incorporates causal analysis and predictive modelling in evaluating and quantifying the business impact specific features have on retention, completing financial goals, and upselling opportunities. We also simulated the dashboard with synthetic PFM usage data, and it was demonstrated to improved forecasting accuracy of 34%, reduced data latency 91.6%, provided 100% improved visibility into features adopted, and frequency of updates, and improved engagement insights 60% better than earlier feature tracking methods (where circular and static reporting were used). This research is novel in its consideration of product strategy and contribution to behavioral analytics in developing and presenting a scalable evidence-based framework to help FinTech teams evaluate user engagement, prioritize features, and meet financial wellness goals.

title: Distributed Federated Temporal Behavior Monitoring for Adaptive Cybersecurity Detection in IIoT Environments
authors: R Banu Priya, Mouleeswaran S K
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: Industrial internet of thing (IIoT) systems are intended to have large numbers of connected devices that on an ongoing basis share operational data to enable automation, monitoring, and intelligent control. It is believed that protection of these systems must be reliable, because a cyber-attack against IIoT devices can interrupt production, destroy assets and threaten personnel security for smart manufacturing plants where synchronous intrusion leads that the whole-body conveyor belt stops. This research study presents a deep distributed reinforcement learning framework boosted with metaheuristicguided gated recurrent units for the cybersecurity detection in IIoT networks titled federated deep reinforcement learning with metaheuristic-gated recurrent units. The model represents temporal attack behavior by recurrent gating operations, which are learned by global search-based optimization to achieve the fine-grained sensitivity towards the evolving cyber patterns. More specifically, we are considering that local nodes have an updated policy without receiving raw data, to guarantee privacypreserving operation while adjusting responses to adversary behavior. A federated coordination phase extracts model updates into a unified global security model, providing enhancement in resilience against long-lasting anomalous activities. Experiment results show that the proposed scheme achieves accuracy detection, quick response under dynamic environment, and low communication cost as well more importantly, it is very applicable to different IIoT system infections.

title: Survey of Language Level Memory Safety
authors: Xinming Guo, Guangliang Yang, Haoxiang Zhang, Can Li, Yi Wang, Min Yang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Cyber Resilience and Endogenous Safety &amp; Security (CRESS)
abstract: Memory safety is essential for programming low-level system software. Over the past decades, numerous language-level efforts, such as C dialects and Rust, have aimed to provide strong safety guarantees for programmers. In this paper, we systematically study the landscape of language-level memory safety and address two key questions: (1) What are the core design principles underlying language-level memory safety? (2) What are the primary challenges in achieving it? We taxonomize related work along two dimensions: syntactic and semantic enforcement mechanisms. Our analysis reveals that existing solutions remain imperfect, often facing inherent trade-offs between safety and performance. Guided by these findings, we outline promising future directions to advance memory safety.

title: Prototype Memory-based Neighboring Feature Fusion Network for Image Manipulation Localization
authors: Songlin Li, Zhiqing Guo, Changtao Miao, Wenzhong Yang, Liejun Wang, Gaobo Yang, Xin Liao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: Image manipulation localization (IML) aims to segment manipulated regions in suspicious images. However, most existing methods rely solely on intrinsic features extracted from the input image and passively model local or global inconsistencies, making it difficult to accurately delineate manipulated regions with ambiguous boundaries. To address these challenges, we propose a prototype memory-based neighboring feature fusion network (PNF-Net), which is inspired by a biological memory mechanism. PNF-Net simulates selective preference by learning manipulation-trace prototypes as memory priors, thereby guiding representation learning toward consistent and discriminative manipulation cues. Specifically, we propose a memory-guided localization module (MLM) that models the consistencies and anomalies between manipulated regions and the background as memory priors, enabling precise localization. We then propose a neighboring feature interaction module (NFIM) that preserves fine-grained details from neighboring shallow features, enhances global semantics from neighboring deep features, and effectively fuses them. Finally, a verification fusion module (VFM) is designed to enrich contextual semantics and improve the completeness and accuracy of localization results. Extensive experiments on multiple benchmark datasets show that our PNF-Net outperforms most state-of-the-art IML models. Our code is available on https://github.com/vpsg-research/PNF-Net.

title: Tunable Domain Adaptation Using Unfolding_supp1-3679328.pdf
authors: Satish Mulleti
publish: N/A
abstract: Machine learning models often struggle to generalize across domains with varying data distributions, such as differing noise levels, leading to degraded performance. Traditional strategies like personalized training, which trains separate models per domain, and joint training, which uses a single model for all domains, have significant limitations in flexibility and effectiveness. To address this, we propose two novel domain adaptation methods for regression tasks based on interpretable unrolled networks—deep architectures inspired by iterative optimization algorithms. These models leverage the functional dependence of select tunable parameters on domain variables, enabling controlled adaptation during inference. Our methods include Parametric Tunable-Domain Adaptation (P-TDA), which uses known domain parameters for dynamic tuning, and Data-Driven Tunable-Domain Adaptation (DD-TDA), which infers domain adaptation directly from input data. We validate our approach on compressed sensing problems involving noise-adaptive sparse signal recovery, domain-adaptive gain calibration, and domain-adaptive phase retrieval, demonstrating improved or comparable performance to domain-specific models while surpassing joint training baselines. This work highlights the potential of unrolled networks for effective, interpretable domain adaptation in regression settings.

title: Investigation of Permanent Magnet Electrodynamic Suspension: Performance Enhancement, Cost Efficiency, and Pole Pitch Optimization
authors: Hao Lu, Baoquan Kou, Changchuang Huang, Yuexuan Lou, Haoquan Zhang, Xu Niu, He Zhang, Lu Zhang
publish: IEEE/ASME Transactions on Mechatronics
abstract: The electrodynamic suspension (EDS) is one of the representatives of the maglev system, which is expected to realize high-speed transportation. In this article, an electromagnetic model of a Halbach permanent magnet array (HPMA) and null-flux-coil (NFC) is established based on a permanent magnet EDS (PMEDS) system. First, the electromagnetic analysis model of the PMEDS system is established by the Biot–Savart Law and the dynamic loop theorem. Subsequently, the comprehensive sensitivity analysis of the electromagnetic parameters of the PMEDS is carried out, and the influence factors of different variables are obtained. The optimization objectives are determined by the levitation drag ratio, levitation mass ratio, average levitation force, and levitation force ripple. Furthermore, the optimization is carried out based on NSGA-II. Then, finite element analysis is used to verify the effectiveness of the optimization results. Next, the rationality of the optimization method is verified by a static experiment. Moreover, the reliability of the optimization results is confirmed through dynamic performance analysis. Finally, the optimal pole pitch ratio of HPMA-NFC is proposed to achieve a lower electromagnetic force ripple. Results show that the optimized parameters enhance the performance of PMEDS within the controllable cost range and provide guidance for engineering applications.

title: Interactive CAD Reverse Modeling via Variational Curve Approximation
authors: Zhenyu Zhang, Pu Li, Yunfeng Ai
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: Reconstructing editable CAD models from mesh data remains a fundamental yet challenging problem in reverse engineering. Existing methods often struggle to achieve both geometric accuracy and topological consistency, especially when recovering analytic design intent from complex meshes. We present an interactive CAD reverse modeling framework based on variational curve approximation, which bridges mesh segmentation and parametric reconstruction in a unified manner. Our method extracts surface-aligned cutting curves from segmented regions and refines them through a variational approximation process that encodes primitive attributes and geometric constraints. This enables the automatic recovery of closed loops, constraint relations, and operation parameters, leading to a compact and topologically consistent CAD representation. In addition, a coplanar profile detection and voxel-similarity-based Boolean inference are developed to restore the topological order of the original modeling sequence. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our approach achieves higher geometric fidelity, better structural consistency, and improved automation compared with stateof- the-art techniques. The reconstructed models are watertight, topologically consistent, and readily applicable to downstream CAD design tasks.

title: A Dynamic Lifecycle-Aware Disaster Recovery Framework for Stateful IoT Applications in Edge Environments
authors: Vitumbiko Mafeni, Phuong Bac Ta, Younghan Kim
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Internet of Things (IoT) systems are increasingly deployed across remote and distributed environments, enabling edge computing capabilities that span multiple lifecycle stages such as deployment, configuration, runtime, failure recovery, and updates. These systems are often decomposed for scalability and adaptability but require integrated coordination across infrastructure, applications, and devices. In disaster recovery scenarios, maintaining stateful continuity across these layers is essential yet remains challenging. Existing migration techniques often incur high overhead, focus narrowly on runtime preservation without lifecycle context, disrupt system modularity, and depend on manual or one-time recovery actions. This paper presents a systematic, GitOps-driven recovery framework for stateful IoT systems, unifying infrastructure, services, and device management under a declarative lifecycle model. The framework automates stateful migration through lifecycle-aware orchestration that preserves configuration versions, topology structure, and orchestration semantics while abstracting infrastructure heterogeneity. Furthermore, it introduces device-binding- aware recovery, treating physical connectivity of device-to-system as first-class declarative resources that can be consistently recovered. We implement and evaluate the framework across diverse IoT workloads and edge platforms, demonstrating significant improvements in recovery time, state consistency, and management overhead compared to conventional migration and failover solutions.

title:
Multilevel, High Resistance Switching in Al
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    O
                    <sub>3</sub>
                    /TiO
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    Bilayer RRAM Devices
authors: Daniel Timbie, Hyeonseon Choi, Simone Bianconi, Stephen Cheng, Leo Ling, Hooman Mohseni
publish: IEEE Transactions on Electron Devices
abstract: Multilevel, high-resistance resistive random access memory (RRAM) devices are desirable for neuromorphic computing applications because of their ability to represent synaptic weights and support energy-efficient computing. High resistance operation is also beneficial for scalability as it reduces parasitic resistances from interconnects in crossbar arrays, which can result in inference errors that increase with array size. Here, we present Al<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>O<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub>/TiO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> bilayer RRAM devices with ultrahigh resistance values (up to 2 G<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\Omega $</tex-math> </inline-formula>) and multilevel programmability, demonstrating the writing of eight states with a standard deviation of separation and a dynamic range spanning three orders of magnitude. These capabilities, combined with long retention times, highlight their potential for implementation in artificial neural networks. To gain insight into their performance, we examine their switching behavior, which exhibits traits characteristic of both filamentary and filament-free metal-oxide RRAM. Initial voltage application induces changes in current that span multiple orders of magnitude, indicating the formation of conductive filaments composed of oxygen vacancies, typical of filamentary RRAM. In contrast, low-frequency noise analysis suggests that switching is governed by the collective movement of oxygen vacancies rather than the closing and rupture of individual filaments. We infer that the conduction in our devices is governed by filaments with a broad spatial distribution of gap distances between filament ends and the electrode, enabling reliable switching of states within an exceptionally high resistance regime.

title: Integrated Network Vulnerability Assessment Using r -Power Entropy and Performance Metrics
authors: Claudio M. Rocco, Kash Barker, Jose E. Ramirez-Marquez, Jose Moronta
publish: IEEE Transactions on Reliability
abstract: In this paper, we propose an integrated approach for network vulnerability and resilience assessment, combining graph theoretical methods with performance-based measures. Using r-power normalized entropy, which quantifies the complexity and randomness of the network's structure by raising the adjacency matrix to the power of r, we analyze both the topological and weighted properties of the network. By simulating link failures and recalculating network performance (e.g., flows and load served), we assess the impact of disruptions, thereby quantifying the changes of entropy before and after the events, benchmarking the results against classic static metrics for links (e.g., betweenness centrality, closeness centrality). We synthesize these findings to identify critical links essential for maintaining network structure and performance. The analysis highlights key trade-offs between structural entropy and real-world performance metrics, demonstrating that static indices have low correlation with operational loss, while the dynamic change in entropy successfully identifies latent, severe failures, providing a framework for network optimization and design. This methodology is applicable to networks where both structural integrity and resilience are vital (e.g., power grids, communication, transportation). We use two real-world electric power system assessments to illustrate the value of the proposed methodology in gaining insights into the performance and vulnerability of power networks, even when only unweighted adjacency matrices are available.

title: SERAH-CIM: Sparsity-Aware Effective Row Activation Analog-Digital Hybrid eDRAM CIM With In-Macro Multi-Row-Multi-Task Control
authors: Hoichang Jeong, Seungbin Kim, Dongwook Kim, Jueun Jung, Kyuho Jason Lee
publish: IEEE Journal of Solid-State Circuits
abstract: This article presents a sparsity-aware analog-digital hybrid embedded dynamic random access memory (eDRAM) computing-in-memory (CIM) processor for highly energy-efficient deep neural network (DNN) acceleration. Although CIM architectures execute multiplication-and-accumulation (MAC) more efficiently than von Neumann architectures, their practical energy efficiency for DNN acceleration remains limited due to several challenges. First, prior CIMs struggled to exploit massive sparsity because of their highly parallelized structures. Second, CIMs typically adopt computation in either the analog or the digital domain, facing fundamental trade-offs between analog-to-digital converter (ADC) overhead and throughput. Third, system throughput is degraded due to 1) workload imbalance among CIM macros during sparsity-aware computation caused by a random sparsity pattern, which ruins CIM macro utilization and 2)frequent refresh and weight-update operations that have plagued prior eDRAM CIMs. To address these challenges, the proposed eDRAM CIM processor introduces four key features: 1) input activation (IA) grouping convolution, which completely skips zero-weight computations by activating only the effective rows of the CIM macro, increasing the effective computation ratio by $4.59\times $ ; 2) a hybrid-CIM macro integrated with SAR-Flash ADC (SF-ADC) and reversed-MAC near-memory logic (RM-NML) for energy-efficient MAC operations in both the analog and the digital domains, improving macro efficiency by $2.39\times $ ; 3) sparsity-aware proactive scheduling (SPS) to maximize CIM macro utilization, reducing system latency by 10.4%; and 4)in-macro Multi-row-Multi-task (MRMT) control that enables concurrent refresh/update during in-memory computation, resulting in a 22.0% reduction in system latency and a $1.3\times $ increase in system energy efficiency. Fabricated in a 28 nm CMOS process, the proposed processor demonstrates high-energy efficiency across various benchmarks, outperforming previous CIM processors by $1.55\times $ and $10.37\times $ for ResNet-18 and VGGNet-16, respectively.

title: GSReuse: Temporally Adaptive Screen-Space Reuse for Accelerating 3D Gaussian Splatting
authors: Chengzhi Tao, Yiyang Sun, Jie Guo, Tao Zhang, Letian Huang, Junqiu Zhu, Daoheng Wang, Yanwen Guo
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: Recent advances in 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) have enabled real-time, high-fidelity novel view synthesis. However, rendering each frame independently in a video sequence leads to redundant computations, especially when adjacent frames share significant visual overlaps. This inefficiency is particularly problematic in VR applications, where high frame rates and stereoscopic rendering amplify the per-frame cost. Existing frame interpolation or reuse strategies typically rely on image-domain information and are thus not directly applicable to 3DGS rendering, which is fundamentally point-based. To address this runtime inefficiency, we propose GSReuse, a lightweight and drop-in accelerator that speeds up 3DGS rendering by reusing computations across consecutive frames. GSReuse operates in screen space and introduces only minimal modifications to existing 3DGS rendering pipelines. It also eliminates the need for retraining scene representations. Given the rendered image, depth map, and camera parameters of the current frame, GSReuse estimates reliable Gaussian splatting motion vectors for all pixels and warps reusable contents to the new view. A tile-based filtering and masking strategy is then applied to determine which regions can be safely reused, allowing the 3DGS renderer to skip redundant rendering operations. We evaluate GSReuse on multiple benchmark datasets, showing that GSReuse significantly improves rendering, while maintaining high visual fidelity. Compared to state-of-the-art video frame reuse/generation methods, GSReuse delivers better image quality with much lower latency, facilitating practical deployment of 3DGS in VR applications.

title: Transformer-Based Missing Data Imputation in Time Series With Application to Flight Test Data
authors: Jicheng Li, Lishuai Li, Yining Dong, Haoran Xie, S. Joe Qin
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Electronics
abstract: Accurate imputation of missing data is essential yet challenging in complex engineering systems testing and scientific experiments, as the data are often high-dimensional, nonlinear, dynamic, and difficult or expensive to reproduce. Recent advances in Transformer architectures have demonstrated strong capabilities for modeling long-term dependencies in time-series data, offering new opportunities for accurate dynamic data imputation. The recent success of Transformer models in large language models (LLMs) opens new opportunities for missing data imputation in complex dynamic systems. In this article, a Transformer-based missing-data imputation technique (TransMIT) is proposed to tackle the challenges of dynamic systems testing data. First, we introduce a hybrid imputation strategy that combines reconstruction-based and prediction-based approaches, allowing the model to leverage both temporal dependencies and cross-correlations at each time step. Second, we design a combined attention mechanism to jointly model temporal and feature dependencies by incorporating transposed input representations to capture complex patterns along both the temporal and feature dimensions. The effectiveness of TransMIT is verified on a real-world flight test dataset, which is expensive to collect, as well as on an industrial boiler process dataset. Experimental results are presented to demonstrate that TransMIT achieves the lowest imputation error, with a scaled RMSE of 0.2.

title: FLOWSAFE- A Hybrid Feature-Localization Transformer Model for Flood Probability Prediction
authors: G. Vasumathi, R. Vani
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: In disaster risk management, flood prediction task is considered as a critical component. For resilience planning and early warning at the time of flood occurrence, predicting it based on environmental, infrastructural, and socio-economic data can be of great help. In this paper, one such concept called FLOWSAFE - a novel feature-localization and weighted self-attention model for flood probability prediction from multivariate environmental and socio-economic data is proposed. It consists of three functional modules namely: Feature Action Encoder (FAE) for capturing hierarchical and multiplicative interactions among numeric predictions. Next, a Cross-Variable Self-Attention Transformer (XVSAT) is used for modelling long-range dependencies across attributes treated as tokens. Finally, the Interpretable Fusion Head (IFH) block produces the flood probability estimate while providing attention-based feature contributions for downstream decision-making. The framework is tested on a large flood prediction dataset with 21 flood-related variables (monsoon intensity, urbanization level, coastal vulnerability, siltation levels, and so on). Experimental results on FLOWSAFE show that it outperforms baseline models like ANN, LSTM, RNN, BPNN, CNN, and XGBoostthrough attainment of 0.96 in <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{2}$</tex> metric, 0.11 in RMSE, 0.95 in accuracy, and 0.08 in MAE. Overall, the developed FLOWSAFE model demonstrated improved predictive accuracy, interpretability, and generalization making it best for early flood warning tasks.

title: Prognostix-XAI: Interpretable Modeling of Mechanical Degradation Dynamics
authors: N. Shree Makesh, M. Naveenkumar, J. Kavitha, AG. Amudieshwar, T. Chankyan
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: Modern industries, with their ever-increasing demands can predict machine downtimes to prevent expensive losses. Explainable AI (XAI)-driven Predictive Maintenance Framework using the AI4I 2020 dataset, comprising 10,000 industrial sensor records with 14 operational features. The task is modeled as both binary failure classification and multi-label failure mode prediction with the hybrid technique of interpretable models to uncover the faint traces of wear and stress evaluation. while LSTM captures temporal degradation patterns. SHAP-based explanations enhance interpretability by identifying key factors such as rotational speed, torque, and tool wear as dominant predictors of failure. The proposed hybrid model achieves an F1-score of 0.94 and accuracy of 98.4%, outperforming traditional methods by 13%. insights that establish a datadriven environment for decision-making towards sustainable production. This article stands as a testimony that explainable AI holds the gateway between predictive accuracy and human interpretability in the upcoming smart manufacturing systems.

title: New Sequence Design Metric for Amp Synchronization Field
authors: Shukai Wang, Zhengchun Zhou, Yang Yang, Xiaohu Tang
publish: 2025 11th International Workshop on Signal Design and Its Applications in Communications (IWSDA)
abstract: Binary sequences are of vital importance in the fields of communication and cryptography. This paper delves into the design of binary sequences for Ambient-powered (AMP) synchronization fields within the context of the Internet of Things (IoT). In our study, we first define the novel aperiodic autocorrelation functions tailored to the AMP scenario. A new metric for aperiodic correlation is proposed, which takes into consideration the data structure of Manchester encoding. By integrating the genetic algorithm, we efficiently search for some optimal binary sequences. Experimental results show that the sequence obtained by this new metric exhibit better performance compared to classical sequences.

title: Cross-Cloud Vulnerability Severity Prediction: A Comparative Machine Learning Analysis of AWS, Azure, and Google Cloud Platform Dataset
authors: Dossan Alisherov, Mohammed Al-Hubaishi
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: Cloud computing serves as the essential foundation for modern IT systems, driving organizations to adopt multiple cloud providers for improved flexibility and vendor independence. However, the transition to multi-cloud environments creates substantial security challenges, as organizations must manage diverse vulnerability landscapes across different platforms. This research develops a comprehensive machine learning framework for automated vulnerability detection and severity prediction across major cloud platforms, utilizing the Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) for AWS, Azure, and Google Cloud Platform (GCP). We developed an advanced feature extraction system that captures 26 distinct features from Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures (CVE) data, including CVSS metrics, temporal patterns, vulnerability categories, and textual indicators. Multiple machine learning models, including Random Forest, XGBoost, and Gradient Boosting, were evaluated and achieved exceptional results. The XGBoost model demonstrated outstanding performance with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{9 8. 5 3 \%}$</tex> accuracy and 0.9853 F1-score using a 60:40 data split. Our experimental results demonstrate that the proposed system significantly outperforms existing vulnerability detection approaches in handling imbalanced datasets and capturing complex feature interactions, providing a practical solution for real-time cloud security management.

title: Cost Optimization in Autonomous Data Pipeline: A Quantitative Approach
authors: Rikin Pravinbhai Patel, Nishant Kumar
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: This article is mostly informative to those datadriven organizations in which it is becoming more difficult and costly to process and run data pipelines. The overblown volume of data, different cloud service choices, and inconsistent pricing plans complicate the ability of organizations to manage the costs of operating without maintaining high-performance. This paper presents an easy but efficient cost optimization model comparison that uses mathematical modeling in order to balance effectively the cost, computing efficiency, and resource utilization. The framework also avoids fixed allocation strategies and assesses the workload behavior and automatically decides the distribution of the computing resources to ensure that the service quality is maintained within the established Service Level Agreement (SLA). The strategy saves unnecessary expenditure but enhances the overall cost, throughput, and reliability by adjusting the resources as per the system conditions. The findings indicate that resource-based datadriven calibration of resources have the potential to reduce costs and enhance performance significantly and assist organizations to develop scalable, sustainable, and cost-effective data infrastructures that meet the requirements of analytic and operational needs of these times.

title: Konnect - A College Exclusive Platform for Social-Academic Collaboration
authors: Benjamin Varkey, Betty Elza John, Christus Joy Puliyani, Criz Manoj, Anusree K
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: In today's academic environment, college students rely on fragmented platforms such as Instagram, LinkedIn, and WhatsApp to manage social interactions, academic collaboration, and professional networking. This lack of integration hampers effective communication and reduces engagement within the student community. To address this gap, we propose Konnect, a secure, college-exclusive networking platform designed specifically for students of concerned colleges. Konnect enables verified student profiles, project-based networking, real-time push notifications, and personalized digital portfolios within a single mobile application. Built using React Native and powered by Firebase for backend services and authentication, the platform ensures scalability, secure access, and crossplatform compatibility. Key features include dynamic feeds, event announcements, study groups, and a collaborative environment that fosters both academic and extracurricular growth. This paper outlines the system architecture, functional components, and anticipated outcomes, positioning Konnect as a next-generation solution for campus engagement and digital student presence.

title: Fortifying the SOC: A Validated LightGBM Engine for Behavioral Insider Threat Detection
authors: Vinny Surya Trinita, Aditya Kurniawan
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: Insider threats pose a critical challenge to Security Operations Centers (SOC), as conventional rule-based SIEM (Security Information and Event Management) often proves inadequate in detecting complex behavioral anomalies. This research proposes a framework to fortify the SOC by using a combined dataset from the CERT Insider Threat repositories (r4.2 and r5.2 versions) and applying robust User and Entity Behavior Analytics (UEBA) methodologies. User activities are aggregated into user-day profiles, and novel features capturing deviations from established behavioral baselines are engineered. The resulting high-dimensional and imbalanced dataset is effectively handled using the Synthetic Minority Over-sampling Technique (SMOTE), then trained and validated using the Light Gradient Boosting Machine (LightGBM) algorithm, which achieves outstanding detection performance. After threshold optimization, the result of evaluation on the test set reached an Accuracy of 0.99, a Precision of 0.91, a Recall of 0.95, a F1-Score of 0.93, and a ROC AUC of 0.99. Explainable AI (XAI) analysis using SHAP confirms that the behavioral deviation features are the strongest predictors. This framework provides a practical, validated detection engine, ideal for integration into modern SIEM platforms to strengthen SOC capabilities against dynamic, sophisticated insider threats.

title: Phytochemical Analysis of Tagetes Erecta by AI to Quality Control and Bioactivity Prediction
authors: Sonia Paliwal, Himanshu Joshi, Kanak Pandey
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: The African or Aztec marigold, Tagetes erecta L., is a useful medicinal plant and is characterized by significant compounds such as xanthophylls and polyphenols that are associated with eye benefits and anti-inflammatory outcomes. Conventional drug development mechanisms are a problem because of the high workload and devastating processes. In this paper, the authors examine AI, ML, and DL technologies to improve the pharmaceutics pipeline of T. erecta. The major developments are Non-Destructive Quality Control (NDQC) with Hyperspectral Imaging to perform quantification in real-time, fast drug discovery through DL algorithms that predict bioactivity, and optimization of extraction and formulation with the help of ML algorithms. Successful implementation faces hurdles regarding data quality and ethical governance, necessitating standardized datasets for compounds' chemical and biological properties.

title: A Progressive Fusion Architecture for Validating Distance Estimation in Aftermarket ADAS Installations
authors: Gangtao Han, Gang Shen, Song Wang, Gaofeng Pan, Changhao Du
publish: 2025 International Conference on Cyber Resilience and Endogenous Safety &amp; Security (CRESS)
abstract: The proliferation of retrofitted Advanced Driver Assistance Systems (ADAS) in the automotive aftermarket has introduced significant challenges regarding performance validation, particularly for distance estimation capabilities that underpin critical safety functions. Small-scale installation workshops typically lack standardized testing infrastructure, creating uncertainty about system reliability and hindering consumer confidence. This work presents a progressive fusion architecture that addresses these validation challenges through intelligent integration of existing sensor outputs. Our methodology employs a hierarchical multi-stage network design to synthesize observations from multiple ADAS sources, generating dependable distance estimates that serve as validation benchmarks for newly installed systems. The approach circumvents the need for expensive ground truth equipment by learning from curated datasets to produce reliable performance indicators. Validation experiments conducted on 24 carefully collected driving scenario recordings demonstrate the framework’s capability to achieve distance prediction with minimal deviation from actual measurements. Comparative analysis reveals that our fusion-based validation approach delivers superior assessment accuracy compared to conventional single-device reference methods, establishing its practical value for aftermarket ADAS quality assurance.

title: Bone Fracture Detection Using Deep Learning: A Review
authors: Anuj Mishra, Himanshu Verma, Richa Sharma, Anuj Singh
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: Bone fracture detection is important for clinical diagnostics and emergency medicine around the world. Manual interpretation of X-rays takes a lot of time, is prone to mistakes, and relies on the availability of experts. Deep Learning (DL) has become an effective solution, showing impressive results in visual recognition tasks. Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) and Transformer-based architectures, like Vision Transformers (ViT), have changed the game for automated feature extraction and image understanding. This review paper provides an overview of DL and Transformer applications in bone fracture detection using X-ray images. We examine recent advancements from 2018 to 2024, discussing datasets, preprocessing, model architectures, evaluation metrics, and hybrid frameworks that combine CNNs and Transformers. Comparative analyses show that hybrid models offer better contextual and spatial learning, outperforming traditional machine learning methods. Lastly, we address challenges such as data scarcity, interpretability, and computational demands. We also look at future directions that highlight explainable AI, multimodal learning, and real-time clinical applications.

title: “Ooru Alert”- A Crowdsourced Urban Issue Reporting System for Bengaluru
authors: R. J Anandhi, Sasikala T, Divyesh Raj Pillai, Padmaja Gadi, SV Aradya, Ashwini Prasathkannan
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: In order to solve municipal concerns in Bengaluru, this research investigates the development and impact of a crowdsourcing application for reporting urban issues. Residents may use the app to instantly report a variety of urban issues, such as poor infrastructure, traffic jams, water supply issues, and road upkeep. To enable targeted responses, these submissions are sent to the relevant civic authorities, including the Bangalore Metropolitan Transport Corporation (BMTC), Bengaluru Water Supply and Sewerage Board (BWSSB), Bruhat Bengaluru Mahanagara Palike (BBMP), and BESCOM. The application contributes to the overall improvement of urban government and public service delivery in Bengaluru by fostering better communication between residents and municipal entities, increasing the effectiveness of problem resolution, and promoting accountability and transparency. The application's capacity to promote civic engagement and cooperative approach to urban administration is also evaluated in the report.

title: Breast Cancer Prediction Enhanced by Robotic Automation: Integrating Histological Subtypes, Lymph Node Awareness, Evolutionary Optimized Neuro-Fuzzy Systems, and Dynamic Temporal Graph Modeling
authors: Karthik Kushala, Venkataramesh Induru, Yashwant Kumar Kolli, Vijai Anand Ramar, Priyadarshini Radhakrishnan, Mamta Arora
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: Breast cancer remains one of the largest health concerns in the modern world, characterized by high mortality and complex diagnosis. Since it is erratic and heterogeneous, it requires sophisticated and all-round approaches to precise detection and personalized treatment. The current study proposes an integrated predictive system based on histological subtype classification via convolutional neural networks (CNNs), lymph node metastasis prediction via logistic regression, and uncertainty management via evolutionary optimized neuro-fuzzy systems. Temporal cancer development dynamics are encoded using graph neural networks, and reproducible, optimal analysis is facilitated through robotic automation with minimal human interaction. Experimental outcomes indicate outstanding improvement with overall prediction accuracy of 95.4%, precision of 96.0%, robustness of 93.9%, and inference time of 8. 7 ms. Clinical interpretability also gets enhanced in the new solution with a score of 92.5% and has noisy input data resistance. With the help of high-performance AI methods and automation, the platform offers actionable insights that have the potential to increase diagnostic accuracy and clinical decision-making for breast cancer treatment.

title: Waveshield: Machine Learning-Enhanced Physical Layer Security Against Eavesdropping in Wireless Networks
authors: J. Surendiran, K. Malarkodi
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: Wireless communication by nature is exposed to eavesdropping because it has an open communicational medium thus traditional encryption cannot be applicable in dynamic environments. In this paper, WaveShield is proposed as a machine-learned physical layer security (PLS) platform, which can be used to secure wireless transmissions against unauthorized interception. WaveShield combines artificial noise injection, beamforming, and randomizing channel state to make the eavesdropper reception worse than, without affecting the legitimate users, maintaining a reliable communication. The framework uses machine learning models to provide prediction of channel variations, optimizing beamforming vectors, and allocating artificial noise dynamically which guarantees proactive defense, unlike the conventional PLS methods. Comprehensive tests of WaveShield on simulated and realworld wireless channel models show that WaveShield is highly effective in enhancing secrecy capacity, bit error rates, and prediction accuracy of dynamic wireless channel channels. Its superiority to conventional PLS approaches is exhibited by comparisons in maintaining secure communication in the face of mobility and low signal to noise ratio conditions. WaveShield offers a framework based on foundations and data, which spans physical layer methods to higher-level security mechanism. It integrates predictive intelligence with adaptive PLS strategies to create a powerful, proactive, and multifaceted system of ensuring that wireless networks in the next generation are secured against serious cases of eavesdropping.

title: Consideration of Superconducting DC Cables for Aircraft
authors: Hina Nitano, Masae Kanda, Sataro Yamaguchi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Applied Superconductivity
abstract: Modern aircraft need more electrical power than conventional ones, and their technology trend is called MEA. As the growing power demand has led to increased cable weight, lightweight cables are required. We are investigating superconducting DC cables for lightweight aircraft applications. Because aircraft applications require low weight and minimal stray magnetic field, we propose a stacked conductor configuration for the cable. The insulation test is a key issue in the stacked structure, and we performed the insulation voltage tests between the cable conductors. The result of the insulation test is sufficient from an experimental point of view. The stray magnetic field around the outer cryogenic pipe was measured to be approximately 0.2 mT at a current of 420 A. This low stray magnetic field is suitable for other aircraft instruments due to its minimal induction effects. Its low stray magnetic field depends on the stacked conductor structure, which is a multi-pole configuration. The estimated total weight of the superconducting DC cable, including the stacked conductor with insulation layers, cryogenic double pipe, the inner pipe supports, and liquid nitrogen, was less than one-seventh the weight of a conventional three-phase AC copper cable used in the present aircraft.

title: From Coarse to Continuous: Progressive Refinement Implicit Neural Representation for Motion-Robust Anisotropic MRI Reconstruction
authors: Zhenxuan Zhang, Lipei Zhang, Yanqi Cheng, Zi Wang, Fanwen Wang, Haosen Zhang, Yue Yang, Yinzhe Wu, Jiahao Huang, Angelica I Aviles-Rivero, Zhifan Gao, Guang Yang, Peter J. Lally
publish: IEEE Transactions on Image Processing
abstract: n motion-robust magnetic resonance imaging (MRI), slice-to-volume reconstruction is critical for recovering anatomically consistent 3D brain volumes from 2D slices, especially under accelerated acquisitions or patient motion. However, this task remains challenging due to hierarchical structural disruptions. It includes local detail loss from k-space undersampling, global structural aliasing caused by motion, and volumetric anisotropy. Therefore, we propose a progressive refinement implicit neural representation (PR-INR) framework. Our PR-INR unifies motion correction, structural refinement, and volumetric synthesis within a geometry-aware coordinate space. Specifically, a motion-aware diffusion module is first employed to generate coarse volumetric reconstructions that suppress motion artifacts and preserve global anatomical structures. Then, we introduce an implicit detail restoration module that performs residual refinement by aligning spatial coordinates with visual features. It corrects local structures and enhances boundary precision. Further, a voxel continuous-aware representation module represents the image as a continuous function over 3D coordinates. It enables accurate inter-slice completion and high-frequency detail recovery. We evaluate PR-INR on five public MRI datasets under various motion conditions (3% and 5% displacement), undersampling rates ( 4 x and 8 x ) and slice resolutions (scale = 5). Experimental results demonstrate that PR-INR outperforms state-of-the-art methods in both quantitative reconstruction metrics and visual quality. It further shows generalization and robustness across diverse unseen domains.
