title: EchoReason: a Two-stage Clinically Aligned Vision-Language Framework for Interpretable Diseases Diagnosis from Multi-Modal Ultrasound
authors: Ying Tan, Jiewen Yang, Xingguo Lv, Bocheng Liang, Kai Xu, Zhuangzhuang Chen, Haiyan Chen, Shengli Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Affective Computing
abstract: Ultrasound imaging is a cornerstone of disease screening, yet its clinical utility is often limited by time-intensive and operator-dependent interpretation, as well as variability in qualitative assessment. In particular, extracting consistent quantitative parameters from multi-view and multi-modal ultrasound sequences (e.g., echocardiography, vascular ultrasound) remains laborious, limiting diagnostic throughput and reproducibility. While deep learning offers automation potential, most existing approaches either ignore clinically established measurement assistant workflows or treat regression and classification as dis joint tasks, sacrificing interpretability and physiological grounding. To address this, we propose EchoReason, an end-to-end two stage vision-language framework that follows a measurement assisted diagnosis paradigm. EchoReason first estimates inter pretable cardiovascular parameters from heterogeneous ultra sound inputs using modality-aware expert–adaptor modules, and then performs disease diagnosis by integrating visual evidence with these measurements and explicit parameter–disease association guidance within a unified vision–language decoder. This design yields accurate predictions while providing auditable in termediate measurements that support evidence-based interpretation. Evaluated on multi-modal, multi-view ultrasound cohort, the model achieves state-of-the-art performance in both parameter estimation and multi-disease classification, demonstrating that clinically structured architectures can improve accuracy, robustness, and interpretability for ultrasound-based decision support.

title: Coordinated Interaction Between EV Charging Stations and Distribution Network via Stepwise Droop Control with SoC Interval Management
authors: Bo Wang, Pei Zhou, Cuo Zhang, Xingying Chen, Zhao Yang Dong
publish: IEEE Transactions on Sustainable Energy
abstract: The flexible charging capability of electric vehicle charging stations (EVCSs) can improve distribution network (DN) operation with source-load uncertainties. To fully harness this potential, this paper proposes a three-stage coordinated interaction method between EVCSs and the DN, centered on a novel stepwise droop control function. In the first stage, a dynamic state of charge (SoC) interval management approach is introduced to determine the optimal SoC interval and the corresponding adjustable power interval for EVCSs, considering charging uncertainties and temporal coupling of energy. The second stage is implemented from the DN perspective using grid-wide information to optimize P-V stepwise droop control functions for EVCSs. To alleviate the computational burden from binary variables, a general binary reduction technique is adopted. In the last stage, each EVCS decomposes the obtained stepwise droop control function into multiple levels of charging power for individual EVs, considering the minimal changes in charging power and the satisfaction of target SoC. Consequently, in real time, the EVCS can self-regulate the charging power based on local voltage measurements. The simulation results verify the effectiveness of the proposed interaction method. Compared with the baseline method, it reduces the DN's average voltage deviation and power loss by 13.29% and 14.68%, respectively.

title: Upskilling for Advanced Digitalization: A Scoping Review
authors: Tanya K Osborne, Hugo-Henrik Hachem, Dan Paulin, Ola Hultkrantz, Ulrika Lundqvist, Kent Thorén, Jan Gulliksen, Fredrick Heintz, Amy Loutfi, Francis Lee, Mattias Wiggberg
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: In a rapidly transforming technological landscape, higher education institutions struggle to meet the demands of industry in providing timely and appropriate upskilling and reskilling opportunities for the workforce. Under the umbrella of “advanced digitalisation”, a term prominent in European discourse, covering a wide range of new digital technologies within the Industry 4.0 and Industry 5.0 paradigms in manufacturing, ICT and engineering, we use scoping review methods to explore the unique challenges and interventions in this area, highlighting potentials for further research. Our scoping review examines 81 peer reviewed Scopus-indexed papers, outlining the current global research on the challenges and interventions in upskilling the workforce for advanced digitalisation. Our results show that research in this area is most prevalent in Europe, North America, and Asia and is most frequently done within the manufacturing and ICT sectors. Due to the fast pace of advanced digitalisation, close collaboration between education providers and industry is required to develop relevant and appropriate upskilling opportunities. We underline the importance of developing strategic management and change management knowledge alongside technology skills. We also highlight the importance of inter- and trans- disciplinary knowledge development. While determining skill needs has attracted substantial research focus, businesses may struggle with developing strategic upskilling plans despite being aware of their skills needs. We conclude that interventions that foster situated engagement and hands-on experience are vital for the future of upskilling.

title: Two-Level Beam Selection Algorithm for mmWave Wireless Power Transfer Systems: Training Overhead Reduction and Performance Analysis
authors: Seungsu Chung, Jaeyeon Ha, Jaehyun Park, Jae Cheol Park, Jungick Moon
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Millimeter-wave (mmWave) wireless power transfer (WPT) systems require efficient beam selection algorithms to maximize energy harvesting at wireless charging devices (WCDs). The conventional exhaustive codebook search method, while optimal, requires significant training overhead that reduces the time available for actual power transfer. This paper proposes a novel two-level beam selection algorithm that significantly reduces the training time slots while maintaining near-optimal energy harvesting performance. The proposed algorithm employs a hierarchical approach: first, it uses sum beams to identify the angle group containing the optimal beam direction, and then applies difference beams with decision statistics based on the difference-to-sum ratio (DSR) to determine the final beam selection within the identified group. We provide a comprehensive analytical framework using detection theory to characterize the beam selection error probability. Furthermore, we develop an optimal resource allocation framework that determines the optimal number of beams to maximize harvested energy by exploiting the concavity property of the harvested energy function. A bisection-based optimization algorithm is proposed to efficiently find the optimal solution. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed algorithm achieves higher harvested energy compared to the exhaustive codebook search method by reducing training overhead.

title: A Two-Layer Deception Attack Detection Framework for UAV
authors: Zhichuang Wang, Jingze Yang, Zhijie Liu, Wenjie Ning
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: This paper investigates the problem of real-time deception attack detection for unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) under resource-constrained conditions. First, deception attacks are classified into trajectory hijacking attacks and constant bias attacks according to their dynamic characteristics. Then, we develop a physically interpretable two-level XGBoost feature construction that decouples transient anomaly detection from long-term bias identification. Furthermore, an event-triggered activation mechanism is developed to effectively reduce computational burden while maintaining reliable detection performance. The verification results of the test set show that for trajectory hijacking attacks, the detection accuracy of the proposed framework reaches 95.8 percent, and for constant deviation attacks, the detection accuracy of the proposed framework is 98.9 percent. Moreover, through analysis of complexity and cost, it is demonstrated that the proposed method is lightweight and highly efficient. Real-world UAV flight experiments further verify the effectiveness and practicality of the proposed method under real deception attacks, highlighting its suitability for real-time onboard deployment.

title: Developed Group Bi-Level Role Assignment_supp1-3678707.pdf
authors: Bo Wang
publish: N/A
abstract: Role-Based Collaboration (RBC) theory and its ECARGO model, composed of Environment, Classes, Agents, Roles, Groups, and Objects, have significantly transformed collaborative work and addressed a range of practical challenges. Existing literature has primarily focused on single-level role assignment, while recent studies have introduced Group Bi- Level Role Assignment (GBLRA), which tackles collaborative assignment problems in which the assignment of subordinate roles triggers the need for leader roles. However, GBLRA still suffers from several limitations, which can be summarized as follows: (1) it does not account for the fact that a role generation plan may produce multiple distinct leader roles at once; (2) it fails to set qualification boundaries for agents acting as leader roles or consider the impact of their competence on group performance; and (3) it overlooks the influence of ungrouped agents on group performance. To address these issues, this paper extends the GBLRA framework and proposes two enhanced models: Developed GBLRA-1 (DGBLRA-1) and DGBLRA-2. Experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed models.

title: Experimental Demonstration of All-optical Input Masking in Photonic Time-stretch Reservoir Computing
authors: Yuanli Yue, Hao Zhang, Chao Wang
publish: Journal of Lightwave Technology
abstract: We propose and experimentally demonstrate a photonic reservoir computing (RC) system with integrated all optical input masks to enhance processing performance. Leveraging photonic time-stretch (PTS) and spectral mixing, the system employs optical wavelengths as virtual reservoir nodes, thereby improving spectral interaction and memory capacity during feedback. To overcome electronic bottlenecks and reduce bandwidth requirements, we design two optical input mask schemes: (i) a programmable optical filter based on parallel diffraction gratings, and (ii) an analog mask generated by a Mach Zehnder Interferometer (MZI). Both approaches introduce randomness directly in the optical domain prior to modulation, enriching reservoir dynamics without relying on high-speed electronics. Proof-of-concept experiments on waveform and spoken-digit classification validate the effectiveness of our method. The proposed system achieves accuracies of 97.19% with the MZI based mask and 98.16% with the diffraction gratings mask, compared with 80.65% in the no-mask case. These results demonstrate that all-optical masking not only significantly improves classification performance but also alleviates electronic constraints, making it highly suitable for real-time recognition in high-speed photonic imaging systems.

title: A Hybrid Ensemble Approach to Crop Yield Prediction in India Using Machine Learning Algorithms
authors: Vaidheswari S, Balaji S
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: Efficient and accurate crop yield predictions models are essential for farmers and policymakers. Even though ensemble Machine Learning (ML) models are widely used in computer science, their application in crop yield prediction are not explored well till now. This study, conducted in Tamil Nadu, India discusses about use of machine learning ensemble models in the prediction of crop suitability and the maximization of yield under varied climatic regimes in India focusing on the implementation of refined regression models like Random Forest and Linear Regression into an enhanced hybrid platform. Hybrid ensemble method optimally combines stacking and bagging methods, where Random Forest prevails over others because of its better ability to handle nonlinear relationships and minimizing prediction errors. This new modelling approach utilizes targeted preprocessing techniques, specifically data imbalances correction by statistical adjustments, thereby effectively reducing inconsistencies in farm data, such as rainfall, temperature, and pesticide usage variations. Large-scale exploratory analysis through heatmap visualizations distinctly marks optimal climatic areas suitable for specific crops, providing actionable insights on regional agricultural planning and strategic resource allocation. Lastly, this study strongly supports the application of hybrid ensemble machine learning as a robust, precise, and efficient solution to the complex problem of maximizing agricultural productivity amidst climatic uncertainties typical of India. Empirical results evidently demonstrate how ensemble regression models enhance predictive accuracy, computational efficiency, and practicability in real- world applications, validating the model's viability for strategic use in sustainable agricultural decision-making systems. The study establishes a significant methodological benchmark for future integration of ensemble learning paradigms into smart agriculture management systems, undoubtedly paving the way for revolutionary breakthroughs in crop development strategies.

title: Hybrid CNN - BiLSTM Framework for EEG-Based Sleep Stage Classification and Drowsiness Modeling
authors: Sasikala. M, Akilandeswari J, Mazhaiyarasan. M, Mythili. S, Koundika Periyasamy, Parthasarathy. L
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: Drowsiness has long been recognized as a major cause of accidents and reduced performance in safety-critical fields such as transportation, aviation, and industrial operations. EEG signals provide a non-invasive and dependable method of monitoring the patterns of brain activity that are related to alertness and fatigue. However, Existing detection approaches are however ineffective in capturing the spatial and temporal dynamics of EEG data which limits classification accuracy, as well as real-time applicability. In a bid to overcome this drawback, our work suggests a hybrid deep-learning architecture that combines the features of the Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) to extract spatial features and Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (Bi-LSTM) networks to model the time series. EEG recordings were automatically processed, split into 5-second epochs, and categorized into four physiologically significant sleep stages (W, N1, N2, and N3). Accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score were used to assess performance. Automatic sleep stage classification is a validated physiological proxy for drowsiness modeling in safetycritical systems because real-world drowsiness correlates neurologically to the transitional boundary between wakefulness (W) and light sleep (N1). The proposed model achieved an overall accuracy of 88.28% for multi-class classification across the four sleep-stage alertness levels: W, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{N} 1, \mathrm{N} 2$</tex>, and N 3. The findings show that the suggested hybrid framework offers a dependable and efficient method for classifying sleep stages based on EEG for drowsiness modeling, with great potential for integration into intelligent fatigue monitoring and safety-support systems.

title: A Class of Distance-Optimal Divisible Codes From Simplicial Complexes
authors: Vidya Sagar, Rong Luo, Avik Ranjan Adhikary, Zhengchun Zhou
publish: 2025 11th International Workshop on Signal Design and Its Applications in Communications (IWSDA)
abstract: Divisible codes are linear codes in which all codeword weights are divisible by a fixed integer, offering elegant algebraic structure and deep connections to projective geometry and combinatorics. Their uniform weight properties make them useful in applications such as secret sharing, symmetric designs, and cryptographic protocols. In this paper, we use a generic method for constructing linear codes over the finite chain ring <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathcal{R}=\mathbb{F}_{2}[u] /\left\langle u^{4}-1\right\rangle$</tex>. Defining sets are constructed with the help of simplicial complexes. By considering an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbb{F}_{2}$</tex>-linear map, we obtain a class of binary <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$t$</tex>-divisible codes. These binary codes are distance-optimal. Moreover, we obtain certain conditions under which these codes are minimal and self-orthogonal.

title: Dual-Agent Adversarial Framework Based on Endogenous Security and Multi-Objective Reinforcement Learning
authors: Xiyou Qian, Yuxuan Ye, Kai Wang, Siyuan Mao, Yujie Wang, Xuewen Yu, Hantao Zhao
publish: 2025 International Conference on Cyber Resilience and Endogenous Safety &amp; Security (CRESS)
abstract: In recent years, reinforcement learning has demonstrated significant potential in achieving automated penetration testing. However, current research primarily focuses on either the offensive or defensive aspects, with efforts centered on unilaterally optimizing penetration testing exploration paths, improving exploration efficiency, or developing effective defense mechanisms. These approaches fail to adequately address the evolving demands of network resilience security. This paper constructs a unified model based on adversarial reinforcement learning that integrates vulnerability discovery and security resilience assessment, while incorporating proactive defense strategies to address endogenous security requirements. By employing a multi-objective Markov decision process model and a weighted Chebyshev decomposition strategy, we achieve a balance between attack depth and breadth, as well as between defense effectiveness and cost. Additionally, through random network distillation and coverage mechanisms, we continuously encourage the agent to explore more cost-effective behaviors, thereby addressing the issue of excessively high defense costs in endogenous security. Experiments conducted on the NASim platform show that, compared with the random strategy, the defender agent trained by our model only incurs an additional 15.5% action overhead, while increasing the average steps required by the attacker by 62.7%. This result validates the effectiveness of the proposed model in vulnerability discovery and security resilience assessment, while also highlighting its superiority in balancing defensive efficacy and resource costs.

title: Optimization of AI-Based Student Answer Sheet Evaluation in Educational Institutes
authors: Prashant Ashok Ghewari, Nishant Kumar
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: Automated evaluation of student answer sheets using AI and large language models (LLMs) has become an essential tool in modern education. This study uses Optimization of Student Answer Sheet Evaluation in Educational Institutes through five classical optimization methods—Dichotomous Search, Golden Section Search, Steepest Descent, Newton, and Conjugate Gradient to minimize overall evaluation cost and teacher efforts while maintaining fairness and accuracy. The optimization problem involves multiple variables such as scanning cost, AI evaluation cost, teacher verification effort, and system computation resources. Three real time Test Cases for AI Student Answer Evaluation were analyzed, representing different levels of answer-key and reference coverage. By comparing the result of classical optimization method, we conclude that iterative grading method converges faster, with Conjugate Gradient Method achieving the lowest total cost and fastest convergence. Our study concludes that Conjugate gradient approach with second order is suitable for optimization of AI based student answer evaluation system for educational Institutes.

title: Automated Cardiovascular Disease Diagnosis via Enhanced ECG Preprocessing, Time-Frequency Spectral Mapping, and Explainable Deep Neural Models
authors: V Bharathi, S Gokulanandhan, L. Sowmiya
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: The proposed project presents a smart diagnostic system that can analyze ECG data automatically in order to identify and differentiate various cardiac conditions with a high level of accuracy. Raw ECG signals are subjected to an improved form of preprocessing such as bandpass filtering, normalization, heartbeat detection and optional deep-learningbased noise-removal to reduce noise and artifacts. Short-Time Fourier Transform (STFT) and Continuous Wavelet Transform (CWT) Timefrequency analysis converts ECG into full rich 2D representations of spectral, temporal and morphological information. Deep learning models such as CNN, LSTM and hybrid CNN-LSTM do a good multi-class classification and explainable AI models, Grad-CAM and SHAP offer a way to interpret the predictions of a model to be used in clinical thus validation. The more complex modules allow real-time tracking and adjustment of patients, detecting multiple diseases, augmenting data, and operating on cloud or edge devices, topped with an automated report creation. It is an allencompassing framework that provides a clinically dependable, interpretable, and scalable answer to accurate and constant cardiovascular disease diagnosis.

title: Compact Wideband Slotted Swastika Shaped Electromagnetic Bandgap Structures for mm-Wave Communication
authors: Yuvashree S, Abirami E, Azraa naaz S A, Mohan C
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: This paper introduces a small wideband Swastikatype Electromagnetic Bandgap (EBG) structure for mm-wave communication platforms. The suggested EBG unit cell is built on a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3.6 \times 3.6 ~\text{mm}^{2}$</tex> square FR-4 substrate that is 0.8 mm thick. To obtain better bandgap properties and harmonic suppression in the mm-wave band, a metallic patch in the shape of a swastika is merged with several circular slots. A broad operational band spanning 29.5 to 33.6 GHz, or a bandwidth of 4.1 GHz around the central frequency of 32 GHz, is demonstrated using full-wave models. The suggested EBG unit cell is ideal for incorporation in compact <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$5 \mathrm{G} / 6 \mathrm{G}$</tex> front-end modules and dense mm-wave antenna arrays owing to its small size and broad stopband capabilities.

title: Performance Evaluation of Handcrafted Feature Descriptors for Skin Disease Classification using Decision Tree Classifier
authors: Ayyappan G, Senthil K
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: Effective dermatologic interventions in addition to clinical decisions entail a timely accurate identification of dermatological conditions. This study involves an analysis of the six manually designed image feature extracting algorithms, i.e. Auto Color Correlogram Filter (ACCF), Binary Patterns Pyramid Filter (BPPF), Fuzzy Color and Texture Histogram Filter (FCTHF), Fuzzy Opponent Histogram Filter (FOHF), Gabor Filter, and JPEG Coefficient Filter (JPEG CF), in collaboration with the Decision Tree classifier in the context of automating dermatological diagnosis. The analysis involved the utilization of images of the Skin Diseases Image Dataset of the Kaggle which comprises of over thirty-eight thousand pictures which span in ten main skin diseases categories. After every feature descriptor was used, the resulting processed image set was classified using the implementation of Decision Tree algorithm via WEKA 3.9.5 environment. Standard evaluation methods were applied in estimating the evaluation measures that comprised of Accuracy and also Precision, Recall, Receiver Operating Characteristic (ROC) and Precision-Recall Curve (PRC) besides Computation Time. The currently conducted evaluation indicated that the overall estimate in performance of JPEG CF in combination with a Decision Tree approach was 97.05 percent accuracy along with precision and recalls of 0.97 and ROC/PRC magnitude of 0.98. FOHF exhibited both a mixture of 95.55 percent rate of accuracy and 0.03 seconds of fastest classification. The proposed models hold the potential of being applied in the platforms of clinical decision support.

title: EfficientNet-Driven Multi-Class Spine X-Ray Analysis for Automated Osteoporosis Detection
authors: A J S NagaLakshmi, T Murali Mohan, Pamidi Srinivasulu, Tulasi Raju Nethala
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: Osteoporosis is a progressive metabolic bone disorder characterized by heightened risk of vertebral fractures, reduced Bone Mineral Density (BMD), and increased fragility. Standard diagnostic modalities like Quantitative Computed Tomography (QCT) and Dual-Energy X-ray Absorptiometry (DEXA) are accurate but remain nonportable, cost-intensive, and often inaccessible in resource-limited and rural clinical environments. This research work develops a noninvasive and automated deep learning framework for osteoporosis detection utilizing X-ray images of spine. The system designs a fine-tuned EfficientNet-B1 framework for extracting strong radiological features and a fully connected classification network for decision-making. The model demonstrated accuracy of 94.2 %, specificity of 96.4 %, and AUC of 0.97 for osteoporosis detection, outperforming traditional CNN baselines. A Django-based web platform allows real-time prediction, making the system deployable in point-of-care and telemedicine screening environments. The results demonstrate that proposed deep learning-based system on spine X-rays offer a fast, low-cost, and clinically feasible solution for early osteoporosis detection.

title: A Review on Deep Learning Techniques for Fast and Explainable Network Intrusion Detection
authors: Jenish M, K. Karuppasamy
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: The increasing complexity and scale of modern networked environments, including Internet of Things (IoT), industrial, and edge-based systems, have intensified the need for effective and trustworthy Intrusion Detection Systems (IDS). While Deep Learning (DL) based IDS have demonstrated high detection accuracy compared to traditional approaches, their practical adoption is constrained by high computational overhead, inference latency, and limited interpretability. This review presents a systematic analysis of recent research on deep learning driven IDS with a particular emphasis on explainability, performance efficiency, and real-time deployability. The study categorizes existing works based on research focus, learning models, explainability techniques, and deployment environments, and evaluates their performance trade-offs using comparative analysis. Furthermore, key technical limitations such as high latency, dataset bias, static training, and vulnerability of post-hoc explainability methods are identified. Emerging research directions, including federated and lightweight IDS, neurosymbolic learning, incremental adaptation, and explainability frameworks, are discussed. The findings highlight a critical research gap in developing IDS solutions that simultaneously achieve high detection accuracy, low latency, resource efficiency, and transparent decision-making. This review aims to provide a consolidated reference for researchers and practitioners toward designing scalable, real-time, and explainable intrusion detection systems for next-generation network security.

title: Application of K-Means Clustering for Optimized Municipal Solid Waste Management
authors: Chandni Manral, Amit Mittal, Anant Deogaonkar, Chandan Vichoray, Siddharth Joshi
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: The appropriate management of MSW in these urbanizing areas is one of the problems encountered on account that waste production is increasing and there are no capabilities for its collection, transfer and disposal. In this paper we present a data-based model, based on K-Means clustering for assisting the waste disposal process. We consider realistic ammonia production and composition also, to cluster the urban zones according to their waste composition, so that segregation is implemented only at such collection points. To analyze the cluster patterns as well as the spatial distribution pattern of the clusters, and to evaluate performance metrics, the program is implemented on MATLAB. It was found that clustering leads to remarkable operational cost savings in terms of travel time, better bin allocation and focused waste separation activities. The proposed approach furnishes valuable feedbacks to municipal authorities for sustainable and economic solid waste management, which can possibly be scaled up for smart city applications.

title: Adaptive Irrigation System Based on Crop Growth Phases and Environmental Monitoring
authors: Ambika R, Divya K S, B. Priyadarshini
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: Rice cultivation is noted for its water-intensive nature, and poor irrigation practice often leads to wastage, lower efficiency, and lower productivity. Crop water stress often occurs in rice fields as a result of variability in soil conditions, climatic conditions, and varieties, which make it a challenge for farmers in determining the amount of water needed during different stages of crop development. Addressing this issue, the following project presents a Rice Irrigation Planner that estimates stage-wise water requirements by exploiting environmental and crop growth data. The system accommodates user input in the form of soil type, zone, variety, soil moisture, temperature, rain, and planting time. Based on these, it generates a thorough irrigation schedule covering the four rice growth stages, namely Nursery, Tillering, Flowering, and Ripening. The novelty in the present work lies in its ability to generate a personalized, stage-wise irrigation schedule with both numeric and graphical outputs, thus ensuring effective water use, curbing wastage, and improved production. The overall goal is generating data-based irrigation management techniques that support a sustainable crop production method.

title: Emotion-Aware Diet Recommendation System Using Deep Learning and Natural Language Inputs
authors: KS Shashikala, Sandyarani Vadlamudi, Jahnavi S R, K Bhoomika, Tejaswini R Konnur, Kallu Deva Keerthana
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: This paper proposes an intelligent diet recommendation framework that incorporates emotional awareness alongside nutritional guidance. The system employs deep learning and natural language processing (NLP) to deliver meal suggestions tailored not only to individual dietary objectives but also to the user's emotional condition. Unlike conventional diet planners that emphasize nutritional requirements alone, the proposed approach integrates psychological well-being into decision making. Emotional cues are extracted from unstructured text inputs, such as diary notes or mood descriptions, through a fine-tuned transformer model (e.g., BERT or RoBERTa). Detected emotions are then mapped to food groups and nutrients linked to mental health regulation, including serotonin-enhancing or magnesium-rich options. A generative model, such as GPT-2, produces coherent, context-aware meal plans in natural language, further refined to support user-specific requirements such as diabetes management or weight control. By bridging the domains of nutrition and affective computing, this work aims to provide a holistic and adaptive approach to diet planning that promotes both physical and emotional well-being.

title: Golay-ZCZ Sequence Sets with Flexible Lengths Based on Matrices of Polynomials
authors: Shibsankar Das, Adrish Banerjee, Udaya Parampalli, Zilong Liu
publish: 2025 11th International Workshop on Signal Design and Its Applications in Communications (IWSDA)
abstract: In this paper, we develop a generalized construction framework of zero-correlation zone (ZCZ) sequence sets based on para-unitary (PU) matrices. We show that any square PU matrix with unimodular sequences can generate ZCZ sequence sets by perfectly controlling its expanded product form, thereby improving upon existing results. The key idea is to impose specific conditions on the expanded product forms of PU matrices to enable accurate computation of periodic correlation functions. In addition, each constructed ZCZ sequence set is a PU column vector and hence it represents a Golay complementary set (GCS). In this work, we call them Golay-ZCZ sequence sets. The lengths, set sizes and ZCZ widths of the constructed Golay-ZCZ sequence sets are all flexible with the form of non-power-of-two. These Golay-ZCZ sequence sets can be directly applied to practical training-based channel estimation schemes in multiple-antenna systems.

title: Numerical Simulation of General Methods of Electric Heating in Industry
authors: Maksim I. Pirozhkov, Sergey A. Bychkov
publish: 2026 ElCon Conference of Young Researchers in Computing &amp; Processing, and Information Security (ElCon-CP)
abstract: The paper presents a comprehensive analysis of electrical heating methods relevant to computing and industrial usage. Various approaches, including resistance, arc, induction, and dielectric heating, are considered with focus on their efficiency, thermal response, and applicability in different heating processes. A comparative assessment is carried out based on analytical modeling and simulation results. The study offers insights into various heating techniques to improve system stability, to compare heating uniformity for every method. Potential applications in hardware reliability enhancement and growing efficiency.

title: Grid Evolution Based on Cluster Fusion for Doubly Fractional Channel Estimation in OTFS Systems
authors: Xiangjun Li, Qianli Wang, Zhengchun Zhou, Zilong Liu
publish: 2025 11th International Workshop on Signal Design and Its Applications in Communications (IWSDA)
abstract: In orthogonal time-frequency space communications, the performance of the uniform grid channel estimation (CE) is generally determined by the delay-Doppler (DD) grid density. A finer grid interval is needed to reduce modeling errors, but this could result in a significantly higher CE complexity when traditional methods are used. Although the grid evolution (GE) method can achieve a good trade-off between complexity and CE performance, there are still many redundant DD grid points that incur additional computational complexity. To address this issue, a new GE method based on cluster fusion for doubly fractional CE is proposed. Simulation results show that our proposed method leads to improved computational efficiency, and achieves a good trade-off between CE performance and complexity.

title: A Blockchain-Based Lightweight Distributed Identity Authentication Mechanism
authors: Bingjing Wang, Xiuju Huang, Cong Zuo, Licheng Wang, Youqi Li
publish: 2025 International Conference on Cyber Resilience and Endogenous Safety &amp; Security (CRESS)
abstract: The growing number of connected devices creates a strong demand for secure and private identity management across different networks. Conventional centralized systems suffer from a single point of failure, while many decentralized, blockchain-based solutions struggle to balance scalability, functional versatility, and privacy protection. To bridge these gaps, we propose a Blockchain-based Lightweight Dual-mode Authentication (BLDA) mechanism. BLDA introduces two distinct authentication pathways: the first achieves constant-time verification based on a dynamic cryptographic accumulator integrated with zero-knowledge proofs (ZKPs), offering optimal efficiency and unlinkability for simple membership checks. The second enables logarithmic-time verification based on a Merkle Patricia Trie (MPT) and ZKPs, providing efficient and privacy-preserving attestation of specific user attributes. Both modes ensure minimal information disclosure during authentication. A security and complexity analysis demonstrates that BLDA provides a secure and efficient framework, well-suited for large-scale applications requiring efficient cross-domain authentication.

title: Enhanced SQL Injection Detection Using Advanced Feature Engineering and Machine Learning Ensemble Methods
authors: Assylzhan Toktassyn, Mohammed Al-Hubaishi
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: SQL injection is one of the most common threats to web applications, and in this paper, we design and evaluate a practical machine learning based approach to detect SQLi attacks through combining rich feature engineering with ensemble learning. For each SQL query, we extract more than 15 statistical, structural, and entropy based features to capture both syntax patterns and semantic cues that indicate malicious intent, and using a public benchmark dataset, we compare 8 machine learning models under two train-test splits (80: 20 and 50: 50) and evaluate them using multiple metrics. Our results show that tree-based ensemble models (especially Random Forest and Gradient Boosting) consistently achieve more than 99% accuracy with F1-scores of 0.99 on both splits, significantly outperforming other methods, and these results indicate that our feature engineering pipeline together with strong ensemble classifiers provide an effective and computationally efficient solution for real-time SQL injection detection in modern web applications. Index terms: SQL Injection, Machine Learning, Feature Engineering, Ensemble Methods, Cybersecurity, Web Application Security.

title: 3D Self-Supervised Contrastive Learning for Early Glioblastoma Detection in MRI Volumes Using Hybrid Swin Transformer-UNet Architecture
authors: Kavitha A, Girivasan M, Gopinath S
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: The Glioblastoma (GBM) is termed as one of the worst and fatal brain tumors. This could be blamed on the late onset, quick development and the limited remedies. To deal with these issues, the present paper suggests a novel approach to the problem by offering a new way of identifying and phases of glioblastoma work in their initial and precise phases. It is a self-supervised contrastive learning optimized categorized deep learning model. Swin Transformer and UNet are hybrid networks on the multimodal MRI data. The device is applicable in documenting the information about the volumetric position and more specific local images of the tumor regions. It also has the benefit that the model is trained on unlabeled volumes of MRI to learn powerful representations and will extrapolate well when shown with small volumes of labelled data. Pre-processing steps of the pipeline are head stripping, N4 bias field collapse and z-score correction. It is important to note that it improves the quality of the picture and makes the intensity uniform in both cases of subject related conditions. It will possess attention-based feature fusion module in which it will merge the strengths of Swin Transformer and the backbone model of the UNet. It is shown to be more delicate, durable and not to undergo early phases as compared to other dominating approaches. The strategy provides self-supervised-learning, hybrid-based, and multimodal analysis, which is a useful and scalable diagnosis tool. It aims at streamlining the clinical decision process leading to improvement of the prognosis of the patient.

title: AI-Powered Fake Job Detection Using Multimodal Hybrid Intelligence
authors: Shree Raam Vishaal K, Sudharsan S, R. Sudha
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: The rapid expansion of recruitment platforms has turned job scams into a growing menace often cloaked in the guise of work-from-home opportunities or seemingly official HR outreach. This paper introduces JobScamShield, a fake-job detection system that employs a hybrid intelligence approach to pinpoint fraudulent job postings and related communications. By melding a DistilBERT transformer-based NLP model, with handcrafted rule-based filters and a credibility-scoring mechanism the system delivers detection that's both precise and attuned to nuances. We built a pipeline that pulls text out of PDFs and images, with OCR runs a reputation check on every URL and verifies a company's legitimacy by scraping web data. A clean structured data flow then lets us fine-tune on our datasets incorporating regularization techniques such as dropout and early stopping to keep the model honest and improve generalisation. On top of that the platform ships, with a dashboard, a blacklist-management tool and a crowdsourced dataset portal all aimed at boosting transparency and fostering learning. JobScamShield runs, inside Docker containers taps into Firebase for its back-end. Presents a React-Tailwind front-end that lets users classify and visualize scams in time through an intuitive web interface. In our experiments the hybrid design nudged accuracy beyond the 94% threshold while slashing rates dramatically compared with stand-alone models. Altogether the system delivers a explainable and readily accessible solution that safeguards job seekers and pushes forward research, on recruitment security.

title: Startup Business Profit Prediction Using Machine Learning Models
authors: Sathika Begum Mohammad Shabi, M. Geetha, K. Vanishree, Muthupandi G, S. Pavithra
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: Start-ups are the empire of the contemporary era, where vision, mission and choices become the very essence of their empire. Start-up experts are leveraging their own capital to accelerate an invention and economic activity, but can not predict the future financial benefit of a start-up because the market stay under researched, therefore there are funding constraints. This study discusses a methodology for predicting start-up profitability that begins by defining data scoping methods, data pre-processing methods, then using statistically driven methods such a Random Forest, supervised learning. This study goes on to analyse a suitable data set to construct, and evaluate predictive models which include indicators such as, R&D spending, admin costs, marketing costs, and location. The model presented improves the previous method with the use of advanced algorithms to provide more insights on predictors of profitability. The predictions also deliver utility value to investors and entrepreneurs to support their decision making and fund management. The analysis will create opportunity for further discussion into start-up analysis, including the opportunity to use larger data sets and to include more qualitative indicators to improve predictive models even further, while highlighting the value of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{A I}$</tex> in the business forecasting.

title: PulmoXLM-Net: A Hybrid Deep Liquid Learning Framework for Automated Lung Cancer Detection and Classification
authors: C. Avinash Reddy, D. Ganga Shivani, B. Lakshmi Devi, M. Architha, G. Dharma Teja, G. Thirumalaiah
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: Lung cancer is a deadly cancer in the world and therefore it is very much important that early and precise detection is done. PulmoXLM-Net is a two step lung cancer detector and classifier research that utilizes Xception, Liquid State Machines (LSMs) and Multi-Class SVM (MSVM) in this paper. In the first stage, preprocessing of CT images in the LIDC-IDRI dataset is done through median filtering and morphological operations and active contour segmentation. Refinement of tumor regions is performed by binary conversion, circle detection and area estimation. Xception network finds out the spatial and texture features of the segmented regions at the second stage that are dynamically added by LSMs to detect sequential patterns then classified into Adenocarcinoma, Large Cell Carcinoma, Squamous Cell Carcinoma and Normal through MSVM. The performance is determined with the help of accuracy, sensitivity, specificity, and precision that would demonstrate the effective segmentation and proper classification. PulmoXLM-Net is a decision support system that is good in lung cancer screening.

title: Numerical Study on Water Vapor Mixing Ratio Effects in Helium Atmospheric-Pressure Plasma Jets
authors: Feifei Suo, Jizhong Sun, Jiao Zhang, Yanhui Wang, Dezhen Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Plasma Science
abstract: This study numerically investigates the impact of varying H<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>O concentrations on the generation of reactive species in a helium atmospheric-pressure plasma jet (APPJ). To understand their influence on the discharge process and the concentrations of reactive species, simulations are performed with different water vapor concentrations (0.2%, 0.5%, and 1%) in the ambient environment. The presence of water vapor leads to electron consumption through attachment reactions with H<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>O molecules, which in turn affects the plasma characteristics. Furthermore, in the afterglow phase following the discharge pulse, continuous reaction pathways govern the production and consumption of reactive species. The results show that variations in ambient humidity influence jet discharge behavior via interactions between the plasma and water vapor.

title: A Systematic Review of Electric Vehicle Supply Chains: Challenges and Research Agenda
authors: Xiaojun Wang, Yudi Zhang, Joseph Amankwah-Amoah
publish: IEEE Transactions on Engineering Management
abstract: Among a growing scholarly discourse on electric vehicle (EV) supply chains, much of the existing literature remains fragmented and lacks a clear agenda for future research. Drawing on the Preferred Reporting Items for Systematic Reviews and Meta-Analyses (PRISMA) protocol, this study presents a systematic review of peer-reviewed research on the EV supply chain published between 2010 and 2025 and yields 501 articles. We combine structured content analysis with topic modeling, which identifies six cross-disciplinary themes: adoption, policy, and market design, EV routing and scheduling, charging infrastructure, shared mobility platforms, grid integration and vehicle-to-grid (V2G) markets, and battery-swapping models. We synthesize the main debates within each theme and surface five cross-cutting challenges that cut horizontally across themes: interoperability and standards; capital intensity and risk allocation; upstream supply, circularity, and asset longevity; data governance; and equity and access. Collectively, our findings provide an integrated framework, a forward-looking research agenda, and impactful scholarly discourse on scaling EV supply chains

title: A Broadband Current Transmitter Based on a Zero-Voltage-Switching Converter for Multifrequency Electromagnetic Exploration
authors: Tao Guo, Zhenzhu Xi, He Wang, Shiqi Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Electronics
abstract: Frequency-domain electromagnetic method (FDEM) is crucial for near-surface exploration, with a trend toward multifrequency transmission to enhance efficiency. However, the inherent inductive nature of transmitter coils severely attenuates high-frequency signals, limiting shallow imaging capabilities—a challenge that existing multifrequency compensation schemes fail to fully address. To overcome this limitation, this article proposes a novel multifrequency square-wave current transmitter based on a zero-voltage-switching quasi-resonant converter (ZVSQRC). This technique effectively negates the inductive suppression, enabling the transmission of high-fidelity, broadband square-wave currents supporting various waveforms. The article presents the circuit topology, analyzes its operating principle, and provides detailed parameter design guidelines and a control strategy. Simulation and experimental results validate the proposed scheme, demonstrating a maximum operating frequency of 45 kHz under the given parameters. Compared to traditional direct-drive transmitters, the proposed circuit delivers significantly higher current amplitudes above 200 Hz, more than doubling the current at frequencies beyond 700 Hz, with the enhancement effect growing with frequency. Furthermore, it exhibits substantial advantages in both operational bandwidth and current amplitude over conventional triple-frequency resonant (TFR) circuits. This work provides a key enabling technology for developing next-generation, high-efficiency, and broadband FDEM systems.

title: Robust Whole-Body Balance Control of Wheeled-Bipedal Robot for Perception-less Terrain Adaptation
authors: In Dong Hwang, Woosong Kang, Wonsuk Jung, Dong Il Park, Young Hun Lee
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: In this paper, we present a robust whole-body controller which enables wheeled-bipedal robots to achieve robust locomotion on various environments in the absence of terrain perception. The proposed controller combines a whole-body motion planner and an optimization-based torque generator. By accounting for the floating base dynamics of the robot, the whole-body motion planner generates the terrain-adaptive motion based on postural stabilizing forces and the zero moment point (ZMP) that maintains the robot’s balance without relying on terrain information. In addition, the optimization-based torque generator integrates a linear quadratic regulator (LQR) and a quadratic programming (QP)-based controller. The LQR controller generates the wheel torques using the wheeled inverted pendulum model (WIPM) to regulate the robot body’s angle while considering the inherent non-minimum phase behavior. Given the computed wheel torques, the hierarchical QP controller distributes the optimal joint torques corresponding to the desired motion to realize the stable balance of the robot. The proposed controller is tested on a wheeled-bipedal robot in the Gazebo simulator. Locomotion abilities on various terrains including slopes, stairs, and uneven terrains and under external forces validated in simulations.

title: DPMB-Net: Dual-level Perturbation and Multi-scale Boundary Attention for Semi-supervised Temporomandibular Joint Segmentation
authors: Xiaoshan Pan, Bin Yao, Jing Guo, Yuzhao Liu, Gongyan Li
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Accurate segmentation of the temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disc and condyle from magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) is critical for clinical diagnosis and treatment planning of orofacial disorders. However, automated segmentation remains a formidable challenge due to the scarcity of high-quality pixel-level annotations, coupled with the anatomical complexity and low-contrast boundaries of the articular disc and condyle. Although semi-supervised learning (SSL) offers a promising solution by leveraging unlabeled data, existing methods often struggle to capture fine-grained morphological details and are prone to overfitting when applied to small-scale medical datasets. To address these limitations, we propose a novel semi-supervised segmentation framework termed Dual-level Perturbation and Multi-scale Boundary Attention Network (DPMB-Net), specifically designed for high-precision TMJ segmentation. Specifically, we introduce a feature-level perturbation strategy via channel-wise dropout within the encoder, compelling the model to maintain consistency between image-level and feature-level perturbations. This approach significantly expands the perturbation space, thereby enhancing the robustness of semantic representations. Furthermore, to address the pronounced scale disparity and boundary ambiguity inherent in TMJ structures, we design a Multi-scale Boundary Attention Head (MBAH). By integrating lightweight multi-scale dilated convolutions with channel attention mechanisms, the MBAH adaptively aggregates boundary contexts to refine structural delineation. Extensive experiments on a private TMJ dataset and public benchmarks demonstrate that our proposed method outperforms state-of-the-art semi-supervised approaches, yielding superior segmentation accuracy and generalization capability under limited annotation scenarios.

title: An Efficient ASCON-Based Group Authentication and Key Agreement Scheme With Non-Linkability and Integrity Assurance for IIoTs
authors: Haowen Tan, Shenmin Gu, Haifeng Zhu, Jian Shen, Zakirul Alam Bhuiyan, Q. M. Jonathan Wu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dependable and Secure Computing
abstract: In recent years, numerous group authentication and key agreement (GAKA) schemes have been proposed for the Industrial Internet of Things (IIoTs). However, the frequent identity updates that are a feature of IIoT environments mean that existing schemes cannot maintain full-lifecycle device anonymity. Meanwhile, static integrity verification approaches cannot effectively cope with the dynamic and heterogeneous nature of industrial networks, making it imperative to design an adaptive data integrity protection mechanism to maintain reliable and resilient communication. Additionally, the simultaneous access of a large number of intelligent industrial devices to gateway nodes creates significant computational and communication challenges, while also increasing the risk of denial-of-service attacks and other concurrent threats. Consequently, current schemes are unable to strike an optimal balance between efficiency and security in large-scale IIoT deployments. In our scheme, we first designed an anonymous token mechanism to achieve sufficient randomness for unlinkability when communicating with the same gateway node across different time periods. Secondly, integrating associated data into the authenticated sponge construction (ASCON) encryption process ensures the legitimacy and integrity of the data during transmission. Third, we conduct rigorous security proofs under the widely accepted Algebraic Group Model (AGM) and Random Oracle Model (ROM), demonstrating that our group authentication mechanism is unforgeable against adaptive chosen-public-key and adaptive chosen-subspace attacks. Finally, the performance evaluation demonstrates that our proposed scheme achieves improvement in computational efficiency, while the communication overhead analysis confirms that the design remains both efficient.

title: A Deadbeat Predictive Adhesion Control Method With Generalized Optimal Creep Model for HST Considering Low Adhesion Condition
authors: Ruiqi Li, Deqing Huang, Wei Yu, Luanhui Li, Hanlin Cai
publish: IEEE Transactions on Control Systems Technology
abstract: Running high-speed trains (HSTs) at the optimal slip ratio can significantly improve energy efficiency and actively prevent wheel slip. However, the intrusion of a third medium (such as water, oil, snow, etc.) introduces random uncertainties in the wheel–rail system’s creep characteristics, making it challenging for existing methods to estimate and track the optimal slip rate accurately, especially under high-speed conditions with low adhesion. To address this, a deadbeat predictive adhesion control method with a generalized optimal creep model is proposed. First, a predictive bandwidth (PB) adhesion force observer is introduced to determine the wheel–rail adhesion coefficient quickly and accurately. Next, a generalized optimal creep model is developed, which incorporates the measured wheel–rail creep curve for running speeds ranging from 40 to 400km/h under water medium conditions, enabling the identification of the optimal slip ratio. Building on the deadbeat control, a fast adhesion torque controller is then designed to track the optimal slip ratio. The hardware-in-the-loop (HIL) simulation results validate the effectiveness of the proposed method. Even when the train operates under high-speed low-adhesion conditions at 300km/h, the proposed method achieves an adhesion utilization rate of 94.38%, which is a 12.5% improvement over the traditional PI adhesion control method.

title: Society Officers
authors: N/A
publish: IEEE Instrumentation & Measurement Magazine

title: Learning 3D Representation from Auto-labeled 2D Object Boxes_supp1-3671587.pdf
authors: Jin Xie
publish: N/A
abstract: Recent advances in LiDAR representation learning with limited annotations show strong promise. Existing well-performed methods mainly focus on distilling the 2D representation into the 3D representation via superpixels. Superpixels are used to construct the cross-modal contrastive learning, leading to semantic ambiguity of 3D features belonging to the same object and impairing the performance. To this end, we aim to leverage unlabeled LiDAR-camera pairs to design a novel pre-training pipeline, which learns from category space directly and pulls the 3D features belonging to the same object close. Specifically, we obtain autolabeled 2D object boxes with a fixed 2D open-vocabulary object detector and transform the labeled 2D object boxes into high-quality pixel-wise label maps with a box-to-label-maps generation algorithm. Based on the pseudo labels, we present a dual-space pre-training 3D network that recognizes accurate categories from the semantic priors of paired 3D points and segments complete objects. Furthermore, we propose a module named AdaptPro to improve performance further when fine-tuning the 3D network under limited annotations, aiming to explore the unpaired 3D features that lack 2D correspondences via category prototypes. The experimental results show that our method achieves state-of-the-art performances on both the nuScenes and SemanticKITTI benchmark datasets. Code is avialable at https://github.com/dengq7/Box4Scene.

authors: N/A
publish: N/A
abstract: In federated deep Fuzzy C-Means (FCM) clustering, conventional federated averaging (FedAvg) struggles with non-independent and identically distributed (non-IID) data and dynamic device participation, leading to model drift and performance degradation during global aggregation. To address this challenge, we propose FedFCD, a federated deep FCM clustering method featuring a novel aggregation mechanism. FedFCD equips each client with a hybrid architecture comprising a contrastive autoencoder (CtAE) and an FCM network (FCMNet), which collaboratively learn stable low-dimensional embeddings and refine soft clustering assignments iteratively. At the server side, we design a two-phase aggregation strategy integrating Bayesian ensemble learning and knowledge distillation (KD). First, the Bayesian aggregation mechanism probabilistically fuses heterogeneous local models' inferences into a consensus assignment by treating each client's model as a candidate hypothesis, thereby constructing a posterior distribution over the global model space through iterative evidence accumulation. Subsequently, dual-source distillation harmonizes pseudo-labels derived from the Bayesian consensus with ground-truth labels from limited shared data, enabling the global model to align its predictions with both semantic anchors and aggregated soft assignments while preserving privacy through distillation loss. Comparative experiments on benchmark datasets demonstrate that FedFCD outperforms baseline methods in clustering accuracy and exhibits enhanced stability under varying conditions, including data heterogeneity, device numbers, and device dropout.

title: Explainable Deep Learning Framework Integrating Hybrid CNN and Vision Transformer for Early Detection and Localization of Tuberculosis in Chest X-Rays
authors: Manu P Jose, Roshini Thanka
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: The subject of this article is an Explainable Deep Learning Model for the Early Detection and Localization of Tuberculosis (TB) in Chest X-rays that has been developed with the aim of combining the accuracy of diagnostic with the interpretability of reliability in the therapy setting for human use. The framework that has been proposed uses a hybrid architecture which combines Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) with Vision Transformers (ViT) for the very accurate capture of local as well as global contextual information in lung regions. Besides, the preprocessing techniques can be applied with the help of Contrast Limited Adaptive Histogram Equalization (CLAHE) and normalization which lead to an increase in contrast and feature visibility. This is then followed by severity-aware data augmentation which acts as a further addition of robustness. The model takes EfficientNetB4 as its CNN backbone and a four-layer Transformer encoder with multi-head self-attention that leads to effective representation learning with less time consumed. Training occurs on the Kaggle TB Chest X-ray dataset comprising 4,200 images, which are categorized as Normal or TBpositive with a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$70-15-15$</tex> train-validation-test split. The model is trained using the Focal Tversky Loss function which is particularly designed for the effective handling of class imbalance issue. The results of the experiments indicate that the proposed CNNTransformer hybrid has achieved 95.5% accuracy which is higher than DenseNet121, ResNet50, MobileNetV2, and InceptionV3 baselines. The coupling of SHAP-based feature selection with the original model increases its interpretability by revealing the most relevant features that have the greatest impact on TB detection. In addition, Grad-CAM++ visualizations together with Transformer attention maps reach a mean Intersection over Union (IoU) of 82.8% in the accurate localization of TB-infected lung regions. Thus, the high diagnostic precision, the improved localization accuracy, and the strong explainability of the proposed model confirm that it is a very good decision-support system for the early detection of TB.

title: Design of Generalized Mimic Defense Module Based on Virtual Heterogeneous Containers
authors: Shen Li, Xu Chenwei, Wu Zicheng, Chen Shi, Zhang Song, Li Zhaozhao
publish: 2025 International Conference on Cyber Resilience and Endogenous Safety &amp; Security (CRESS)
abstract: In mimic defense systems, heterogeneous executors are mostly implemented using CPU hardware modules with different instruction set architectures. This heterogeneous executor construction technology has led to issues such as high costs, complex management, and insufficiently flexible architecture, which have restricted its application. Therefore, this paper proposes a method for constructing heterogeneous executors based on virtual heterogeneous containers. Applications with equivalent functions programed in different languages are subjected to containerized packaging to form heterogeneous container executors. Combined with customized mimic defense network cards, a flexible, lightweight, and low-cost generalized mimic defense module has been constructed, and it has been tested and applied in power information systems.

title: A Fair and Efficient Pricing Framework for EV Battery Swapping Based on Real-Time Life Cycle Prediction
authors: B. Venkatesu Goud, C. Venkata Bharani, S. Soban, E. Venkata Krishna, A. Naga Pranitheswar Reddy, C. Narendra Reddy
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: The study presents an integrated machine-learning framework for accurately predicting the Remaining Useful Life (RUL) of electric vehicle (EV) batteries and applying these predictions to dynamic fair pricing in battery-swapping stations. The framework employs XGBoost Regression alongside Random Forest, K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN), and ensemble techniques such as Stacking and Voting Regressors to enhance predictive accuracy and robustness. Real-world battery operational parameters, including discharge time, cycle index, voltage thresholds, and charging duration, are used for model training and evaluation. Ensemble models, particularly stacking, demonstrate superior performance with high <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}$</tex> values and low prediction error, confirming their effectiveness in capturing nonlinear degradation behavior. The predicted RUL is further integrated into a pricing mechanism that supports transparency, cost efficiency, and improved battery lifecycle management for EV users. The proposed system contributes to sustainable EV infrastructure by enabling real-time predictive maintenance and data-driven operational decisions.

title: Lung Vision: AI-Powered Tumor Detection in Pulmonary Imaging
authors: Srija N, Dineshkumar K, Harish K, Mohammed Faayiz M F
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: Affecting millions of people worldwide, lung cancer continues to be a significant health issue. Identifying lung carcinomas in their earliest stages leads to better therapies and has a higher chance of curing one's disease. As a result, diagnosing patients with lung cancer at the initial stages is imperative for improved prognosis. Since the sizes of tumors during these initial stages are quite small and subtle, radiology specialists take a considerable amount of time to check CT scans for lung tumors, with many making a mistake while reviewing a scan because of the likelihood of making a mistake while reviewing a scan manually. To overcome this limitation, an automated deep- learning-based automated image-analysis system is presented in this research to identify and categorize lung tumors quickly and accurately. The workflow for the development of the deep-learning system includes preprocessing techniques to, first, aid in eliminating background noise and enhancing tumor area visibility, and, second, improve the recognition of tumor boundaries in CT images. Once the preprocessed images were obtained, a convolutional neural network based upon a ResNet architecture was implemented for feature extraction and classification of their characteristics by eliminating the issues associated with vanishing gradient problem and allowing the deep-learning neural network to learn deeper and more unique sets of detailed features. In doing this, the system will now be able to identify small and/or early-stage tumors that would normally go undetected by a physician's manual visual inspection of CT images. The results of the experiments conducted in this research indicate that the proposed deeplearning model has a much greater level of accuracy, precision, recall, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$F 1$</tex> score than that of leading architectures/models (i.e., VGG16, InceptionV3, DenseNet121, and UNet), making it the better choice for the early diagnosis of lung cancer.

title: Early Breast Cancer Detection Using an Advanced Deep Learning Neural Network Framework
authors: M. Shoukath Ali, Jalagam Priyanka, A. Gopinath, Shalini Vaibhav Wankhade, Mohammad Shabbir Alam, Pooja Kallappagol
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: Breast cancer is the most prevalent malignancy afflicting American women; fortunately, mortality rates can be drastically reduced with early detection and prompt treatment. The high expense and radiation exposure of digital mammography with CE prevent its widespread usage, despite the fact that it outperforms ultrasound and conventional mammography in thick breast tissue in terms of accuracy. Early Breast Cancer Detection uses data pretreatment with Weiner Filtering and a region growth picture segmentation technique based on GSO. GSO ideally creates initial seed spots and threshold values. A hybrid strategy is used to increase feature extraction and forecast performance by combining traditional and deep learning methods. Introducing HitNet, a Hybrid Ternary RNN that uses ternary weights and activations, with a slope component in the activation functions to further improve accuracy and close the accuracy gap between full precision and quantised models. The suggested model is effective, as it obtains a precision of 94.06% when tested using a conventional RNN architecture. By merging state-of-the-art imaging analysis with hybrid neural network modelling, this method supplies a powerful instrument for early diagnosis of breast cancer, which has the ability to decrease breast cancer death rates and enhance diagnostic accuracy.

title: AI-Based Phishing Email and Link Detection Framework Through Generative AI and Explainable ML
authors: Sathiyapriya S, Subhasri B, D. Deepa
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: Phishing remains one of the most resilient cyberthreats, relying on social engineering and misleading email or website links to take advantage of users and organizations. Rulebased filters and traditional machine-learning (ML) methods work for previously seen threats but tend to miss upcoming or sophisticated attacks that leverage semantic trickery, including urgent communication, domain spoofing, or contextsensitive baits. This work proposes a hybrid AI-based framework integrating Generative AI and Explainable Machine Learning (XML) for real-time phishing email and malicious link detection. The suggested architecture incorporates a Generative AI component—relying on large language models (LLMs) with retrieval-augmented generation (RAG)—to explain the semantic meaning of email content and flag high-risk linguistic patterns and suspicious domains. A downstream ML classifier identifies Safe, Suspicious, and Phishing cases, and an Explainable ML module (utilizing SHAP and LIME) emphasizes the most impactful features, i.e., manipulated words, forged senders, or malicious URLs. Comparisons on benchmark phishing datasets such as PhishTank, Kaggle phishing data, Enron emails, and SpamAssassin show that the approach outperforms baseline ML models and conventional filters in detection accuracy, recall on zero-day attacks, and false positive reduction. Additionally, explanation-based feedback increases analyst trust and minimizes triage time.

title: Quantum-Inspired Intrusion Detection System Using Dual Metaheuristic Optimization and Deep Neural Analysis
authors: Dinsha P K, Santhosh R
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: The high rate of cyber threat development poses serious threats to the traditional Intrusion Detection System (IDS) that in most cases fail to detect effectively and falsely alarms when handling high volumes of high dimensional network traffic. In order to overcome these weaknesses, this study suggests a Quantum-inspired Hybrid IDS and it combines dual metaheuristic optimization and quantum-classical Deep Learning (DL) models. The framework initially preprocesses the raw network traffic data and then, the proposed Self-Adaptive Grasshopper-Whale Optimization Algorithm (SAGWOA) is employed to select features efficiently, i.e., to reduce the number of data dimensions and maintain important data. To enhance the nonlinear learning ability, Variational Quantum Classifier (VQC) is utilized which exploits quantum superposition and entanglement concepts. Besides, the proposed Quantum aided Deep Convolutional Support Vector Machine (QDC-SVM) is used for learning spatial and hierarchical patterns and performing quantum-enhanced classification. Here, the Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) is used to enable the deep feature learning and Quantum Support Vector Machine (QSVM) is employed to increase the margin separation between normal and attack traffic. Experimental analysis on benchmark datasets, such as NSL-KDD and CICIDS2017 shows the proposed hybrid IDS performs superior, in terms of detection accuracy (98.64 and 98.66% respectively) and lower false alarm rates (0.049% and 0.016% respectively), in comparison with traditional solutions.
