title:
Effects of Size Scaling on Reliability Issues of 3-D Trench Hf
                    <sub>0.5</sub>
                    Zr
                    <sub>0.5</sub>
                    O
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    Capacitor: Polarization Switching Kinetics Analysis and TCAD Simulation
authors: Dongping Qiao, Qingxiao Zhu, Yuanquan Huang, Hongye Yuan, Yihao Chen, Chengyan Tian, Zihao Lu, Pengfei Liu, Tiancheng Gong, Qing Luo
publish: IEEE Transactions on Electron Devices
abstract: In this work, we present a systematic study of size scaling effects on the reliability issues of Hf<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0.5</sub>Zr<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0.5</sub>O<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> (HZO) ferroelectric 3-D trench capacitor. First, based on the deep analysis of polarization switching kinetics, we find that with size scaling, the distribution of polarization switching becomes increasingly dispersed. Moreover, by characterizing defect density and simulating electric field profiles, we reveal that the electric field distribution becomes increasingly discrete with size scaling, which is attributed to the unique trench structure. Furthermore, possible solutions to suppress these effects are proposed, which will offer key guidelines for enhancing the reliability of 3-D trench capacitor.

title:
Dual-Gate WSe
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    /GeSe Heterojunction Phototransistors for Photodetection and Logic Operations
authors: Qing Mi, Yichi Zhang, Ningning Zhang, Tian Miao, Huiyong Hu, Hao Sun, Liming Wang, Zhangming Zhu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Electron Devices
abstract: Precise control over the conducting channel in optoelectronic devices is crucial for enhancing performance and enabling multifunctional operations. This article presents a dual-gate phototransistor based on a WSe<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>/GeSe van der Waals heterojunction. The top gate (TG) and the back gate (BG) modulate different regions of the channel, respectively. The rectification ratio (RR) of the heterojunction can be modulated under the BG and TG. Under 532-nm light illumination, the device achieves a responsivity (R) of 94 mA/W and a specific detectivity (D*) of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$4.23\times 10^{{11}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> Jones, both of which can be significantly modulated by the dual-gate structure. Meanwhile, the device achieved fast photo response, with rise and fall times of 0.24 and 0.51 ms, respectively. Additionally, the implementation of complex optoelectronic combinatorial logic operations is achieved through coordinated modulation of the dual gates. These results highlight the significant potential of the proposed device for developing next-generation optoelectronic systems.

title: Stability-Constrained Optimization of a FO(I+PID) Controller for Robust AGC Amid Variable Communication Latency and Hybrid Cyber Threats
authors: Deepak Kumar, Mohamed Alkhatib, Pulakraj Aryan, G. Lloyds Raja, Utkal Ranjan Muduli
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industry Applications
abstract: The phase lag associated with conventional internal model control strategies for thermal power plants (TPP) with dead time (DT) affects its ability to restore frequency and tie-line power deviations. For the aforementioned reason and foreseen merits of fractional calculus, a robust uni-parametric fractional order integral and proportional integral derivative (FO(I+PID)) controller is proposed for a renewable integrated TPP amid DT. This FO(I+PID) controller is designed based on a frequency-shifted version of the TPP's fractional model, which eliminates the need for phase-lag inducing internal model control filters in conventional designs. The frequency-shifting factor (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mu$</tex-math></inline-formula>) serves as the only tuning parameter that determines the performance and robustness of the FO(I+PID)-based load frequency control strategy, thereby offering the advantage of simplicity, which is desirable in practice. After applying Pade's approximation and stability considerations (to obtain the upper and lower bounds of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mu$</tex-math></inline-formula>), its optimal value is obtained using the crayfish optimization algorithm. The efficacy of this scheme is demonstrated through simulations, hardware-in-loop validation, and the IEEE 39-bus system by considering various practical scenarios like step load changes, hybrid cyber attacks, variable communication delay, nonlinearities associated with the TPP, and rigorous sensitivity-based stability/fragility analysis by perturbing TPP model/controller parameters. It outperforms <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$Max\_{v}$</tex-math></inline-formula> of tie-line power deflection by 70.57% and 72.44% compared to FIMC and TFC, respectively. Moreover, settling time is reduced to 103.2s, outperforming TFC (149.87s), FIMC (114.3s), and PI (131.87s). It confirms the better transient performance of the FO(I+PID) controller over reported techniques.

title: Generating Audiovisual Synergy Fluid Animation for Highly Immersive VR Experience_supp1-3679130.mp4
authors: Xiangcheng Zhai
publish: N/A
abstract: Generative content is increasingly applied in VR to provide immersive experiences, yet maintaining high generation quality remains challenging for audiovisual effects. Particularly in dynamic fluid phenomena, achieving realism and presence requires adherence to physical laws. To accomplish this objective, this work proposes an audiovisual synergy fluid animation generation framework, which enhances immersion by improving motion texture fidelity and audiovisual consistency. It comprises Detail-Enhanced Texture generator (DET) and Physics-Guided Audio generator (PGA). DET integrates Global-Local Physics guidance (GLP) and Temporal Texture Modeling (TTM) to produce video textures, explicitly optimizing dynamic details by leveraging local motion cues and assigned cumulative differences. PGA incorporates Visual Semantic Augmenter (VSA) and Rhythm Semantic Adapter (RSA) to synchronize audio by fusing static visual semantics with dynamic motion semantics to improve temporal coherence. By integrating DET and PGA, this framework strengthens audiovisual immersion in VR natural dynamic scenes from both visual and auditory perspectives. Quantitative and qualitative evaluations demonstrate that our approach surpasses most existing methods in terms of texture realism and audiovisual synchronization, offering new insights for advancing immersive experiences in dynamic VR phenomena.

title: Noise and Bandwidth Performance Optimization for Readout Electronics of dc Superconducting Quantum Interference Device
authors: Yang Shao, Shandong Li, Fuqing Shang, Zhengyu Wang, Quanming Gao
publish: IEEE Sensors Letters
abstract: Superconducting quantum interference devices (SQUIDs) impose stringent requirements on readout electronics in terms of both low noise and wide bandwidth. However, in flux-locked loop (FLL) readout systems, the overall noise performance is typically limited by the noise of the preamplifier, while insufficient bandwidth under high magnetic flux slew-rate conditions can readily lead to SQUID loss of lock. To address these issues, this paper proposes a noise and bandwidth co-optimization technique for dc SQUID readout circuits. This approach achieves bandwidth extension through a composite amplifier topology and, in combination with the noise suppression characteristics of a parallel architecture, realizes coordinated optimization of bandwidth and noise performance at the architectural level. Based on gain–bandwidth product theory and the statistical superposition principle of noise, optimal system bandwidth can be achieved by coordinated matching of the gains of the two amplification stages, while the statistical addition of uncorrelated noise sources is exploited to reduce the overall input-referred voltage noise. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed readout electronics scheme achieves a wide bandwidth of 1.2 MHz and an ultralow input-referred voltage noise of 0.58 nV/√Hz, providing an effective technical solution for high-bandwidth, low-noise SQUID applications.

title: Co-Designed Quasi-Reflectionless Low-Noise Amplifier With Bandpass Filter and RF Switching Functionalities
authors: Steven Matthew Cheng, Andrés Fontana, Dimitra Psychogiou
publish: IEEE Microwave and Wireless Technology Letters
abstract: In this letter, a reflectionless low-noise amplifier (LNA) filter (RLNAF) is presented, incorporating four distinct functions: 1) filtering; 2) reflection cancellation; 3) low-noise amplification; and 4) RF switching within a single component. It is comprised of a transmissive path and two absorptive paths. Specifically, a complex-termination methodology is applied to the transmissive path incorporating a bandpass filter (BPF) and LNA functionalities, removing the need for additional interstage matching networks (MNs). The absorptive paths are composed of a bandstop filter terminated with a loading resistor in one port to selectively dissipate the out-of-band reflected RF signals. Moreover, switch-off functionality is demonstrated by reconfiguring the bias of the transistor. For proof-of-concept validation purposes, two RLNAF prototypes are designed, manufactured, and measured at C-band. The RF characteristics are given as follows: RLNAF I: <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$f_{cen} = 4.65$</tex-math> </inline-formula> GHz, gain = 15.8 dB, noise figure (NF) = 2.07 dB, and quasi-reflectionless characteristics better than 10 dB from DC to 4.27 and 4.34 GHz to 6.42 GHz and at least 9.8 dB from 4.27 to 4.34 GHz; RLNAF II: <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$f_{cen} = 3.7$</tex-math> </inline-formula> GHz, gain = 15.3 dB, NF = 1.09 dB, and quasi-reflectionless characteristics better than 10 dB from DC to 4.75 and 5.75 GHz to 7.03 GHz and at least 6 dB from 4.75 to 5.75 GHz.

title: Lightweight Edge-Deployable Voltage Prediction Method for Distribution Networks With High Photovoltaic Penetration
authors: Zhijin Lyu, Wei Liu, Wei Du, Tian Gao, Xibin Shan, Kaiyuan Hu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Informatics
abstract: Under the continued growth of high penetration distributed photovoltaics (PV), PV interface voltages exhibit pronounced randomness, rapid fluctuations, and even overvoltage, making short-term voltage prediction increasingly critical yet more challenging under dynamic operating conditions. This growing reliance on accurate and timely prediction directly exposes the shortcomings of existing cloud-based forecasting schemes, which are constrained by communication delays, bandwidth limitations, and unstable connectivity, ultimately restricting their ability to provide the fast response required by a prediction-control loop. To address these issues, this study proposes a lightweight edge-deployable voltage prediction (LEDVP) method that consist of an ensemble algorithm and a distributed framework. The ensemble algorithm combines variational mode decomposition for voltage-signal decomposition and extreme gradient boosting for extracting the most informative features, which are then fed into a Mamba state-space sequence model to perform efficient linear-time temporal inference, forming a compact and lightweight prediction process suitable for embedded edge platforms. Moreover, by quantizing model parameters, pruning redundant structures, reusing intermediate caches, and optimizing the model specifically, the LEDVP can be deployed in the distributed framework. Experimental results show that the LEDVP method achieves near-lossless accuracy with a 1–3 s latency on NVIDIA Orin and yields approximately 40–50% lower root mean square error compared with representative decomposition-based neural network methods, while the LEDVP framework maintains stable resource usage. These results demonstrate the engineering feasibility and practical value of LEDVP for real-time voltage prediction, PV interface voltage regulation, and edge-side autonomy in PV-rich distribution networks.

title:
A 1.8-to-3.0-GHz Fully Integrated All-in-One CMOS Frequency Management Module Achieving −47-/+42-ppm Inaccuracy From −40
                    <sup>∘</sup>
                    C to 95
                    <sup>∘</sup>
                    C and −150/+70 ppm After Accelerated Aging
authors: Runtao Huo, Tong Zhang, Weihao Jie, Yanling Zheng, Deyong Li, Yiwen Zhang, Li Gao, Yang Zhao, Yongfu Li, Honglan Jiang, Minmin You, Jingquan Liu, Patrick P. Mercier, Hui Wang
publish: IEEE Journal of Solid-State Circuits
abstract: This article presents a fully integrated all-in-one CMOS frequency management module (FMM) that features a direct-frequency-synthesis (DFS) architecture by fully integrating an LC-based reference of a temperature- and aging-stable frequency <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$f_{\text {LC}} =1024$</tex-math> </inline-formula> MHz and by directly operating the synthesizer at <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$f_{\text {LC}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>. The FMM grants a <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$10\times $</tex-math> </inline-formula> wider loop bandwidth for over 20 dB of additional ring-oscillator (RO) noise suppression and supports fast frequency hopping with a settling time below <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$7~\mu $</tex-math> </inline-formula>s. Moreover, the FMM exploits the high-frequency sinusoidal reference with direct sampling, eliminating the need for a power-hungry divider and avoiding the division-ratio-squared in-band noise amplification, ultimately achieving an over 3-dB improved FoM<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">JIT</sub>. A 1.8-to-3.0-GHz frequency range with a 1-MHz channel step is achieved by employing a phase modulator for fractional-N frequency synthesis. Fabricated in 40-nm CMOS, the FMM consumes 13.5 mW and occupies an active area of 0.28 mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>. The measured 18 FMM samples achieve a worst case frequency inaccuracy of −47/+42 ppm from −40 to <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$95~^{\circ } $</tex-math> </inline-formula>C, and after one week of accelerated aging at <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$150~^{\circ } $</tex-math> </inline-formula>C, the same samples exhibit −150/+70-ppm inaccuracy. The proposed FMM shows an rms jitter and spur of 932 fs and −53.6 dBc, respectively, with a fast frequency hopping that settles within <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$7~\mu $</tex-math> </inline-formula>s, representing a fully integrated FMM with state-of-the-art frequency inaccuracy and excellent frequency-hopping capabilities.

title: A Trust Model for Human-Machine Interaction in Virtual Reality
authors: Lida Ghaemi Dizaji, Nusrat Z. Zenia, Yaoping Hu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: Human trust in machines is critical for effective human-machine interaction in virtual reality (VR). Prior work defined a three-layered framework of such trust but also indicated two deficiencies. Firstly, there is an absence of a model with metrics spanning all layers to objectively capture fluctuations of the trust (trust dynamics) in real time. Secondly, there is an inadequate consideration of human and machine reliability for the trust. Herein, this study proposed a trust model by defining metrics for all the layers and evaluated this model by considering human and machine reliability. Using objective and subjective data, the evaluation was based on two VR use-cases. The outcomes of the evaluation confirmed the pertinence of the model to capture trust dynamics in the presence of human and machine reliability. The objective data was notably more sensitive to capturing trust dynamics than the subjective counterpart. The model could enable designing trustworthy and adaptive VR.

title: Prescribed-Time Performance Platoon Control for Heterogeneous Connected Autonomous Vehicles With Information Protection Spacing Policy
authors: Jiaxin An, Yulian Jiang, Yanzheng Zhu, José de Jesús Rubio, Shenquan Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: This work addresses the adaptive prescribed-time performance platoon control (PTP-PC) problem for heterogeneous connected autonomous vehicles, which faces two key challenges: invading vehicle tracking and unknown overall disturbance. An information protection spacing policy is proposed to tackle the issue of tracking platoon by the invading vehicle. Besides, the variable gain nonlinear extended state observers (NESOs) and variable gain hyperbolic tangent tracking differentiators (HTTDs) are designed to effectively address the overall disturbance and complexity explosion issues, respectively. To obtain the specified transient and steady-state performance for the entire platoon, a prescribed-time performance function is established. On the basis of the above presented techniques, a distributed adaptive PTP-PC scheme is developed to ensure the vehicular bistability and superior prescribed performance. For practical considerations, both numerical simulations and co-simulation experiments based on PRESCAN/SIMULINK have been conducted to simulate a traffic scenario involving the transportation of college entrance examination papers, and the results have verified the feasibility of the developed scheme.

title: ABC-iVLP: Indoor Visible Light Positioning Using Attention Mechanism-based BiLSTM-CNN
authors: Qinghui Chen, Zhenheng Chen, Hong Wen, Ming Chen, Jie Ma, Chen Chen, Wenjuan Ruan
publish: IEEE Photonics Journal
abstract: Indoor positioning is essential in applications such as intelligent warehousing and innovative healthcare. Conventional solutions, such as the Global Navigation Satellite System (GNSS) and Wi-Fi, suffer from low accuracy or high deployment costs. In contrast, visible light positioning (VLP) offers immunity to electromagnetic interference and low-cost deployment. However, VLP systems still face challenges due to multipath-induced noise and insufficient use of temporal correlations in fingerprint models. To address these issues, an attention-mechanism-based bidirectional long short-term memory (BiLSTM) and a convolutional neural network indoor VLP (ABC-iVLP) are proposed in the paper. The proposed ABC model comprises a three-stage framework for dynamic weight allocation that incorporates a signal-to-noise attention mechanism to dynamically suppress noise, a BiLSTM to capture temporal dependencies in received signal strength, and a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) to extract spatial features. Additionally, the proposed ABC-iVLP model is validated in a 2 m × 2 m × 3 m room, yielding a maximum positioning error of 6.47 cm and an average positioning error of 2.33 cm. Compared with the ABC iVLP model without attention, BiLSTM, and CNN, the average positioning error is reduced by 32.5%, 43.4%, and 18.7%, respectively. Furthermore, robustness is verified across varying LEDcounts and ground heights, demonstrating the superiority of ABC-iVLP over existing schemes.

title: Ubiquitous Multi-Scale Distributed Self-Attention Hardware Co-Optimization for Complex English Translation via Neural Differential Equations and Differentiable Quantum Circuits
authors: Fei Gao, Lijun Zhang
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: This study originally proposed a multi-scale distributed self-attention hardware collaborative optimization framework. This framework can break through the bottlenecks of complex English translation algorithms under the limitations of low-resource corpus, long-distance dependency capture and hardware computing power. This framework has three core optimization innovations. First, multi-scale distributed selfattention realizes the joint modeling of global semantics and local semantics through cross-scale feature fusion and parallel computing; second, neural differential equations are introduced to characterize the dynamic evolution of hidden states in a continuous time modeling manner; third, differentiable quantum circuits (DQCs) are used to perform quantum feature mapping on word vectors. This operation can comprehensively enhance the expressive power of the translation model. At the hardware level, this study optimizes the delay and energy consumption through joint constraints of multi-objective optimization functions. This operation can achieve the coordinated configuration of algorithms and hardware resources. Experimental results demonstrate that this approach achieves excellent results on the WMT'19, IWSLT'17, and self-built corpora. Specifically, on the WMT'19 dataset, BLEU improved to 38.7, while TER decreased by approximately 9%. Furthermore, in hardware testing, the proposed algorithm reduced average inference latency to 96.2 ms and energy consumption to 1.37 J/sentence. Ablation experiments further validated the independent contribution of each module to overall performance improvement.

title: YOLO Based Product Classification in Smart Factory Using Internet of Things
authors: Aradhya Ajit Gaonkar, Vignesh Chougule, Nihal Jain, Nikhil Deshpande, Anurag Hurkadli, Meenaxi M Raikar, Uma Hiremath
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: The evolution of Industry 4.0 emphasizes the convergence of artificial intelligence (AI), computer vision, and Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT) for achieving intelligent, autonomous, and efficient manufacturing systems. This paper presents a real-time smart manufacturing framework that integrates YOLO-based object detection with MQTT-enabled IIoT infrastructure to automate the classification of bottle caps on a production line. The system captures live video streams using an IP camera and processes them using a YOLOv8 model to distinguish between valid bottle caps and defective components based on object confidence thresholds. The classified results are communicated to an ESP32 microcontroller via the MQTT protocol, which actuates a servo motor to segregate the items accordingly. A Flask-based web dashboard facilitates realtime monitoring, count visualization, and system control. The prototype is built to classify the products in factory scenario using Internet of Things concepts. The manager at the factory is able to view the statistics of the defective products from the remote location. The product classification accuracy obtained is 95%. In the prototype deep learning and IoT concepts are integrated for product quality inspection that would be a part of smart manufacturing.

title: Deep Unfolding-Based Graph Transformer Framework for Early Detection of Diabetic Retinopathy Using OCT Images
authors: Aaron Bose, Nirmal Varghese Babu, J. S Rajkumar
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: Diabetic Retinopathy (DR) is a serious diabetes complication and a major cause of avoidable blindness, and hence its early and proper detection is important. The current research offers a smart risk prediction framework for DR from Optical Coherence Tomography (OCT) images, diagnosing the disease in four grades: Normal, Mild, Moderate, and Severe. In order to improve the image quality, a Deep Unfolding Network (DUN) removes speckle noise with maintaining retinal structures. To balance datasets, a Latent Diffusion Model (LDM) produces high-quality synthesized OCT images at all steps. Features are extracted by a Swin Transformer with Retinal Layer Attention (RLA-Swin), retaining both global patterns and subtle anatomical details. A Concrete Autoencoder (CAe) extracts important features, and a Graph Convolutional Network (GCN) classifies them final. Results show 98.5% accuracy with excellent precision, recall, and F1-scores. The model is able to efficiently deal with noise, data scarcity, and small stage differences, presenting a solid, interpretable, and clinically useful instrument for detecting DR early.

title: Lightweight Bayesian Deep Learning Framework for Uncertainty-Aware Decision Making on Edge IoT Devices
authors: D. Joseph Jeyakumar, Michael Raj TF, S. Mahaboob Basha, Moorthy Agoramoorthy, M. Prabu
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: The increasing reliance on Internet of Things (IoT) ecosystems has heightened the demand for intelligent decisionmaking at the network edge, where devices often operate under stringent constraints in memory, computation, and energy. Conventional deep learning models deployed on such devices typically emphasize predictive accuracy but fail to incorporate uncertainty estimation, thereby limiting their reliability in safetycritical or dynamic IoT environments. Bayesian deep learning offers a principled mechanism to quantify predictive uncertainty; however, existing methods are computationally intensive and impractical for real-time edge deployment. To address this challenge, this paper proposes a lightweight Bayesian deep learning framework explicitly designed for uncertainty-aware decision making on resource-constrained IoT devices. The framework leverages variational inference and model compression techniques to balance accuracy, uncertainty estimation, and efficiency, ensuring scalability across diverse IoT modalities. Extensive experiments on benchmark IoT datasets and edge hardware prototypes demonstrate that the proposed approach achieves up to 32 % reduction in energy consumption and 27 % faster inference compared to conventional Bayesian methods, while preserving robust uncertainty quantification. The novelty of this study lies in integrating lightweight Bayesian modeling with edge-oriented optimization, bridging the gap between uncertainty-aware AI and the practical limitations of IoT deployment. This contribution enables more reliable, efficient, and adaptive decision-making in real-world edge intelligence scenarios.

title: Detection and Recognition of Multilingual Textual Information from Road Traffic Panels using Advanced Deep Learning Models)
authors: Syed Hassaan Ali Shah, Aitzaz Ahmed, Ali Muqarab Awan, Jawaria Maqbool
publish: 2026 ElCon Conference of Young Researchers in Computing &amp; Processing, and Information Security (ElCon-CP)
abstract: For autonomous navigation and intelligent transportation systems, Multilanguage text extraction is required. The main technological challenge is extraction of textual information from road scene images. The paper suggests an entire end-to-end process of road scene text identification and recognition concerning the issues of Bilingual traffic signs i.e. Arabic and English. The proposed methodology provides the most advanced image preprocessing and specific OCR settings and comprises the YOLOv11 object detector to achieve better multilingual-text recognition performance. The experiment has a two-stage setting: stage one is to train YOLOv11 with image resolution 1024×1024 and carries out the detection and classification of signs in 3 categories (guide signs, regulatory signs, warning signs) and then a more advanced OCR pipeline with Contrast Limited Adaptive Histogram Equalization (CLAHE), bilateral filtering and adaptive thresholding is applied, to get best text extraction. The system is based on EasyOCR and Arabic Reshaper integration to support the specifics of Arabic scripts. The state-of-the-art object detection model proved to be exceptionally accurate on all guiding classes after experimental validation on the comprehensive ASAYAR dataset. The scores are 97.9, 89.5, and 78.3 as the F1-score of guide signs, regulatory signs, and warning signs, respectively. Moreover, the multilingual optical character recognition module, the data set supports, gives accuracy of 90–99 percent on Arabic, 90–95 percent on the English, and on numerical plate recognition, 100 %.

title: CPP: A Stealthy Attack through Critical Path Poisoning in Federated Reinforcement Learning
authors: Chengze Zhang, Jia Wang, Xianghui Cao
publish: 2025 International Conference on Cyber Resilience and Endogenous Safety &amp; Security (CRESS)
abstract: In federated reinforcement learning (FRL), agents achieve collaborative training by sharing model parameters, enabling distributed computation while preserving local data privacy. However, this cooperative paradigm is vulnerable to malicious participants. Existing model poisoning attack algorithms only inject erroneous parameters, overlook the inherent structural dependencies between attack vectors and the internal architecture of neural networks, and fail to leverage model characteristics to achieve more precise and stealthy attacks. Thus, this paper proposes CPP, a stealthy model poisoning attack algorithm based on critical neurons in FRL. The proposed algorithm can identify critical neurons that play a dominant role in decision-making and selectively inject malicious updates into their parameters, thereby enhancing both the effectiveness and stealthiness of the attack. Furthermore, to mitigate the risk of update self-cancel in multi-agent attacks and filtering by defensive mechanisms, we introduce a consistency attack strategy that adjusts the attack among attackers. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed algorithm can effectively bypass vector-space-based defenses and has the potential to enhance the effectiveness of existing poisoning attacks through integration.

title: High Gain Meander-Line Antenna for Healthcare and Biomedical WSN Applications
authors: Kirthikaa. V, Keerthana. R, Mohan. C
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: A very high gain Meander-Line antenna designed for sustainable performance in Wireless Sensor Network (WSN) applications is described in this dissertation. To enhance the suggested antenna's radiation efficiency and impedance matching has a reconfigured ground plane and meander-line patch with many square-shaped slits. The antenna, which is assembled on a 1.6 mm thick FR-4 substrate, retains a high gain of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$48 \times 56 \times 1.6 \mathbf{m m}^{\mathbf{3}}$</tex>, which makes it suitable for straightforward integration, low-power, and high-efficiency systems for sensing. The ISM (Industrial, Scientific, and Medical) band, which has been widely used for WSN communication, is encompassed by the antenna's resonance at 2.4 GHz. The simulated <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$S_{11}$</tex> of −38 dB confirms that the antenna couples efficiently with the feed, showing minimal reflection. The recommended antenna is a promising option for contemporary wireless sensor networks and Internet of Things applications because to its design, high gain, affordability, and dependable operation.

title: Remote Sensing and Spectral Imaging for Diagnosing Fungal Diseases in Common Beans Across Seasonal Variations
authors: R. Agila, F.Mary Harin Fernandez
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: This paper proposes a hyperspectral remote sensing model in the diagnosis of fungal diseases in common beans under different seasons. A VNIR hyperspectral imaging system (400–1000 nm) was used and the training of Random Forest models was done using field-validated spectral data as well as biochemical data. The proposed system demonstrated a classification accuracy of 95.8% and a root mean square error (RMSE) of 0.042 with a tolerance of seasonal variability of less than 3%. Strong predictors of early fungal stress were found to be the critical wavelengths of 680 nm, 710 nm and 740 nm. Spectral indices like NDVI and REP demonstrated a high correlation <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(R 20.9)$</tex> with the disease severity, which increases an early ability to detect the disease in agriculture. Relative analysis showed that there was a 15 percent difference in the reflectance of healthy and infected leaves. Hyperspectral imaging and machine learning are combined and offer a noninvasive, precise, and scalable solution to managing disease and crop monitoring accurately in sustainable farming.

title: AgriVision: Deep Learning-Based Plant Disease with Daily Progress Tracking and Severity Classification
authors: Ananthajothi K, Hindusha K, Keerthana S
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: Agriculture remains the foundation of food security, while crop diseases persist as a threat to yield and farmer incomes globally. Early diagnosis and timely action are essential, but conventional manual surveys are slow, inconsistent, and impractical for great farmlands. The available deep learning-based applications show robust disease detection capabilities; however, they offer static, one-time accuracy without information on disease severity, progression, or recovery. The new system presents a multi-featured deep learning architecture that combines Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) for disease classification with a tracking of progression and predictive feedback module. Through the examination of sequential symptom and leaf image sequences, the system tracks the health of plants over time and offers actionable advice, specifying whether the health of the plants is recovering, stable, or declining. The architecture provides for adaptability, scalability, and real-time prediction, increasing the accuracy and explainability of the evaluation of plant health. By integrating classification with predictive analytics, the study adds to the development of intelligent, sustainable, and data-driven agricultural applications.

title: Cognitive-Sensor-Aware Home Learning Control: Personalized Multimodal Adaptation Via Meta-Learning
authors: Huiying Wan, Ruoyuan Ping
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: This study proposes a cnovel ognitive-aware home learning control model. This model implements a groundbreaking meta-learning approach for the multimodal personalized adaptation. The proposed model uses multimodal sensors to collect learners' emotional, cognitive, psychophysiological, and environmental data in real time. It also constructs a high-dimensional tensor joint representation to capture multimodal interaction characteristics. To address the noise and uncertainty of sensor signals, this study introduces a novel dynamic Bayesian network for state transition modeling. Then, information theory and Wasserstein-1 distance are combined as optimization tools to quantify modal contributions and optimize personalized control objectives. To address the differences in modal dependence among learners, this paper obtains rapid adaptive adjustment of personalized sensor weights through dynamic factor modeling and a meta-learning strategy. Results from a short-term experiment (20 middle school students, 30 minutes each session) showed that the proposed method improved the Pearson correlation coefficient by approximately <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 5 \% - 2 5 \%}$</tex> and reduced the RMSE by approximately <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3 0 \%}-\mathbf{4 0 \%}$</tex> compared to the uni-modal approach. Long-term experiments (7 days, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1}$</tex> hour per day, totaling approximately <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{8 4 0}$</tex> hours of data) showed that the predictive correlation of the uni-modal approach decreased significantly, while the proposed method remained stable. Numerically, the Pearson correlation coefficient decreased only slightly by approximately 2 %, while the RMSE remained essentially unchanged. The modal weights can be dynamically adjusted to adapt to long-term learning changes.

title: Blockchain-Enabled Federated Learning Framework for Secure, Scalable, and Privacy-Preserving Healthcare AI
authors: Kanchoju Ramalingachary, V.R. Vimal, Kavitha Vootla, M. Shaheda Begum, Thella Preethi Priyanka, S. Kaliappan
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: Healthcare data sharing across institutions presents major challenges related to privacy, security, and trust, as sensitive patient information must comply with regulations such as HIPAA and GDPR. Conventional centralized learning techniques reveal patient information to possible breaches and unauthorized access, which is inapplicable to healthcare analytics on a large scale. In order to overcome these drawbacks, the current study suggests a Blockchain-Integrated Federated Learning (FL) system that allows training a model in a decentralized manner with guaranteeing confidentiality to data and verifiable integrity. The system uses federated learning model that uses Multi-Layer Perceptron (MLP) and secure aggregation and differential privacy to safeguard the local model parameters in the process of training. The option of blockchain technology is included to reflect model changes based on cryptographic hashing and smart contracts to offer a transparent and immutable registry of collaboration between healthcare nodes. The effectiveness of the proposed solution is proven by experimental results, which showed an impressive 97 percent classification accuracy, low blockchain-imposed latency and high privacy preservation metrics. Also, scalability assessment shows that the accuracy of the model grows with the size of the client base involved, which proves the flexibility and strength of the framework. On the whole, this federated framework built on blockchain-based data privacy, trust, and performance as well as guarantees auditability and transparency, thus making it a secure and trustworthy approach to next-generation privacy-sensitive healthcare AI systems.

title: Deep Fuzzy C-Means Clustering in a Federated Heterogeneous Scenario
authors: Longmei Li, Wei Lu, Witold Pedrycz
publish: IEEE/CAA Journal of Automatica Sinica
abstract: In federated deep Fuzzy C-Means (FCM) clustering, conventional federated averaging (FedAvg) struggles with non-independent and identically distributed (non-IID) data and dynamic device participation, leading to model drift and performance degradation during global aggregation. To address this challenge, we propose FedFCD, a federated deep FCM clustering method featuring a novel aggregation mechanism. FedFCD equips each client with a hybrid architecture comprising a contrastive autoencoder (CtAE) and an FCM network (FCMNet), which collaboratively learn stable low-dimensional embeddings and refine soft clustering assignments iteratively. At the server side, we design a two-phase aggregation strategy integrating Bayesian ensemble learning and knowledge distillation (KD). First, the Bayesian aggregation mechanism probabilistically fuses heterogeneous local models' inferences into a consensus assignment by treating each client's model as a candidate hypothesis, thereby constructing a posterior distribution over the global model space through iterative evidence accumulation. Subsequently, dual-source distillation harmonizes pseudo-labels derived from the Bayesian consensus with ground-truth labels from limited shared data, enabling the global model to align its predictions with both semantic anchors and aggregated soft assignments while preserving privacy through distillation loss. Comparative experiments on benchmark datasets demonstrate that FedFCD outperforms baseline methods in clustering accuracy and exhibits enhanced stability under varying conditions, including data heterogeneity, device numbers, and device dropout.

title: High Sensitivity Iontronic Flexible Pressure Sensor Based on Microstructured Electrodes for Wearable Devices
authors: Xiuxiu Yan, Yajie Li, Ying Zhang, Weihua Bai, Hairong Kou
publish: IEEE Instrumentation & Measurement Magazine
abstract: Flexible pressure sensors play a crucial role in many fields such as wearable devices, biomedicine, and intelligent robot sensing. This study proposes a high sensitivity iontronic flexible pressure sensor based on microstructured electrodes. The pressure detection range of this flexible pressure sensor is <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0-850 \text{kPa}$</tex>. Within the detection range of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0-20 \text{kPa}$</tex>, the sensitivity can reach up to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2.0365 \text{kPa}^{-1}$</tex> at most. It also shows good repeatability, stability and durability, and has a short response time (100 ms) and recovery time (50 ms). After a series of practical application tests, this flexible pressure sensor has successfully monitored tiny movements of parts such as the throat, face, eyebrows, and wrists, in activities such as speaking, swallowing, breathing, smiling, frowning and pulse detection, as well as larger movements such as finger bending. This sensor is not only innovative in its material structure but also meets the requirements of the instrumentation and measurement field in terms of performance and calibration methods, providing high-precision measurement solutions for applications such as wearable medical devices, human health monitoring, and robot skin.

title: Railway Safety Enhancement Using a Preceding Intelligent Vehicle With Adaptive Sensor Switching and Degraded Communication Handling
authors: Jayarajan P, Hadi Sahal N, Akash G, Dinesh N
publish: 2026 4th International Conference on Intelligent Data Communication Technologies and Internet of Things (IDCIoT)
abstract: Railway transportation continues to be one of the most cost-effective and safest options for international and national cargo transport and long-distance travel. However, unexpected trespassers, natural disasters, and track structural failures and cracks continue to threaten operational reliability. This paper presents a comprehensive approach to improving railway safety through the deployment of a Preceding Intelligent Vehicle (PIV) system that extends the capabilities of conventional pilot vehicle technologies. The proposed system employs adaptive sensor switching, prioritizing non-visual sensors under low-visibility conditions and vision-based sensing under favourable environmental conditions. In addition, a degraded communication mode with bandwidth prioritization and fail-safe operation enables reliable alert transmission in tunnels and other signal-obstructed environments. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed PIV system improves real-time hazard detection accuracy and response time, providing a scalable and cost-effective solution for railway accident prevention with minimal infrastructure modification.

title: Adversarial Verification of the Machine Learning Models Security
authors: Maria E. Guseva, Maxim O. Kalinin
publish: 2026 ElCon Conference of Young Researchers in Computing &amp; Processing, and Information Security (ElCon-CP)
abstract: The critical deployment of machine learning (ML) models is vulnerable to adversarial attacks. The paper proposes a novel verification framework leveraging Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) to quantitatively assess ML model vulnerability. The presented method analyzes PSO convergence metrics, the proportion of successful adversarial particles, and perturbation norms to distinguish adversarial samples from clean samples. Experimental study has demonstrated that the proposed method effectively detects adversarial attacks by evaluating a model's local stability. This approach demonstrates the higher performance and effectiveness than the Satisfiability Modulo Theories (SMT) formal approach implemented in the state-of-art solutions, like Marabou. It contributes to more secure and reliable ML development, offering a practical method to mitigate risks associated with deploying vulnerable ML models in high-stakes industrial applications.

title: Real-Time Route Planning in Disaster Evacuation using Real-Time Disaster Information
authors: Anna Iakubchik, Ian Iakubchik
publish: 2026 ElCon Conference of Young Researchers in Computing &amp; Processing, and Information Security (ElCon-CP)
abstract: Evacuation from a building where disaster (fire, earthquake etc.) happened is not an easy task. The “evacuation route” known to everybody might not be useful in real life because some parts of the shown route might be impassable due to the spread fire, smoke or crumbling, and it is very hard for the evacuating people to find another route if the well-known route does not work. Meanwhile, many buildings have installed cameras and sensors for the localization of the fire threat and overall monitoring. This information can be used to automatically assess which places are more dangerous or hard to go through, and then automatically plan a route for evacuating the people. It can also be recalculated in real-time if the situation changes. We propose a method using potential field method and graphs to find and recalculate a route. It proves effective and computationally efficient for several-story buildings.

title: Environmental Intelligence: Data driven insights for stakeholders on the environmental sustainable development goals
authors: Hiran H. Lathabai, Vinith Kumar Nair, A S Anandhukrishna, Prema Nedungadi, Walter Leal Filho, Raghu Raman
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Environmental intelligence (EI) leverages AI and big data to garner critical insights for advancing the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) related to environment - SDG 13 (Climate), SDG 14 (Oceans), and SDG 15 (Land). However, investigations that target synthesis of its scope, methods, and contributions for proactive environmental and STI policymaking are scanty. So do frameworks for carrying out such investigations. This paper introduces a macro-meso-micro assessment framework that integrates machine learning-based BERTopic modeling, keyword co-occurrence mapping, and targeted content analysis to examine EI publications indexed in the Dimensions database (up to June 21, 2025) related to SDGs 13, 14 and 15. The novel top-down scientometric framework is aligned with the pilot version of guidance list for bibliometric analyses (GLOBAL). The macro–meso–micro assessments identify (i) five core EI topics, (ii) sixteen technological areas within the five topics, and (iii) key specific developments and activities concentrated on climate risk assessment, ecosystem and biodiversity monitoring, carbon accounting, and pollution tracking, with emerging attention to explainable AI, Ecorobotics, and green buildings, etc. Micro assessment synthesizes evidence from 29 publications on key technologies and 12 publications on cross-goal interactions to characterize technical advances, typical data/analytics pipelines, and decision-support use cases. Cross-SDG analysis reveals bidirectional links between environmental SDGs and allied goals—SDG 6 (Water), SDG 7 (Energy), SDG 9 (Industry), SDG 11 (Cities), and SDG 12 (Consumption)—particularly via water quality sensing, renewable energy siting, industrial emission management, urban heat/flood analytics, and supply chain traceability. This study contributes (i) a delineated EI landscape tied to SDGs (ii) a topic–technology map which highlights promising emerging technologies, and (iii) a reproducible, GLOBAL-protocol compliant framework for bibliometrics. The implications of this paper are threefold: it aligns standards and data access to enable EI deployment; it prioritizes multi-SDG R&D and capacity building; and it directs integration of EI with STI intelligence and epidemiological intelligence to enable co-development of EI toolchains across policy, academia, and industry to advance pursuit of environmental and allied SDGs.

title: Battery Storage in More- and All-Electric Fishing Vessels: Real-Time Simulation and Experimental Verification
authors: Phillip Lewis, Milad Beikbabaei, Chandler Kemp, Mohammad Heidari Kapourchali, Ali Mehrizi-Sani
publish: IEEE Transactions on Energy Conversion
abstract: The transition of fuel-reliant fishing vessels to more- and all-electric alternatives is essential to reduce the fishing industry's dependence on fuel. Most research on the energy transition in the maritime industry has focused on larger vessels. Larger vessels typically have multiple generation units; however, fishing vessels have fewer onboard resources, making control and operation more challenging. This paper introduces the development of a real-time simulation testbed for small fishing vessels using two Typhoon HIL 604 real-time simulators. Data obtained from an actual purse seine fishing vessel are applied in developing, validating, and benchmarking models for vessel dynamics, propeller behavior, and hydraulic performance. The models are adaptable and represent a 58-foot fishing vessel, including its propulsion system, hydraulic load, and battery storage capable of operating in both grid-forming and grid-following modes. The testbed is then used to examine integration of a battery energy storage system (BESS): during transit, during fishing operations, and during both transit and fishing operations. The results demonstrate the distinct benefits of both small and large battery installations on fishing vessels, including shorter travel time between the harbor and fishing grounds, stable voltage and frequency across operating modes, and reduced noise during fishing operations.

title: Low-Speed Sensorless Control With Fault-Tolerant Ability for SRM Using Unsaturated Inductance Reconstruction and Over-Relaxed Newton Method
authors: Dexu Lv, Wen Ding, Shuo Chen, Jun Cai, Zhonggang Yin
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Electronics
abstract: This article proposes a low-speed sensorless control method for switched reluctance motor (SRM) with open-circuit fault tolerance. The scheme estimates the rotor position based on the reconstructed unsaturated inductance and the over-relaxed Newton method. First, the full cycle of the SRM is divided into three regions: conduction, demagnetization, and pulse injection, and the inductance calculation for each case are provided. A dual-level-fitting-based unsaturated inductance reconstruction method is proposed to reconstruct the unsaturated inductance based on the current and saturated inductance. Then, a rotor position estimation scheme using the full-cycle unsaturated inductance and the over-relaxed Newton method is proposed. The scheme takes the sum of the squares of the differences between the reconstructed unsaturated inductances and the Fourier series inductance as the cost function. The over-relaxed Newton method is employed to find the minimum value of this cost function, which yields the rotor position. In addition, the initial position estimation method and the rotor position estimation method for open-circuit faults are given. Eventually, the proposed scheme is verified on a three-phase 12/8 SRM to verify its feasibility and effectiveness. The experimental results show that the scheme can operate stably at low speeds, has self-starting capability, and open-circuit fault tolerance.

title: GCMT-Net: Generative Context-driven Multimodal Temporal Network for Video Anomaly Detection in Consumer Applications
authors: Liangbin Gao, Minmin Cheng, Kai Cui, Hongwei Lu, Wei Feng, Shenghao Liu, Xianjun Deng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Consumer Electronics
abstract: Video constitutes a significant content modality within consumer electronics applications. Video Anomaly Detection (VAD) aims to automatically identify anomalous events deviating from normal patterns in consumer videos. This technology directly supports quality assurance and user experience optimization in consumer electronics products, while also enabling functionalities such as environmental safety monitoring for consumer electronics applications. However, traditional methods rely heavily on single-modal features, making it difficult to effectively utilize the complementary clues between different modalities. They have limitations and semantic gaps in explaining complex scenes and understanding the video context. Moreover, most methods overlook complex cross-modal correlations and fail to fully exploit the potential of multimodal semantic clues. To address these challenges, this work proposes an innovative Generative Context-driven Multimodal Temporal Network (GCMT-Net) to detect abnormal video for consumer electronic applications. The GCMT-Net introduces a context generation network that transforms visual context into rich textual descriptions, alleviating semantic gaps that are difficult to capture with traditional visual features. A novel multi-scale temporal module then integrates visual, auditory, and textual information, significantly enhancing the model’s ability to recognize complex anomalous events and improving its overall robustness. Furthermore, modal consistency learning ensures semantic alignment between modalities and consistent feature representations across scales. Experiments on four benchmark datasets demonstrate the superior performance of the proposed framework in addressing VAD challenges in complex consumer application scenarios.

title: Int3DNet: Scene-Motion Cross Attention Network for 3D Intention Prediction in Mixed Reality
authors: Taewook Ha, Woojin Cho, Dooyoung Kim, Woontack Woo
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: We propose Int3DNet, a scene-aware network that predicts 3D intention areas directly from scene geometry and head-hand motion cues, enabling robust human intention prediction without explicit object-level perception. In Mixed Reality (MR), intention prediction is critical as it enables the system to anticipate user actions and respond proactively, reducing interaction delays and ensuring seamless user experiences. Our method employs a cross attention fusion of sparse motion cues and scene point clouds, offering a novel approach that directly interprets the user's spatial intention within the scene. We evaluated Int3DNet on MoGaze and CIRCLE datasets, which are public datasets for full-body human-scene interactions, showing consistent performance across time horizons of up to 1500 ms and outperforming the baselines, even in diverse and unseen scenes. Moreover, we demonstrate the usability of proposed method through a demonstration of efficient visual question answering (VQA) based on intention areas. Int3DNet provides reliable 3D intention areas derived from head-hand motion and scene geometry, thus enabling seamless interaction between humans and MR systems through proactive processing of intention areas.

title: MultiCam: On-the-fly Multi-Camera Pose Estimation Using Spatiotemporal Overlaps of Known Objects_supp1-3679095.mp4
authors: Shiyu Li
publish: N/A
abstract: Multi-camera dynamic Augmented Reality (AR) applications require a camera pose estimation to leverage individual information from each camera in one common system. This can be achieved by combining contextual information, such as markers or objects, across multiple views. While commonly cameras are calibrated in an initial step or updated through the constant use of markers, another option is to leverage information already present in the scene, like known objects. Another downside of marker-based tracking is that markers have to be tracked inside the field-of-view (FoV) of the cameras. To overcome these limitations, we propose a constant dynamic camera pose estimation leveraging spatiotemporal FoV overlaps of known objects on the fly. To achieve that, we enhance the state-of-the-art object pose estimator to update our spatiotemporal scene graph, enabling a relation even among non-overlapping FoV cameras. To evaluate our approach, we introduce a multi-camera, multi-object pose estimation dataset with temporal FoV overlap, including static and dynamic cameras. Furthermore, in FoV overlapping scenarios, we outperform the state-of-the-art on the widely used YCB-V and T-LESS dataset in camera pose accuracy. Our performance on both previous and our proposed datasets validates the effectiveness of our marker-less approach for AR applications. The code and dataset are available on https://github.com/roth-hex-lab/IEEE-VR-2026-MultiCam.

title: Dynamic Construction Strategy for Virtual Power Plants Considering Distributed Energy Resource Interaction Uncertainty and Response Recovery Process
authors: Yanjia Wang, Da Xie, Chen Shi, Xitian Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industry Applications
abstract: The interaction uncertainty of Distributed Energy Resources (DERs) during grid dispatch poses a challenge to the efficient and economical construction of Virtual Power Plants (VPPs). To address this, this paper proposes a dynamic construction strategy for VPPs that considers the interaction uncertainty of DERs and the response recovery process. First, the interaction effects of DER power variations are quantified through Seasonal Trend Decomposition (STL) and cross-correlation analysis, and an linkage matrix is constructed to depict the uncertainty. Then, a comprehensive evaluation method is proposed, which not only accounts for the direct economic cost of response scheduling but also innovatively introduces the optimization of the post-scheduling response recovery process, using Model Predictive Control (MPC) to minimize the total recovery cost. On this basis, a dynamic construction strategy using a Generative Adversarial Network (GAN)-Copula function is proposed. For resources with significant interaction, a joint probability distribution model is established to accurately predict the power interaction impacts during the scheduling process, thereby dynamically adjusting the final aggregation set and improving its economy. Simulation results based on data from 247 charging stations in Shenzhen and 8,497 communication base stations in Hefei indicate that, compared to existing methods, this approach can reduce scheduling response costs by approximately 12.71%.

title: Thin-Core Fiber MZI-Based Flexible Tactile Sensors Enable Multi-Perception in Wearables and Robots_supp1-3677354.mp4
authors: Binbin Luo
publish: N/A
abstract: Tactile sensors are functional devices that emulate the ability of human skin to perceive external stimuli and play a critical role in healthcare, industrial manufacturing, and virtual reality. In this study, a flexible tactile sensor based on an optical fiber Mach-Zehnder interferometer (MZI) with multifunctional sensing capability is proposed. The sensor adopts an MZI structure encapsulated by polydimethylsiloxane (PDMS) films, in which the MZI is formed by splicing a thin-core fiber (TCF) between two multimode fibers (MMFs). Owing to the high sensitivity of the MZI structure to external stimuli, applied mechanical inputs are converted into variations in optical interference intensity. The proposed sensor exhibits a sensitivity of 1.27 dB/N within a pressure range of 0 to 10 N, along with excellent dynamic response over a frequency range from 0.1 to 200 Hz, a fast response time of 50 ms, and good repeatability and stability. As a proof of concept, the sensor demonstrates the ability to identify object properties such as hardness, texture, and shape. In addition, it enables physiological pulse signal monitoring and can be applied to synchronous and intelligent gesture control of robotic hand. The tactile sensor developed in this work features high sensitivity, a wide measurement range, simple fabrication, and immunity to electromagnetic interference, indicating broad application potential in wearable devices and human-machine interaction (HMI).

title: Real-Time Edge-Deployable Non-Invasive Blood Glucose Estimation Using PPG Sensor Signals via a Dual-Stream Spatio-Temporal Graph-Transformer
authors: Sia Ahuja, Suryansh Malhotra, Jyoti Yadav
publish: IEEE Sensors Letters
abstract: Photoplethysmography (PPG) sensor-based glucose monitoring offers non-invasive continuous tracking for diabetic management, but deep learning approaches often face challenges in practical edge deployment due to computational complexity. We propose a hybrid Graph Convolutional Network (GCN) Transformer architecture that jointly models spatial feature relationships and temporal dependencies using learned adjacency matrices with real time performance on mobile processors. The proposed method extracts frequency-domain and temporal morphological features using Short-Time Fourier Transform (STFT) and Discrete Wavelet Transform (DWT), respectively, and integrates them through dual Transformer encoders with bidirectional cross-attention. The GCN propagates information over constructed spatiotemporal feature graphs, enhancing glucose-related patterns through neighborhood consensus. The model performance is evaluated on 6,727 PPG segments from 217 subjects, achieves RMSE of 4.887 mg/dL, MAE of 1.973 mg/dL, and R<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$^{2}$</tex-math></inline-formula> of 0.989, with 96.1% predictions within clinically acceptable <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\pm$</tex-math></inline-formula>15 mg/dL threshold. The compact 1.16M-parameter architecture enables edge deployment with 76 ms inference latency on Snapdragon 778G processors, demonstrating feasibility for continuous glucose monitoring.

title:
A Reusable Enzymatic Sensor for Rapid H
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    O
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    Detection with a Compact 3D-Printed Prototype
authors: Cheng-Chih Hsu, Qian-Yi Liu, Shu-Wei Chuang, Chen-Ming Tsai, Ching-Liang Dai
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: This study develops a reliable system for H<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>O<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> concentration measurement, integrating a compact, low-maintenance optical configuration with a reusable enzymatic sensor. The 3D-printed compact system demonstrated high photocurrent stability, exhibiting fluctuations of less than 3 nA over a 10-minute period. The sensor exhibited excellent operational stability across 60 repeated usage cycles and provided rapid quantification within 150 seconds. Significantly, this research precisely defines the unsaturated, saturated, and inhibition regions for various peroxidase (POD) concentrations in H<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>O<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> measurement. It establishes a practical procedure for quantifying unknown H<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>O<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> concentrations, thereby broadening the applicability of POD enzymes in this analytical field. Moreover, the reusable design contributes to reduced biochemical waste, aligning with green analytical practices.

title: A Novel Comprehensive Parametric Macromodeling Method for MEMS Sensors Based on the Reduced-Order State-Space Models and Machine Learning Approaches
authors: Hao Xu, Yun-Cong Mo, Hao-Dong Zhang, Jun-Jie Wen, Zai-Fa Zhou, Qing-An Huang
publish: Journal of Microelectromechanical Systems
abstract: Transient characteristics are critical for the collaborative design of MEMS devices and interface circuits. However, traditional behavioral models often oversimplify transient dynamics, while interpolation-based parametric model order reduction (PMOR) suffers from the curse of dimensionality and grid constraints. Therefore, a novel comprehensive parametric macromodeling method based on an amalgamation of the reduced-order state-space models (SSMs) and machine learning (ML) for MEMS devices is proposed to parameterize the transient characteristics of the macromodel, referred to as the multi-channel matrix parameterization method (MMPM). This method can map structural parameters directly to system matrices, ensuring both high accuracy and seamless circuit simulator compatibility. Unlike black box ML models, MMPM preserves the physical state-space structure, enabling stable and efficient co-simulation. In MMPM, a multi-channel feed-forward neural network (FNN) is trained on a dataset of physically consistent reduced-order matrices to predict system dynamics under varying geometries. Taking a thermal flow sensor as an example, the method effectively parameterizes critical structural variables to capture full dynamic behaviors, including transient heating/response times and steady-state temperature differences. While the one-time offline construction of the parametric macromodel takes approximately 634.9 s, the subsequent online co-simulation is instantaneous (< 1.0 s), offering a speedup of orders of magnitude compared to finite element analysis (FEA). Validation results demonstrate an average relative error of less than 8.6%, proving that MMPM is a robust, scalable, and efficient approach for comprehensive MEMS macromodeling. [2025-0223]

title: Table of Contents
authors: N/A
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dielectrics and Electrical Insulation

title: Architectural Scalability and Attention Stabilization for Robust Impulsive Acoustic Spectrogram Classification Using Swin Transformer V2
authors: Pafan Doungpaisan, Peerapol Khunarsa
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Impulsive acoustic event classification based on spectrogram representations remains challenging due to the non-stationary and broadband characteristics of transient signals. While convolutional neural networks (CNNs) have been widely adopted for spectrogram-based classification, their reliance on local convolutional receptive fields may limit the modeling of long-range spectro–temporal dependencies. This study investigates the architectural scalability and attention stabilization properties of Swin Transformer V2 for robust impulsive acoustic spectrogram classification. A comprehensive experimental framework is developed using twelve time–frequency representations, including Bark, Chromagram, Cochleagram, Constant-Q Transform (CQT), Delta, Delta-Delta, Log-Mel, Mel, MFCC, Spectral Contrast, Short-Time Fourier Transform (STFT), andWavelet spectrograms. Multiple Swin Transformer configurations are evaluated and compared with widely used CNN baseline architectures, including ResNet50, ResNet101, EfficientNet-B0, and InceptionResNetV2, under a unified training protocol with five-fold stratified cross-validation. Experimental results demonstrate that Swin Transformer V2 consistently achieves higher classification accuracy and macro-F1 scores than CNN baselines across diverse spectrogram representations. In addition, the hierarchical attention mechanism provides improved stability across data partitions and enhanced robustness under spectrogram resolution variability. These findings highlight the effectiveness of transformer-based architectures for modeling complex spectro–temporal structures in impulsive acoustic signals and provide insights into the architectural behavior of Swin Transformer models for spectrogram-based acoustic classification.
